source.subject: Novacore vendor record update — banking split & legal address change
messages:
From: Apolline Duval <apolline.duval@novacore.fr>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: payables@novacore.fr
Date: Tue, 03 Nov 2026 09:14:00 +0000
Subject: Vendor record update — bank split & HQ relocation

Hi Ava and Monica,

I hope you're both well. Quick heads up from Novacore: we completed an internal legal reorganization last month and are splitting our payment routing across two legal entities. Effective 2026-12-01, payments for US contracts should go to:

  - Novacore US, Inc. (remit: ACH bank ending 6782)
  - Novacore Global Solutions (for EU/APAC invoices; SEPA/Intl)

We are also moving our North American headquarters to 4100 Harborview Ave, Suite 300, Seattle, WA 98104 — the new legal address will appear on invoices starting 2026-12-01.

Attached: a bank letter on company letterhead and our updated onboarding form. We also included a signed W-9 for your records. Please let us know what you need from Redwood to update the vendor record and whether upcoming POs will require reissue to the new remit entity.

Attachments (stubs): Novacore_bank_letter.pdf [PDF 85KB], Novacore_signed_onboarding_form.pdf [PDF 220KB], W-9_Novacore.pdf [PDF 45KB]

Thanks,
Apolline Duval
Head of Finance Operations
Novacore Solutions
apolline.duval@novacore.fr
+33 6 12 34 56 78

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Apolline Duval <apolline.duval@novacore.fr>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 10:22:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Vendor record update — bank split & HQ relocation

Thanks for the update, Apolline — congratulations on the reorganization. A few quick items so we can process this cleanly on our end: 

  1) Can you confirm the legal entity that should appear on Redwood POs and invoices for existing contracts (Novacore US, Inc. vs Novacore Global Solutions)?
  2) Please confirm the effective date for payment routing (you mentioned 2026-12-01). Will any invoices dated before that continue to be paid to the current account?
  3) Finance (cc'd) requires a bank verification letter on bank letterhead AND a short confirmation from your treasury that the ACH routing is valid for vendor payables. If possible, include a contact for a bank operations person.
  4) Are there any tax withholding implications we should be aware of given the entity split?

We typically update vendor remittance in our AP system and run a verification ACH before switching. Monica, Marcus — can we put this through the vendor-change checklist (forms + bank letter + ACH test)? If everything looks good, we'll change remit instructions and update the remit address on active POs where necessary.

Appreciate the attachments — I'll loop back after finance verifies.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Procurement Manager
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
(415) 555-0124

On Tue, Nov 3, 2026 at 09:14 Apolline Duval <apolline.duval@novacore.fr> wrote:
> Hi Ava and Monica,
> 
> I hope you're both well. Quick heads up from Novacore:...
> 
> [original attachments]

From: Apolline Duval <apolline.duval@novacore.fr>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, payables@novacore.fr
Date: Thu, 05 Nov 2026 07:03:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Vendor record update — bank split & HQ relocation

Hi Ava, Monica, Marcus —

Thanks for the quick reply. Short answers below: 

  1) For Redwood contracts executed before 2026-12-01 please keep Novacore US, Inc. as the billing/legal entity; for new invoices dated on/after 2026-12-01 please remit to Novacore US, Inc. (remit account ending 6782). We've attached a box-to-box bank letter and a signed confirmation from our treasury.
  2) Confirmed effective date: 2026-12-01. Invoices issued before that date should route to the existing account; anything dated 2026-12-01 or later should route to the new remit details.
  3) Bank operations contact: Claire Montagne, Wells Fargo Treasury Services — claire.montagne@wellsfargo.com, +1 206-555-1199.
  4) Tax: no withholding changes for US-sourced payments; W-9 attached for your file.

DocuSign link for the amended onboarding form: https://www.docusign.com/console/documents/ABC12345 (signed copy attached).

Attachments (stubs): Novacore_bank_letter.pdf [PDF 85KB], Novacore_signed_onboarding_form.pdf [PDF 220KB], W-9_Novacore.pdf [PDF 45KB]

Let me know if you need anything else to start the verification.

Best,
Apolline

On Wed, Nov 4, 2026 at 10:22 Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the update, Apolline — congratulations on the reorganization....

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Apolline Duval <apolline.duval@novacore.fr>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 16:34:00 +0000
Subject: RE: Vendor record update — bank split & HQ relocation

Ava / Monica —

I completed initial verification on Novacore's submitted docs this morning. Summary:

  - Bank letter and ACH details match; bank contact (Claire Montagne) responded to our verification request and confirmed the routing.
  - W-9 and signed onboarding form are in good order.
  - I kicked off a $0.01 micro-ACH verification to the new remit account (ending 6782); if that posts successfully by Friday we can flip the remit instructions for invoices dated 2026-12-01 onward.

Next steps for procurement: update the vendor record in the AP system with new remit address (4100 Harborview Ave, Suite 300, Seattle, WA 98104) and add "Novacore US, Inc. / remit ACH ****6782" as the primary remit instruction for new invoices. Monica, can you amend any active POs that would be invoiced after 2026-12-01 or send a quick note to owners to reissue?

I'll follow up once the micro-ACH posts. If anyone wants to review the attached verification notes I uploaded them to the shared drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cNovacoreOnboarding

Marcus Lin
Senior Finance Analyst — Accounts Payable
Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com
(650) 555-0786

source.subject: Dedicated capacity floorplan & surge architecture - Lumos Bio
messages:
From: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 4 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0800
Subject: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the latency numbers looked strong for our baseline load. We’re starting to firm up our infrastructure ask for Q2 2027 and want to include Redwood Dedicated in the procurement packet. Before we loop procurement I need a concise set of reservation options we can present to our leadership and infra team. Goal: keep cost predictable while guaranteeing low tail latency during daytime clinical workloads.

Quick context:
- Expected steady-state inference: ~160 concurrent requests during daytime peaks (US EST 8a-6p).
- Typical prompt length: 512 tokens. Models: proprietary 13B family (we may finetune), plan to run 1–2 variants.
- Traffic pattern: weekday peaks with occasional scheduled burst testing windows (max 4x baseline for short windows).
- Internal compliance: must be VPC-connected and have audit log retention for 1 year.

What we need from you: three capacity options laid out as a simple "floorplan" table — minimum committed GPUs, burst/scale options, recommended ramp schedule and activation timeline, and any blocker items for on-prem vs. VPC deployment. Attachments or one-sheet preferred for exec review.

Target timeline: decision by end of November.

Thanks — Joel
Joel Pattinson
Head of Platform
Lumos Bio

---

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Nov 2026 10:27:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts

Joel — appreciate the concise context. Attached are three pragmatic reservation floorplans and a short SOW stub to help procurement: cluster-floorplan-options.xlsx and Lumos_Bio_SOW_v1.pdf. High level summary below so you can paste into the packet.

Option A — Pilot / Low-commit (90-day)- Minimum commit: 8 A100-equivalent GPUs- Burst: up to 32 GPUs for scheduled test windows (auto-burst policy)- Ramp: immediate activation of 8 GPUs, burst cap available within 48–72 hours provisioning window- Use case: validate finetune + production smoke testing- Pricing: monthly reserved baseline + per-burst usage. Estimated monthly baseline cost range: $XXk–$YYk

Option B — Business (recommended for stable prod)- Minimum commit: 32 A100-equivalent GPUs- Burst: 64 GPUs available via short-term capacity lanes and scheduled bursts- Ramp: stage activation over 8 weeks (32 -> 48 -> 64 bursts enabled), full committed baseline online in 2 weeks- Use case: primary production serving with predictable latency and 2 model variants- Pricing: lower per-token unit cost vs. Option A; we include a 10% startup credit on first two months for a 12-month commit

Option C — Enterprise / Large-commit- Minimum commit: 96 A100-equivalent GPUs- Burst: 128 GPUs with multi-region fallback- Ramp: staged over 12–20 weeks with canary lanes and A/B traffic steering- Use case: multi-product rollout, strict SLOs and geo-bursting

Notes / restrictions:
- VPC deployment: includes private control plane, KMS integration; on-prem air-gap requires additional security assessment (see SOW).
- Audit logs: 12-month retention available as an add-on; standard retention is 90 days.
- Network: we recommend 10GbE minimum egress for the baseline 32-GPU option; see Ben’s note in the next message.

If you want, we can convert Option B into a one-page commitment slide for procurement and include estimated burn numbers. Which option should I flesh out first?

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: cluster-floorplan-options.xlsx; Lumos_Bio_SOW_v1.pdf

---

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 6 Nov 2026 14:43:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts — technical sizing notes

Joel / Aisha — adding a short technical annex to support the Options above. I attached network_requirements.docx with a brief checklist. Key sizing notes: 

1) GPU selection and effective throughput- For your 13B family we estimate ~13–16 tokens/sec per A100-80GB-equivalent GPU under float16 serving with KV cache. That maps to ~10–12 concurrent steady requests per GPU at 512 tokens average (depending on batching).

2) Minimum recommended baselines (conservative rule-of-thumb):- 8 GPUs: safe for pilots and small smokes (~80–100 qps sustained with aggressive batching)- 32 GPUs: production baseline for 160 concurrent daytime users at low latency assuming request coalescing and prefix caching- 96 GPUs: multi-product or multi-variant fleets with headroom for retraining jobs and throughput spikes

3) Burst and ramp behavior- We recommend configuring both automated burst lanes and a short-notice provisioning policy. Autofill burst usually requires a 24–72 hour warm-up window. For your scheduled test windows we can provision guaranteed burst lanes that come online in under 4 hours if pre-committed.

4) Network and storage- Baseline egress: 10Gbps for 32-GPU baseline. For 96 GPUs plan for 25–40Gbps depending on expected KV cache miss rates.

5) Safety and rollout- Canary lanes with sample-based quality monitoring; automatic fallbacks to smaller model variants if latency or error budgets breach.

If helpful I can run a quick capacity simulation with your request traces (anonymized) to produce expected percentiles for P50/P95/P99 latency against each option.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

Attachments: network_requirements.docx

---

From: Joel Pattinson <joel@lumosbio.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 8 Nov 2026 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity planning for next-year production rollouts

Aisha / Ben — thanks for the quick turnaround and clear options. After reviewing with infra and product leadership we want to move forward with Option B (32 GPU baseline with burst to 64) and the staged 8-week activation schedule. Two asks before procurement: 

1) Please send a one-page commitment slide + the SOW with pricing filled in so procurement can attach to the PO.
2) Please confirm DocuSign timeline — we need the signed agreement by Dec 3 to meet our kickoff date in mid-Jan.

We prefer the 12-month commit with the 10% startup credit you flagged. Also confirm the VPC deployment includes KMS integration and 12-month audit log add-on (we’ll cover the add-on cost).

If that all checks out I’ll get PO prep started and loop procurement for the DocuSign link.

On Thu, Nov 5, 2026 at 10:27 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com> wrote:
> Joel — appreciate the concise context. Attached are three pragmatic reservation floorplans and a short SOW stub to help procurement: cluster-floorplan-options.xlsx and Lumos_Bio_SOW_v1.pdf. High level summary below so you can paste into the packet.
>
> [snip — options A/B/C summary]

Regards,
Joel

---

source.subject: Request: allocation & calculation for partial degradation on Pegasus network
messages:
From: Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Pegasus Platform Ops <ops@pegasus-cloud.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

Hi Ben,

We observed an increase in 5xx rates and tail latency for your account's inference endpoints between 08:15 and 08:57 PT this morning while Pegasus performed a rolling network maintenance affecting our east-us backbone. Our initial aggregation shows ~42 minutes of partial degradation (elevated latency and ~2.4% 5xx). I'm attaching our outage log (pegasus-outage-log.csv).

Per our standard process, we'd like Redwood to confirm the impact on your side and advise if you're requesting a credit under the SLA. If you can share your request and any evidence, we'll route it to billing.

Thanks,
Elena Soto
Senior Support Lead
Pegasus Cloud

Attachment: pegasus-outage-log.csv (text/csv, 23 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

Elena,

Thanks for the heads up and for the log. We've pulled our telemetry for the same window. Key notes:
- Redwood's edge metrics show a single 5xx spike from 08:22–08:27 (approx 5 minutes); host-level latency had several 95th-percentile blips between 08:18–08:30 but aggregated token-level latency across RPCs stayed within SLA bands for ~95% of traffic.
- Our automated prefix-KV cache shows no eviction events correlated with the window. We did see increased TCP retransmits originating in the Pegasus network segments (matching your log).

We will attach our internal response timeline and a snippet of the request-level latencies for the period (redwood-response-timeline.pdf). Based on a preliminary read, Redwood believes the majority of the 42-minute window is attributable to Pegasus' maintenance (third-party). Per SLA section 5.2 (Provider outages vs. Third-Party dependency), we dispute a full-credit allocation and request credit apportionment tied to traffic-level impact.

Proposed ask: Pegasus to propose a quantified credit for the portion you attribute to your network (we'll provide our per-route delta to compute proportional credit). Can you confirm the exact start/end timestamps you used and whether internal retries were suppressed by your maintenance policy?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachment: redwood-response-timeline.pdf (application/pdf, 56 KB)

From: Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Pegasus Platform Ops <ops@pegasus-cloud.com>
Date: 2026-11-02T08:37:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

Ben,

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the timeline. Our start/end timestamps were 08:15:00–08:57:00 PT and were derived from BGP flap and interface counters across the affected PoPs; I've included the raw csv lines in the attached pegasus-outage-log.csv. title: Request: allocation & calculation for partial degradation on Pegasus network
preview:
From: Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Pegasus Platform Ops <ops@pegasus-cloud.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

Hi Ben,

We observed an increase in 5xx rates and tail latency for your account's inference endpoints between 08:15 and 08:57 PT this morning while Pegasus performed a rolling network maintenance affecting our east-us backbone. Our initial aggregation shows ~42 minutes of partial degradation (elevated latency and ~2.4% 5xx). I'm attaching our outage log (pegasus-outage-log.csv).

Per our standard process, we'd like Redwood to confirm the impact on your side and advise if you're requesting a credit under the SLA. If you can share your request and any evidence, we'll route it to billing.

Thanks,
Elena Soto
Senior Support Lead
Pegasus Cloud

Attachment: pegasus-outage-log.csv (text/csv, 23 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena@pegasus-cloud.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Incident: Elevated errors/latency impacting /v1/inference - 2026-11-01

Elena,

Thanks for the heads up and for the log. We've pulled our telemetry for the same window. Key notes:
- Redwood's edge metrics show a single 5xx spike from 08:22–08:27 (approx 5 minutes); host-level latency had several 95th-percentile blips between 08:18–08:30 but aggregated token-level latency across RPCs stayed within SLA bands for ~95% of traffic.
- Our automated prefix-KV cache shows no eviction events correlated with the window. We did see increased TCP retransmits originating in the Pegasus network segments (matching your log).

We will attach our internal response timeline and a snippet of the request-level latencies for the period (redwood-response-timeline.pdf). Based on a preliminary read, Redwood believes the majority of the 42-minute window is attributable to Pegasus' maintenance (third-party). Per SLA section 5.2 (Provider outages vs. Third-Party dependency), we dispute a full-credit allocation and request credit apportionment tied to traffic-level impact.

Proposed ask: Pegasus to propose a quantified credit for the portion you attribute to your network (we'll provide our per-route delta to compute proportional credit). Can you confirm the exact start/end timestamps you used and whether internal retries were suppressed by your maintenance policy?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachment: redwood-response-timeline.pdf (application/pdf, 56 KB)

source.subject: Dedicated capacity validation — performance next steps and test plan
messages:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com>; Sarah Wu <sarah.wu@nexahealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-01T15:12:00-08:00
Subject: Post-demo recap — perf targets and validation plan

Thanks again for carving out time today — great session. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment on SLOs and the validation plan if we move to a Dedicated (reserved) pool: 

Key SLO targets you asked about (NexaHealth goals):
- Latency: p95 end-to-end generation <= 180 ms for 256-token streaming completions (single-turn predictive API)
- Sustained throughput: 5k tokens/sec aggregate across a pool serving 1k concurrent lightweight streams
- Concurrency: steady-state 800 concurrent live sessions with occasional 1k user spikes (burst window ~3m)
- Streaming behavior: partial tokens should appear within 60 ms after model emits them (low first-byte latency)

Initial sizing hypothesis (ballpark):
- Hardware shortlist: H100 (80GB int8 path), A100 (40/80GB), and one inference-optimized L4-based node for lower-cost baseline testing.
- Reserved throughput target: configure pool for 6k tokens/sec headroom; autoscale policy to add bursts up to 9k tokens/sec for up to 10 minutes.
- Suggested concurrency shards: 4 nodes with continuous batching + prefix/KV cache to keep p95 in budget; we'll validate with synthetic streams.

Attached: nexa_slo_estimates_v1.xlsx (rough compute-time and token-cost model), plus demo-recording-20261101.mp4.

Proposed validation plan (short):
1) We run 3-day perf run: baseline (A100), H100, and L4 variant. Each run: 6h warmup, 12h steady state with realistic mix (60% short chat, 30% multi-turn, 10% long-context batch queries).
2) Metrics: p50/p90/p95 latency, tail latency at 99.9, token throughput, GPU utilization, cost per 1M tokens, and streaming first-byte latency.
3) Failure modes: run a few overloaded scenarios to confirm graceful fallback behavior (route to cheaper variant or reduce batch size).
4) Deliverable: a one-page recommendation with cost/latency tradeoffs + runbook for Reserved Pool (including autoscale knobs and rollback thresholds).

Ask from Nexa: can you confirm the expected traffic mix and whether any sessions require sub-100ms first-byte guarantees? Also share a sample 1-hour replay or synthetic script if available so we can align the test profile.

I'll loop in Engineering (Marcus/Vanessa) to sketch the test matrix and infra needs. If procurement wants a provisional pricing model we can provide an initial reserved-capacity quote after the first perf run.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142

[Attachment: nexa_slo_estimates_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)]
[Attachment: demo-recording-20261101.mp4 (video)]
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T17:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap — perf targets and validation plan

Thanks Alex — clear summary. A couple of engineering clarifications from my side before we propose the final runbook: 

1) Tokenization and sequence lengths: the cost model in the spreadsheet assumes average output length 256 tokens and average input length 32 tokens. If Nexa's multi-turn sessions tend to have longer context (~1k tokens), GPU memory and KV cache behavior will change the sizing significantly.

2) Streaming-first-byte: to hit sub-60ms we need to ensure the serving path uses small-batch low-latency kernels (no heavy quantization path that batches aggressively). H100 gives a better tail profile for streaming, A100 is closer on cost per token for large batches. L4 is cost-efficient but we should expect higher p95 for streaming workloads.

3) Autoscale policy: propose target utilization 65% for each reserved node and burst policy to add two hot nodes at 85% sustained for 3m. That gives room for concurrency spikes while keeping steady-state cost predictable.

I'll draft a test matrix matrix.csv and suggested JMeter/streaming-playback commands for the synthetic run. Vanessa — can you capture the exact req/resp shapes from the demo (sample payloads) so we can mimic client behavior (partial chunk sizes, keepalive)?

-Marcus
Principal Performance Engineer | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Wu <sarah.wu@nexahealth.com>, procurement@nexahealth.com
Date: 2026-11-02T09:18:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap — perf targets and validation plan

Alex / Marcus — appreciate the detailed plan. Quick answers from our side: 

- Traffic mix: the 60/30/10 split is accurate for most hours. Peak hours during business ops see closer to 70% short chat. The multi-turn sessions often accumulate to ~600–900 tokens over a typical support case, not 1k typically.
- First-byte: ideally <80ms. We can accept 120–180ms p95 for whole-turn latency as long as the streaming chunks begin earlier; i.e., users see incremental text quickly.
- We have a 1-hour replay of anonymized events we can share (S3 link); I'll coordinate with Sarah to get you upload access.

Procurement note: we need the provisional TCO before our Nov 15 budget review (ballpark cost + one-time validation engagement fee).

We'll get back with the S3 link today. Thanks,
Liam
Head of Platform Integrations | NexaHealth
liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-02T10:32:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap — perf targets and validation plan

Liam — thanks, that clarifies a lot. A couple of operational asks so our perf runs are realistic: 

- Please confirm the TLS termination pattern (client-side keepalive, connection reuse counts) and whether users typically open a long-lived stream or re-open per-turn.
- For the replay: we need per-event timestamps and an indication of which sessions include file attachments (those increase token counts). If you can't provide raw timestamps, a synthetic distribution (events/minute) is fine.

Next steps on our side: I'll stand up the three candidate pools (H100, A100, L4) in our staging region (us-west-2) and schedule the first A100 baseline run for 2026-11-04 01:00 UTC. Marcus — I'll share the test harness and a small checklist for pre-run validation.

-Vanessa
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai
From: Sarah Wu <sarah.wu@nexahealth.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com>, procurement@nexahealth.com
Date: 2026-11-03T11:05:00-08:00
Subject: Fwd: S3 upload — Nexa anonymized replay and procurement note

Forwarding the S3 link and a short procurement attachment.

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Sarah Wu <sarah.wu@nexahealth.com>
Date: 2026-11-03T10:50:00-08:00
To: alex.martinez@redwood.ai
Subject: S3 upload: nexareplay-202610.zip

Hi Alex — the anonymized 1-hour replay is uploaded (s3://nexa-prod-ml/replays/nexareplay-202610.zip). It contains per-event timestamps, token counts, and flags for attachments.

Procurement attached our standard PO template (Nexa_PO_template.pdf) — we will fill with provisional numbers after your validation run.

Thanks,
Sarah
Engineering Program Manager | NexaHealth
[Attachment: Nexa_PO_template.pdf (pdf)]

Procurement note: we need the provisional TCO before our Nov 15 budget review (ballpark cost + one-time validation engagement fee).

We'll get back with the S3 link today. Thanks,
Liam
Head of Platform Integrations | NexaHealth
liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com

To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-02T10:32:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap — perf targets and validation plan

Liam — thanks, that clarifies a lot. A couple of operational asks so our perf runs are realistic: 

- Please confirm the TLS termination pattern (client-side keepalive, connection reuse counts) and whether users typically open a long-lived stream or re-open per-turn.
- For the replay: we need per-event timestamps and an indication of which sessions include file attachments (those increase token counts). If you can't provide raw timestamps, a synthetic distribution (events/minute) is fine.

Next steps on our side: I'll stand up the three candidate pools (H100, A100, L4) in our staging region (us-west-2) and schedule the first A100 baseline run for 2026-11-04 01:00 UTC. Marcus — I'll share the test harness and a small checklist for pre-run validation.

-Vanessa
Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai

text:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com>; Sarah Wu <sarah.wu@nexahealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-01T15:12:00-08:00
Subject: Post-demo recap — perf targets and validation plan

Thanks again for carving out time today — great session. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment on SLOs and the validation plan if we move to a Dedicated (reserved) pool: 

Key SLO targets you asked about (NexaHealth goals):
- Latency: p95 end-to-end generation <= 180 ms for 256-token streaming completions (single-turn predictive API)
- Sustained throughput: 5k tokens/sec aggregate across a pool serving 1k concurrent lightweight streams
- Concurrency: steady-state 800 concurrent live sessions with occasional 1k user spikes (burst window ~3m)
- Streaming behavior: partial tokens should appear within 60 ms after model emits them (low first-byte latency)

Initial sizing hypothesis (ballpark):
- Hardware shortlist: H100 (80GB int8 path), A100 (40/80GB), and one inference-optimized L4-based node for lower-cost baseline testing.
- Reserved throughput target: configure pool for 6k tokens/sec headroom; autoscale policy to add bursts up to 9k tokens/sec for up to 10 minutes.
- Suggested concurrency shards: 4 nodes with continuous batching + prefix/KV cache to keep p95 in budget; we'll validate with synthetic streams.

Attached: nexa_slo_estimates_v1.xlsx (rough compute-time and token-cost model), plus demo-recording-20261101.mp4.

Proposed validation plan (short):
1) We run 3-day perf run: baseline (A100), H100, and L4 variant. Each run: 6h warmup, 12h steady state with realistic mix (60% short chat, 30% multi-turn, 10% long-context batch queries).
2) Metrics: p50/p90/p95 latency, tail latency at 99.9, token throughput, GPU utilization, cost per 1M tokens, and streaming first-byte latency.
3) Failure modes: run a few overloaded scenarios to confirm graceful fallback behavior (route to cheaper variant or reduce batch size).
4) Deliverable: a one-page recommendation with cost/latency tradeoffs + runbook for Reserved Pool (including autoscale knobs and rollback thresholds).

Ask from Nexa: can you confirm the expected traffic mix and whether any sessions require sub-100ms first-byte guarantees? Also share a sample 1-hour replay or synthetic script if available so we can align the test profile.

I'll loop in Engineering (Marcus/Vanessa) to sketch the test matrix and infra needs. If procurement wants a provisional pricing model we can provide an initial reserved-capacity quote after the first perf run.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142

[Attachment: nexa_slo_estimates_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)]
[Attachment: demo-recording-20261101.mp4 (video)]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com>
Date: 2026-11-01T17:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap — perf targets and validation plan

Thanks Alex — clear summary. A couple of engineering clarifications from my side before we propose the final runbook: 

1) Tokenization and sequence lengths: the cost model in the spreadsheet assumes average output length 256 tokens and average input length 32 tokens. If Nexa's multi-turn sessions tend to have longer context (~1k tokens), GPU memory and KV cache behavior will change the sizing significantly.

2) Streaming-first-byte: to hit sub-60ms we need to ensure the serving path uses small-batch low-latency kernels (no heavy quantization path that batches aggressively). H100 gives a better tail profile for streaming, A100 is closer on cost per token for large batches. L4 is cost-efficient but we should expect higher p95 for streaming workloads.

3) Autoscale policy: propose target utilization 65% for each reserved node and burst policy to add two hot nodes at 85% sustained for 3m. That gives room for concurrency spikes while keeping steady-state cost predictable.

I'll draft a test matrix matrix.csv and suggested JMeter/streaming-playback commands for the synthetic run. Vanessa — can you capture the exact req/resp shapes from the demo (sample payloads) so we can mimic client behavior (partial chunk sizes, keepalive)?

-Marcus
Principal Performance Engineer | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai


From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Wu <sarah.wu@nexahealth.com>, procurement@nexahealth.com
Date: 2026-11-02T09:18:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap — perf targets and validation plan

Alex / Marcus — appreciate the detailed plan. Quick answers from our side: 

- Traffic mix: the 60/30/10 split is accurate for most hours. Peak hours during business ops see closer to 70% short chat. The multi-turn sessions often accumulate to ~600–900 tokens over a typical support case, not 1k typically.
- First-byte: ideally <80ms. We can accept 120–180ms p95 for whole-turn latency as long as the streaming chunks begin earlier; i.e., users see incremental text quickly.
- We have a 1-hour replay of anonymized events we can share (S3 link); I'll coordinate with Sarah to get you upload access.

Procurement note: we need the provisional TCO before our Nov 15 budget review (ballpark cost + one-time validation engagement fee).

We'll get back with the S3 link today. Thanks,
Liam
Head of Platform Integrations | NexaHealth
liam.oconnor@nexahealth.com

messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Brooks <tbrooks@cloudport.io>
Date: 2026-11-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Monthly KPI packet + QBR prep — exec notes

Team — attaching the November KPI packet and a short agenda for next week's QBR prep (attached: 20261101-kpi-dashboard.pdf, qbr-prep-agenda.gdoc). High-level callouts from the dashboard (please scan before EOD Monday):

- Latency: P95 increased ~18% week-over-week driven by long-tail spikes on /chat-route (seen during the 10/24 deploy). Rolling average still within SLO but the tail risks affecting latency SLAs for Dedicated customers.
- Cost: Token spend is on a +14% trajectory month-over-month; model mix shift and a few high-throughput accounts are the main contributors. See cost-breakdown-nov.csv for per-customer run-rates.
- Uptime: 99.93% last 30d. Two short infra incidents (2–3 min each) caused minor error bumps; full RCA in the doc.
- Usage: Active routes up 9%, peak qps increased ~12% at 9:30–11:30 PT on weekdays.

Asks for the QBR / OKR check-in:
1) Marcus — can you confirm the proposed mitigation (cache TTL bump + guardrail for the /chat-route) and whether we can ship a hotfix this week?
2) Rishi — please prepare the one-slide cost breakdown for execs (top 3 drivers + 90-day run-rate).
3) Laura — approval to include a short customer-impact blurb for Brightline Health in the executive summary? Elena is copied.
4) Grace — add a short section on Ops readiness and staffing gaps for next quarter.

Please comment inline on the gdoc if you have slide edits. If no major objections, I’ll finalize the QBR deck Tuesday morning and circulate the exec summary.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Head of Product
Redwood Inference

Attachments: 20261101-kpi-dashboard.pdf (18 pages, charts + highlights), qbr-prep-agenda.gdoc (live), cost-breakdown-nov.csv (per-account run rates)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Tom Brooks <tbrooks@cloudport.io>
Date: 2026-11-01T13:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Monthly KPI packet + QBR prep — exec notes

Thanks Ava — quick notes inline.

On Mon, Nov 1, 2026 at 9:12 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — attaching the November KPI packet and a short agenda for next week's QBR prep...

- Latency: Confirmed that the 10/24 deploy introduced a regression in context handling for the /chat-route which created long tail. Ops rolled back the offending kernel within 40m; we still had cache warming issues afterward.
- Mitigation: I support the cache TTL bump for public routes (suggest +25% for route group A) plus a short-term circuit (reject > X tokens from unverified origins). Ops can ship hotfix Wed EOD if we greenlight.
- Cost: I dug into the cost-breakdown CSV — two enterprise accounts (Brightline and one unnamed) are ~46% of incremental spend. We should propose a pricing/usage review with Brightline in the QBR.

Action items I can take this week:
- Draft the technical appendix slide showing before/after P95 for the hotfix (I'll attach a small chart).
- Coordinate with SRE to get the hotfix milestone on Wednesday.

– Marcus
Senior Engineering Manager, Serving Runtime

Attachment: cost-breakdown-nov.csv (referenced above)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-02T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Monthly KPI packet + QBR prep — exec notes

Ava/Marcus — pulled the numbers and sketched a one-slide summary for execs. Quick summary here (will paste to the gdoc):

- Top 3 cost drivers: 1) Brightline bursty usage (40% of MoM increase), 2) model mix shift to larger parameter variant for research tests (+6% cost), 3) Testnet jobs mis-tagged as production (we're quarantining these).
- Proposed ask for execs: approve a short usage review with Brightline (billing adjustment and co-designed throttling) and permission to run an autoscaling tweak experiment for two weeks to reclaim ~8–10% cost.
- OKR check: On track for "Reduce inference unit cost by 12%" — currently at -6% vs baseline; if we approve autoscaling test and Marcus executes cache hotfix, expect to hit ~10–11% by EOY.

Slides I’ll prep:
- Exec one-liner (impact + ask)
- 90-day run-rate chart
- Proposed pricing/ops plan for Brightline (owner: Sales + Finance)

Note: Laura, if Brightline consent is required for the usage review, please advise — Elena is on-copy.

Thanks —
Rishi Malhotra
Product Ops

(Inline: short table pasted to gdoc and link to 'cost-breakdown-nov.csv')

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@brightline.health>, Tom Brooks <tbrooks@cloudport.io>
Date: 2026-11-03T11:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Monthly KPI packet + QBR prep — exec notes

Appreciate the updates — fronting a few decisions so the deck can be finalized:

1) Approve inclusion of Brightline Health as the customer example in the exec summary. Elena — we'll only include non-sensitive, aggregated usage/cost context; flag anything you need redacted.
2) Marcus: greenlight the hotfix schedule for Wed EOD. Please include rollback criteria in the ticket.
3) Rishi & Ava: we need a concise 200–300 word executive summary that can be handed to the CRO in advance of the QBR. Rishi, your one-slide + Ava's one-liner are fine; Ava please assemble and send final deck by Tuesday 08:00 PT.
4) Grace: please enumerate any hiring/coverage asks (two-week horizon) and include estimated impact on uptime/cost if we don't fill them.

I'll prep a short note for the CEO summarizing asks and the proposed approval items. If anyone expects dissent, flag now. Otherwise I’ll mark these as approved and we’ll move to finalize.

– Laura Bennett
VP, GTM Operations
Redwood Inference

-----Original Message-----
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Sent: Monday, November 1, 2026 9:12 AM
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>; Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>; Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>; Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Subject: Monthly KPI packet + QBR prep — exec notes

(See original message and attachments)

– Marcus
Senior Engineering Manager, Serving Runtime

Attachment: cost-breakdown-nov.csv (referenced above)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-02T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Monthly KPI packet + QBR prep — exec notes

Ava/Marcus — pulled the numbers and sketched a one-slide summary for execs. Quick summary here (will paste to the gdoc):

- Top 3 cost drivers: 1) Brightline bursty usage (40% of MoM increase), 2) model mix shift to larger parameter variant for research tests (+6% cost), 3) Testnet jobs mis-tagged as production (we're quarantining these).
- Proposed ask for execs: approve a short usage review with Brightline (billing adjustment and co-designed throttling) and permission to run an autoscaling tweak experiment for two weeks to reclaim ~8–10% cost.
- OKR check: On track for "Reduce inference unit cost by 12%" — currently at -6% vs baseline; if we approve autoscaling test and Marcus executes cache hotfix, expect to hit ~10–11% by EOY.

Slides I’ll prep:
- Exec one-liner (impact + ask)
- 90-day run-rate chart
- Proposed pricing/ops plan for Brightline (owner: Sales + Finance)

Note: Laura, if Brightline consent is required for the usage review, please advise — Elena is on-copy.

Thanks —
Rishi Malhotra
Product Ops

(Inline: short table pasted to gdoc and link to 'cost-breakdown-nov.csv')

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena@brightline.health>, Tom Brooks <tbrooks@cloudport.io>
Date: 2026-11-03T11:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Monthly KPI packet + QBR prep — exec notes

Appreciate the updates — fronting a few decisions so the deck can be finalized:

1) Approve inclusion of Brightline Health as the customer example in the exec summary. Elena — we'll only include non-sensitive, aggregated usage/cost context; flag anything you need redacted.
2) Marcus: greenlight the hotfix schedule for Wed EOD. Please include rollback criteria in the ticket.
3) Rishi & Ava: we need a concise 200–300 word executive summary that can be handed to the CRO in advance of the QBR. Rishi, your one-slide + Ava's one-liner are fine; Ava please assemble and send final deck by Tuesday 08:00 PT.
4) Grace: please enumerate any hiring/coverage asks (two-week horizon) and include estimated impact on uptime/cost if we don't fill them.

I'll prep a short note for the CEO summarizing asks and the proposed approval items. If anyone expects dissent, flag now. Otherwise I’ll mark these as approved and we’ll move to finalize.

– Laura Bennett
VP, GTM Operations
Redwood Inference

To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>; Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>; Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>; Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>

source.subject: Unexpected embedding usage — test environment traffic caused overage
messages:
From: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@astercloud.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: AsterCloud Billing <billing@astercloud.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sudden 10x jump in embedding usage (Oct 24-28) — please investigate

Hi Ava,

We just received the October invoice and there is a ~10x spike in embedding-related charges for the period Oct 24–28. Our engineering team confirms we didn’t ramp production traffic — only some internal test runs on the sandbox. We estimate ~12M tokens but the invoice shows ~120M. Can you investigate what was counted as billable tokens and share the raw usage slice for that window?

We need an explanation for procurement and to determine if this is a metering/config issue on our side or something in Redwood’s tokenization/aggregation. We’ve attached the billing alert screenshot.

Thanks,
Marta Lopez
Head of Platform Operations, AsterCloud Inc.
marta.lopez@astercloud.com
+1 415-555-0123

[attachment: billing-alert-oct28.png]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@astercloud.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sudden 10x jump in embedding usage (Oct 24-28) — please investigate

Hi Marta — thanks for the flag and the screenshot. I’m looping in Soojin (billing) and Karthik (infra/telemetry). We’ll pull the raw usage export and a sample of requests for the window you called out.

Quick heads-up on what we’ll look for: tokenization differences between chunked documents vs. your expected counting, duplicated requests from CI/test harnesses, and any streaming/chunk replays that could have inflated counts.

Can you confirm whether the sandbox keys were exposed to a shared CI runner around Oct 24 (we’ve seen similar patterns when a load-test job got scheduled accidentally)? If helpful, we’ll DOA a provisional credit while we finish the audit.

I attached a small sample request extractor so you can see the fields we’ll compare.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
[attachment: sample-requests-clip.jsonl]

From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@astercloud.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Oct 2026 08:46:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sudden 10x jump in embedding usage (Oct 24-28) — initial telemetry export attached

Hi team — I pulled the initial usage export for AsterCloud for Oct 20–31 and attached the slice (usage-export-20261029.csv). Preliminary observations:

- There is a cluster of requests between Oct 24 02:10–04:20 UTC with unusually high embedding token counts.
- The request metadata shows the same API key used across ~8 different client IPs (likely a CI runner cycling through ephemeral addresses).
- Many of the large-count entries have long 'document' payloads that were chunked into ~2–4 chunks each on the server side and each chunk was counted toward the token tally.

Next steps: Karthik, can you pull a sample of the raw request bodies and the server-side chunking logs for that interval (we need to confirm whether client-side chunking vs server-side chunking is responsible)? Marcus, can you sanity-check whether any retry/backoff behavior or streaming reconnects caused repeated charging for the same logical request?

I’ve CC’d Marta for visibility. If this turns out to be an unintended CI ramp, we should propose a one-time invoice adjustment and provide guidance to avoid repeats.

Thanks,
Soojin Lee
Revenue Operations Manager, Redwood Inference

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@astercloud.com>
Date: Sun, 01 Nov 2026 07:21:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sudden 10x jump in embedding usage (Oct 24-28) — root cause notes

Folks — I grabbed server logs for the Oct 24 02:00–05:00 UTC window and sampled 150 requests (attached sample-requests-clip.jsonl was expanded on our side). Key findings:

- AsterCloud’s sandbox key appears to have been used by a scheduled job that re-run a document ingestion pipeline with long multi-page PDFs. Those documents were chunked server-side into 1k–2k token segments for embedding. Each chunk triggers an embedding call and is counted independently.
- There were intermittent client retries: we see several 409/timeout-induced retries where the client retried after a short backoff, but because the pipeline did not use idempotency tokens for embed operations, retries resulted in duplicate embedding calls.
- Streaming reconnects: for a small percentage (~4%) of requests the websocket/stream connection dropped and the client resumed by re-sending the last chunk without idempotency. That produced duplicate chargeable calls.

Action items we recommend to AsterCloud:
1) Rotate sandbox keys and lock the CI runner to a narrow IP range or use key restrictions (we can provide a how-to).
2) Add a deduplication idempotency token for batching/ingest jobs so retries are tolerated without duplicate billable calls.
3) Use our recommended chunking heuristics (we'll send a short doc) to reduce number of chunks per document.

From Redwood side, I’m proposing a provisional credit of 75% of the spike delta while we finish the full audit (we applied the provisional note to draft credit note id CN-20261101-P). Marcus — can you confirm the credit calc and whether any tokenization edge-case should be adjusted because of server-side chunking behavior?

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Senior Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marta Lopez <marta.lopez@astercloud.com>
Date: Sun, 01 Nov 2026 15:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Sudden embedding usage — credit proposal and next steps

Thanks — I validated the sample and the proposed credit math. Notes:

- We recomputed billable tokens using our conservative model (counting prompt + chunked doc tokens, collapsing immediate retries within 30s as duplicates where logs indicate identical payloads). Using that method, the spike delta is ~98M tokens. A 75% provisional credit equals ~73.5M tokens credited (I converted tokens to your plan's unit price to build CN-20261101-P).
- I confirmed that streaming reconnects accounted for ~3.9% of duplicates — those are included in the credit calculation.
- Attached: provisional-credit-note-20261101.pdf (internal finance template). If AsterCloud agrees, we’ll post the credit to the Nov invoice schedule and issue a final audit summary within 7 business days.

Marta — once you sign off on the provisional credit we’ll:
1) Apply the credit as a negative line on your next invoice (visible in the console).
2) Deliver the audit CSV showing deduped requests and a short tech note with recommended idempotency headers and chunking limits.

If AsterCloud prefers a one-time refund instead of a credit, Finance can do that but it takes an extra 5–7 business days to process.

— Marcus Lin
Principal Product Reliability Engineer, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

On Sun, Nov 01, 2026 at 07:21 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:
> Folks — I grabbed server logs for the Oct 24 02:00–05:00 UTC window and sampled 150 requests...

source.subject: Request: async run SLO tolerance and idempotency plan
messages:
From: Maya Singh <maya@acme.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 28 Oct 2026 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Async runs - request for SLO tolerance and idempotency confirmation

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the Dedicated cluster looked promising. We're moving towards production ingestion for our nightly enrichment pipeline ("Nightly Enrich V2") and need to lock down operational commitments so our compliance and platform teams can sign off. Two things we need clarified:

1) Job completion window: our runs are variable but the target is to have all jobs completed within 24 hours of submission. Given spikes, can Redwood provide a formal tolerance? e.g., 99% within 12 hours, 100% within 48 hours, with defined crediting if missed.

2) Retries & idempotency: our controller retries failed tasks aggressively. We need guidance on idempotency tokens you require so retries are safe across your async queue and any automatic server-side retries. Specifically: guaranteed-at-most-once vs at-least-once behavior, and any dedupe window we should assume.

I've attached a workload_summary.csv with job sizes and a signed sla_req_form.pdf to help. Can you put together a short mitigation plan and note any constraints (capacity, reserved windows, or required request headers)?

Appreciate it — Maya
Maya Singh
Head of Platform Engineering, ACME Corp
maya@acme.ai

Attachments: workload_summary.csv (csv), sla_req_form.pdf (pdf)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Maya Singh <maya@acme.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 28 Oct 2026 11:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Async runs - request for SLO tolerance and idempotency confirmation

Maya — thanks for the detail and the CSV. We'll take this through both product and infra. Quick ack and next steps below: 

- We can propose a temporary SLO carveout for Nightly Enrich V2 while we finalize capacity sizing (Dedicated).
- To scope the carveout precisely we need: peak concurrent tasks, distribution of small/medium/large job lengths (the CSV helps), and whether you require hard retry guarantees or can tolerate replay within a dedupe window.

Marcus — can you draft a technical mitigation plan and recommend the idempotency token format we should require (header name + TTL)? Laura — can you start loop-in for contract language for a temporary tolerance and the process for credits if the window is missed?

I'll follow up with ACME by EOD tomorrow with a draft.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0123

On Wed, Oct 28, 2026 at 08:14 AM Maya Singh <maya@acme.ai> wrote:
> Hi Ben,
>
> Thanks again for the demo last week — the Dedicated cluster looked promising...

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Singh <maya@acme.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 29 Oct 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Async runs - request for SLO tolerance and idempotency confirmation

Hi all,

I dug into the workload_summary.csv. Quick read: 72% small (<30s), 22% medium (30s–6m), 6% large (>6m). Peak concurrent tasks observed in your sample: ~9k. With Dedicated capacity we can hit these throughput numbers but need to be explicit about failure modes and dedupe. Proposal below:

Operational SLO proposal (temporary carveout):
- Target: 99% of jobs complete within 12 hours, 99.95% within 24 hours, 100% within 48 hours (credit-triggered if 24hr 99.95% missed).
- Rationale: gives buffer for bursty nights and hardware maintenance windows; 48hr final bound avoids unbounded tail.

Retries & idempotency requirements:
- Require an Idempotency-Token header (Idempotency-Token: <uuid-v4>) on job submission; tokens are scoped per-job-id.
- Server dedupe window: 72 hours for token-based dedupe (tokens expire after 72h). After expiry a replay is treated as a new job.
- Delivery semantics: at-least-once for the async queue; with the token dedupe you get effective once semantics for processing. Clients MUST be prepared for duplicates within the dedupe window for external side effects.
- Recommended retry/backoff: exponential backoff starting at 30s, max backoff 20m, with jitter; controller should stop retries after 6 attempts and escalate with a failure webhook.

Monitoring and credits:
- We'll expose per-job lifecycle events in the Console (submission, started, completed, failed). We can create a route-level SLO monitor and an alert that computes 24h completion percent.
- For the temporary carveout we propose a credit schedule: if 24h 99.95% misses by >0.5%, 5% service credit for the affected billing month; >2% miss -> 15% credit and a mandatory root cause review.

Operational caveats:
- The credit policy assumes Dedicated reserved capacity with the configured autoscaling policy (we'll attach recommended pool sizing). If you're on shared Hosted capacity the tail can be longer; we recommend Dedicated for this workload.

I'll attach a draft operational_runbook.md and pool-sizing notes. If this looks reasonable Ben, I'll turn it into a short doc for legal.

Thanks,
Marcus Lin
Principal Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai

- 99% complete within 12 hours; 99.95% within 24 hours; final completion guarantee within 48 hours.
- Dedupe window: Idempotency-Token header required; dedupe window 72 hours.

source.subject: Phase-gated prepay incentive request — Catapult Health
messages:
From: Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Hi Kimberly + Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the throughput and latency numbers look promising for our remote clinical summarization workload. Leadership asked me to explore a 2–3 year committed plan, but they want a structure that:

- Keeps year 1 cash outlay lower via phase-gated prepay (so we can prove production metrics)
- Gives escalating discounts if we hit certain throughput thresholds and maintain 99.7% SLA
- Allows a mid-term reallocation if utilisation skews heavily toward year 2

I've attached a simple spreadsheet with a proposed staging (Year1 40%, Year2 35%, Year3 25%) and suggested breakpoints. Can Redwood provide a formal proposal that includes: a) the discount tiers, b) true-up mechanics, and c) a short clawback for material underrun?

We'd like to present something to our CFO by Oct 20.

Best,
Sara
Sara Patel
Dir, Platform Ops — Catapult Health
attachments: Catapult-Phase-Gated-Spreadsheet.xlsx
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Sara — thanks for the clear ask and the spreadsheet. Ben and I reviewed; this is the right kind of flexibility for health-tech buyers. A few quick clarifying Qs so we can scope the proposal:

1) When you say "phase-gated" — do you want firm cash prepay at the start of each year (but with defined release thresholds), or a single upfront prepay split by internal billing schedule?
2) Is Catapult open to a small uplift on unit price in exchange for a guaranteed annual renewal floor?
3) Which patent/data retention requirements should we flag for the Private deployment option (might affect discounts)?

We'll draft a two-option proposal (2-year accelerated and 3-year laddered) with explicit true-up windows and a 60–90 day clawback for underrun. Marcus, could you run a quick ROI sketch for both options?

Thanks — Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference — redwood.com
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-05T14:21:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Attaching a short ROI note and two pricing vectors:

- 2-year Accelerated (A): Year1 45% prepay, Year2 55% prepay settlement; ~20% blended discount if Year1 hits 50% of committed tokens.
- 3-year Laddered (B): Year1 35% / Year2 35% / Year3 30%; step discounts at 60/85/110% of baseline usage (5/8/12% respectively).

I flagged a potential margin hit under A if year 2 doesn't materialize; recommend a 90-day true-up window and a clawback capped at 15% of credited discount. Laura — legal comments below on simple contract language.

Attachments: Draft-AML-Terms-v3.pdf
Marcus
Marcus Lin
Principal, Finance Operations | Redwood Inference
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-07T09:06:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Quick legal:
- Keep clawback language limited to material underrun defined as cumulative usage < 70% of committed over 12 months.
- Add a one-time mid-term reallocation clause: customers can reassign up to 20% of remaining year commitment across years without penalty (requires 45-day notice).
- For Public/Private deployment mix, require a security appendix to qualify for the top-tier discounts.

I'll draft standard clauses and mark optional toggles. Laura
Laura Bennett
Head of Contracts & Privacy | Redwood Inference
From: Rajiv Singh <rajiv.singh@catapulthealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>, procurement <procurement@catapulthealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T08:33:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Kimberly — thank you. High level from finance: Catapult prefers Year1 cash exposure <= 50% of the total committed consideration for FY1. They like the 3-year laddered because it reduces near-term cash. A few asks:

- Can Redwood provide a sliding discount that also accounts for peak-day throughput (we have bursty months)?
- Any flexibility on the cap for reallocation? Would 30% be reasonable?
- Can SLA-based credits be pre-funded as a small reserve?

We'd appreciate a clean redline we can share with our GC.

-Rajiv
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Singh <rajiv.singh@catapulthealth.com>, Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T11:17:00-07:00
Subject: Proposal: 3-year laddered prepay with peak-day rider (initial offer)

Rajiv / Sara — based on your feedback we've prepared an initial, conservative commercial offer:

Option B (recommended, 3-year laddered):
- Commitment: 3-year term, baseline annual quantity = 1.0x
- Billing: Year1 prepay 40%, Year2 prepay 35% invoiced at start of year, Year3 prepay 25%
- Discount schedule (applies to consumed tokens vs baseline): 0–60%: 0% discount; 60–85%: 6%; 85–110%: 10%; >110%: 14%
- Peak-day rider: measured peak-day throughput above baseline contributes to a separate burst pool with a 5% reduced unit uplift when aggregated monthly (helps burst months)
- Reallocation: up to 25% reallocation allowed (45-day notice) — no penalty for first reallocation; subsequent reallocations limited and subject to small admin fee
- Clawback/true-up: 90-day annual true-up; clawback capped at 12% of credits issued in previous year; material underrun defined as cumulative consumption < 68%
- Security appendix required for Private deployment discounts (signed addendum)

We can tighten the reallocation cap to 30% if Catapult moves to a 45% Year1 prepay instead of 40%. I'll attach a marked commercial term sheet and a draft redline. Can you confirm if your GC prefers DocuSign or a downloadable redline?

Next steps:
1) Catapult to confirm preferred Option (B vs A) and Year1 cash tolerance by Oct 26.
2) Redwood to send formal redline/DocuSign on Oct 27.

Thanks — Kimberly
From: Rajiv Singh <rajiv.singh@catapulthealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>, procurement <procurement@catapulthealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-30T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 3-year laddered prepay with peak-day rider (initial offer)

Kimberly — appreciate the clear proposal. Catapult's CFO reviewed and we're broadly aligned with Option B. Two final asks before we proceed to GC:

1) Increase the >110% discount tier to 16% (we expect to grow quickly if early trials succeed).
2) Accept a 30% reallocation cap while keeping Year1 at 40% prepay (our treasury team prefers the lower upfront).

If you can accept both, we'll instruct our GC to prioritize review and request a DocuSign. Otherwise we may propose 45% Year1 to get 30% reallocation.

We'll wait for your confirmation so we can initiate DocuSign next week.

Regards,
Rajiv
Rajiv Singh
VP Finance — Catapult Health

1) When you say "phase-gated" — do you want firm cash prepay at the start of each year (but with defined release thresholds), or a single upfront prepay split by internal billing schedule?
2) Is Catapult open to a small uplift on unit price in exchange for a guaranteed annual renewal floor?
3) Which patent/data retention requirements should we flag for the Private deployment option (might affect discounts)?

We'll draft a two-option proposal (2-year accelerated and 3-year laddered) with explicit true-up windows and a 60–90 day clawback for underrun. Marcus, could you run a quick ROI sketch for both options?

Thanks — Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference — redwood.com


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-05T14:21:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Attaching a short ROI note and two pricing vectors:

- 2-year Accelerated (A): Year1 45% prepay, Year2 55% prepay settlement; ~20% blended discount if Year1 hits 50% of committed tokens.
- 3-year Laddered (B): Year1 35% / Year2 35% / Year3 30%; step discounts at 60/85/110% of baseline usage (5/8/12% respectively).

I flagged a potential margin hit under A if year 2 doesn't materialize; recommend a 90-day true-up window and a clawback capped at 15% of credited discount. Laura — legal comments below on simple contract language.

Attachments: Draft-AML-Terms-v3.pdf
Marcus
Marcus Lin
Principal, Finance Operations | Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-07T09:06:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Quick legal:
- Keep clawback language limited to material underrun defined as cumulative usage < 70% of committed over 12 months.
- Add a one-time mid-term reallocation clause: customers can reassign up to 20% of remaining year commitment across years without penalty (requires 45-day notice).
- For Public/Private deployment mix, require a security appendix to qualify for the top-tier discounts.

I'll draft standard clauses and mark optional toggles. Laura
Laura Bennett
Head of Contracts & Privacy | Redwood Inference


From: Rajiv Singh <rajiv.singh@catapulthealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>, procurement <procurement@catapulthealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T08:33:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Interest in multi-year incentive tied to usage corridors

Kimberly — thank you. High level from finance: Catapult prefers Year1 cash exposure <= 50% of the total committed consideration for FY1. They like the 3-year laddered because it reduces near-term cash. A few asks:

- Can Redwood provide a sliding discount that also accounts for peak-day throughput (we have bursty months)?
- Any flexibility on the cap for reallocation? Would 30% be reasonable?
- Can SLA-based credits be pre-funded as a small reserve?

We'd appreciate a clean redline we can share with our GC.

-Rajiv


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Rajiv Singh <rajiv.singh@catapulthealth.com>, Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T11:17:00-07:00
Subject: Proposal: 3-year laddered prepay with peak-day rider (initial offer)

Rajiv / Sara — based on your feedback we've prepared an initial, conservative commercial offer:

Option B (recommended, 3-year laddered):
- Commitment: 3-year term, baseline annual quantity = 1.0x
- Billing: Year1 prepay 40%, Year2 prepay 35% invoiced at start of year, Year3 prepay 25%
- Discount schedule (applies to consumed tokens vs baseline): 0–60%: 0% discount; 60–85%: 6%; 85–110%: 10%; >110%: 14%
- Peak-day rider: measured peak-day throughput above baseline contributes to a separate burst pool with a 5% reduced unit uplift when aggregated monthly (helps burst months)
- Reallocation: up to 25% reallocation allowed (45-day notice) — no penalty for first reallocation; subsequent reallocations limited and subject to small admin fee
- Clawback/true-up: 90-day annual true-up; clawback capped at 12% of credits issued in previous year; material underrun defined as cumulative consumption < 68%
- Security appendix required for Private deployment discounts (signed addendum)

We can tighten the reallocation cap to 30% if Catapult moves to a 45% Year1 prepay instead of 40%. I'll attach a marked commercial term sheet and a draft redline. Can you confirm if your GC prefers DocuSign or a downloadable redline?

Next steps:
1) Catapult to confirm preferred Option (B vs A) and Year1 cash tolerance by Oct 26.
2) Redwood to send formal redline/DocuSign on Oct 27.

Thanks — Kimberly


From: Rajiv Singh <rajiv.singh@catapulthealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Patel <sara.patel@catapulthealth.com>, procurement <procurement@catapulthealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-30T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 3-year laddered prepay with peak-day rider (initial offer)

Kimberly — appreciate the clear proposal. Catapult's CFO reviewed and we're broadly aligned with Option B. Two final asks before we proceed to GC:

1) Increase the >110% discount tier to 16% (we expect to grow quickly if early trials succeed).
2) Accept a 30% reallocation cap while keeping Year1 at 40% prepay (our treasury team prefers the lower upfront).

If you can accept both, we'll instruct our GC to prioritize review and request a DocuSign. Otherwise we may propose 45% Year1 to get 30% reallocation.

We'll wait for your confirmation so we can initiate DocuSign next week.

Regards,
Rajiv
Rajiv Singh
VP Finance — Catapult Health

messages:
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@stellarhealth.com>; Ken Thomas <ken.thomas@stellarhealth.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T09:00:00-07:00
Subject: Intro: Redwood + StellarHealth — VP intro (Thu 10/22) — confirmation

Hi Olivia / Ken —

Thanks again for setting aside time for the intro with your VP today at 10:00 PT. Marcus here from Redwood — just confirming we have the 10:00–10:30 PT slot on your calendar. I attached a short deck with where we’d like to focus (security, latency/cost for inference at scale).

Agenda (high-level):
- 10:00 — quick intros (5 min)
- 10:05 — what Redwood solves for teams running LLMs in production (10 min)
- 10:15 — customer examples + cost patterns (5 min)
- 10:20 — Q&A / next steps (10 min)

Attachments: redwood_intro_v1.pdf (deck), one-pager-security.pdf
Calendar invite: meeting-invite-20261022.ics

If anything changes, ping me here or reply to this thread. Looking forward to it.

Best,
Marcus
Marcus Lin
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@stellarhealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Intro: Redwood + StellarHealth — VP intro (Thu 10/22) — confirmation

Hi Olivia — quick reminder — Marcus and I will join the 10:00 PT intro in ~40 minutes. If the VP needs to join a few minutes late we can hold for 5–7 minutes.

If you prefer to reschedule, no problem — just let us know a couple of windows and we’ll prioritize.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer — Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ken Thomas <ken.thomas@stellarhealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: VP no-show / quick status — StellarHealth intro (10:00 PT)

Folks — quick note from the room: Ken joined from StellarHealth but Olivia's VP (and Olivia) were still in back-to-back meetings. We waited until 10:10 and then ran a 10-minute recap of value props with Ken to avoid wasting time.

What happened in the 10 minutes we had:
- Quick overview of Redwood platform: hosted, dedicated, private deployments
- Highlighted latency + batching options that cut token cost for a high-throughput pipeline (took a quick cost slide from the deck)
- Ken asked for a short security appendix — I dropped one-pager-security.pdf into the chat

Immediate ask:
- Can Ava stitch a 15–20 minute follow-up with Olivia + VP? This needs to be a high-priority rebook (they requested VP-level time).
- I’ll send Ken the slide excerpt and a short summary.

I left the deck and the one-pager in the calendar invite.

— Marcus
(On behalf of the meeting)
From: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@stellarhealth.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>; Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ken Thomas <ken.thomas@stellarhealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-22T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: VP no-show / quick status — StellarHealth intro (10:00 PT)

Marcus / Ava —

So sorry — our VP was pulled into an emergency Ops call and couldn’t make it. Thank you for running through the highlights with Ken and for the materials.

We really want the VP to see this (this is a priority for our cloud modernization initiative). Olivia's availability for a focused 20-minute slot:
- Fri 10/23 08:30–09:00 PT
- Fri 10/23 11:30–12:00 PT
- Mon 10/26 09:00–09:30 PT

Ken is happy to join any of those. If you can take the 10/23 11:30 PT slot, that would be ideal for us.

Apologies again for the disruption — we want to keep momentum.

Thanks,
Olivia Barnes
VP, Strategic Partnerships
StellarHealth
olivia.barnes@stellarhealth.com
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Barnes <olivia.barnes@stellarhealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-23T10:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Redwood x StellarHealth — VP intro (reschedule) — confirmed (Fri 10/23 11:30 PT)

Thanks Olivia — 11:30–11:50 PT on Fri 10/23 is locked on our side. Calendar invite updated for the VP + Ken; I attached the short deck and the security appendix again for quick pre-read.

Quick briefing for the VP intro (2x bullet minutes):
- Why Redwood: predictable inference economics + consistent runtime across hosted/dedicated/private (latency and cost levers we can show live)
- Risk/compliance highlight: SOC2/audit logs + optional VPC/on-prem control plane (see one-pager-security.pdf)
- Ask from you: 15 minutes to validate fit and next step is a technical workshop with their infra lead

Next steps:
- Marcus will send a 2-slide cost snapshot tailored to StellarHealth's projected throughput before the call.
- Ken / Olivia: please share any particular infra constraints or questions you want covered so we can prep.

Attachments: redwood_intro_v1.pdf, one-pager-security.pdf
Calendar: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=redwood-stellar-1023
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/8721

See you Friday —
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer — Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0147
