source.subject: Orchestrating an architecture lab slot with Zenport Analytics — timing and prework
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@zenport.ai>; Priya Kapoor <priya.kapoor@zenport.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-18T16:12:00-07:00
Subject: Architecture lab — slot options + draft agenda

Hi Liam / Priya,

Thanks again for the discovery call this morning — super helpful context. Based on what we discussed, the best next step is a focused architecture lab where our engineers walk through a short live run of the inference topology, integration touchpoints (VPC peering / IAM), and a short cost/latency sizing exercise with a sample workload.

Two 90-minute options that work for our engineering team:
- Tue, Oct 26, 10:00–11:30 PT
- Wed, Oct 27, 13:00–14:30 PT

Please tell me which slot is better for you, or share two alternatives and I will coordinate.

Attached: agenda_draft.pdf (rough) — the lab will be hands-on but we can keep the infra demo constrained to a sandbox.

Prep asks for your side: a short list of the target model sizes / expected QPS and whether you prefer a live VPC connectivity check during the session or a follow-up checklist. If you have a network/SEC contact we should include, please copy them.

Zoom link will be created once time is locked.

Thanks — looking forward to it.

Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: agenda_draft.pdf (PDF, 1 page)
From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@zenport.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Kapoor <priya.kapoor@zenport.ai>
Date: 2026-10-19T09:03:00+01:00
Subject: Re: Architecture lab — slot options + draft agenda

Hi Ava,

Thanks — both slots look close but Oct 26th at 10:00 PT clashes with a board prep meeting for our CTO. Wed the 27th at 13:00 PT should work for Priya and me, but we need the session to finish by 14:15 PT (we have back-to-back reviews).

Two small asks: can you include a short (10 min) segment on how Redwood handles KV cache eviction and long-running chat contexts? Also we have a security engineer who may join briefly to ask about audit logs and KMS integration — I'll loop them if possible.

If you can tentatively book the 27th and share an updated agenda and Zoom invite, I will confirm on our end.

Best,
Liam
Liam O'Neill
Head of Platform Engineering, Zenport Analytics
liam.oneill@zenport.ai

On Mon, Oct 18, 2026 at 4:12 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message)
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-19T14:25:00-07:00
Subject: FW: Architecture lab — slot options + draft agenda

Team — forwarding Zenport replies. The prospect prefers Wed 10/27 13:00–14:15 PT (shorter window). They want a 10-min KV-cache eviction explanation + a quick audit-logs/KMS QA. Can we tighten the run order to 75 minutes and still show a short live run with sample workload?

Proposed running order (75 min):
- 5m intros & objectives (Ava / Liam)
- 15m infra & topologies (Marcus)
- 10m KV cache / long-context handling (Marcus)
- 25m live inference run + token-cost breakdown (Soojin)
- 10m audit/logs/KMS + Q&A (Marcus / Soojin)
- 10m next steps / rollouts (Ava)

Marcus — are you ok with the order? Any blockers for doing the live run in ~25 mins if we use a pre-warmed small VM and a limited prompt set? Also please flag any infra prework you need from Zenport (e.g., temporary IAM role, sample dataset location).

Soojin — can you take the live run and have a short slide showing token-cost vs latency tradeoffs (3 metrics) and a fallback plan if we hit rate limits during the demo?

I'll tentatively confirm Wed 10/27 13:00 PT once I get your OK and will send calendar + Zoom + agenda_v2.

Thanks — Ava
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@zenport.ai>, Priya Kapoor <priya.kapoor@zenport.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-20T10:45:00-07:00
Subject: Confirmed: Zenport architecture lab — Wed Oct 27, 13:00–14:15 PT (Zoom invite)

Hi Liam / Priya,

Confirmed Wed Oct 27, 13:00–14:15 PT. Zoom: https://zoom.us/j/987654321

Attached: lab_agenda_v2.pdf (finalized, includes 10m KV cache segment) and infra_checklist.xlsx (what we need from your infra team ahead of time).

Quick prework asks (from infra_checklist.xlsx):
- Temporary IAM role with read-only access to the sample bucket (S3) by Oct 25
- Point of contact for a VPC peering check (name + best contact time)

Agenda highlights: Marcus will cover topology and KV eviction; Soojin will run the live inference with cost/latency commentary. We'll keep the demo confined to a sample dataset — no production data required.

If the security engineer is joining briefly, no problem — we set aside 10 minutes at the end for audit/KMS questions.

See you then.

Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Ava.chen@redwood.ai

Attachments: lab_agenda_v2.pdf (PDF, 2 pages), infra_checklist.xlsx (XLSX, 1 sheet)
From: Liam O'Neill <liam.oneill@zenport.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Kapoor <priya.kapoor@zenport.ai>
Date: 2026-10-21T08:00:00+01:00
Subject: Re: Confirmed: Zenport architecture lab — Wed Oct 27, 13:00–14:15 PT (Zoom invite)

Hi Ava — quick heads up: our COO got called into an unexpected investor review that overlaps with the latter half of the 13:00 PT slot. Can we nudge the session 45 minutes earlier (start 12:15 PT) or move to Thu Oct 28 at 11:00 PT? Either works for us, but earlier on Wed is preferable if your team can adjust.

If you need me to run a Doodle, I can — otherwise tell me what you can do and I will confirm.

Thanks,
Liam

source.subject: Gilmore Analytics: production spike after model v3.1-quant rollout
messages:
From: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Greg Wallace <greg.wallace@gilmore.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Aditya —

We pushed your v3.1-quant model yesterday evening (kanban release window) and starting overnight we’re seeing a major change in production: token p95 latency is ~5x baseline, and our request error rate climbed from ~0.5% to ~12%. These errors are decode-time timeouts and some "model not available" during peak bursts. This is affecting our customer-facing summarization flows; we’ve paused non-critical jobs, but the real-time API used by our mobile app is impacted and we risk churn.

Attached: a short sample of error logs and a capture of a failed request (gilmore-error-sample-20261018.log). We rotated keys and retried to rule out auth. The only change on our side was flipping to the new model tag per your docs.

We need an urgent triage and a rollback option. We’re also going to trigger procurement/finance if SLA credits are on the table — this is material.

Can we get a timeboxed remediation plan and a firm ETA for either a patch or rollback? We can make time for a call now if helpful.

— Elena
Customer Success Lead
Gilmore Analytics

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Elena — thanks for the heads up and for sending the logs. I’m very sorry for the disruption. We’ve flagged this as a Sev-1 and Marcus from inference ops is taking lead on triage.

Immediate plan:
- Marcus will start an investigation focused on the quantization profile we shipped with v3.1 and the runtime kernel selection that runs on your Dedicated pool.
- If we confirm a regression, we will propose either a rollback to v3.0 (same variant you used last month) or a hotfix qprofile within 4–8 hours.
- I’ll coordinate a short call with your team and our eng/CS leadership in the next 60 minutes.

Marissa, Lauren — looping you in for commercial context and to start the credit/contract thread if needed.

Elena — attaching the logs to our ticket and will update this thread when we have a root-cause hypothesis.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

[attachment: gilmore-error-sample-20261018.log]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Quick triage notes (initial):
- Reproduced locally against a subset of the sample requests. The v3.1-quant qprofile enables an aggressive KV-cache compaction heuristic intended to reduce memory, but it seems to increase cache churn under your real-time burst pattern. That churn causes additional KV re-fetches and kernel re-init, pushing decode p95 up and occasionally tripping our request timeout fallback (hence the 5–15% observed timeouts).
- Error messages in logs point to "kv-cache-miss -> deferred fetch" sequences followed by longer kernel scheduling delays on your GPU pool (Dedicated config: g5x-large).

Proposed immediate actions: 
1) Hot rollback to model:v3.0 (non-quantized or the v3.0-qsafe profile) for your production route. This is the least risky; we can do a targeted swap on your route in ~25 minutes with no config change required.
2) Parallel: prepare a conservative qprofile for v3.1 (v3.1-qconservative) that disables the compaction heuristic and re-test in canary. Target 48-hour turnaround for validated qprofile.

title: Gilmore Analytics: production spike after model v3.1-quant rollout
preview:
From: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Greg Wallace <greg.wallace@gilmore.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Aditya —

We pushed your v3.1-quant model yesterday evening (kanban release window) and starting overnight we’re seeing a major change in production: token p95 latency is ~5x baseline, and our request error rate climbed from ~0.5% to ~12%. These errors are decode-time timeouts and some "model not available" during peak bursts. This is affecting our customer-facing summarization flows; we’ve paused non-critical jobs, but the real-time API used by our mobile app is impacted and we risk churn.

Attached: a short sample of error logs and a capture of a failed request (gilmore-error-sample-20261018.log). We rotated keys and retried to rule out auth. The only change on our side was flipping to the new model tag per your docs.

We need an urgent triage and a rollback option. We’re also going to trigger procurement/finance if SLA credits are on the table — this is material.

Can we get a timeboxed remediation plan and a firm ETA for either a patch or rollback? We can make time for a call now if helpful.

— Elena
Customer Success Lead
Gilmore Analytics


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 09:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Elena — thanks for the heads up and for sending the logs. I’m very sorry for the disruption. We’ve flagged this as a Sev-1 and Marcus from inference ops is taking lead on triage.

Immediate plan:
- Marcus will start an investigation focused on the quantization profile we shipped with v3.1 and the runtime kernel selection that runs on your Dedicated pool.
- If we confirm a regression, we will propose either a rollback to v3.0 (same variant you used last month) or a hotfix qprofile within 4–8 hours.
- I’ll coordinate a short call with your team and our eng/CS leadership in the next 60 minutes.

Marissa, Lauren — looping you in for commercial context and to start the credit/contract thread if needed.

Elena — attaching the logs to our ticket and will update this thread when we have a root-cause hypothesis.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

[attachment: gilmore-error-sample-20261018.log]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Gilmore <elena@gilmore.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Oct 2026 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sudden latency + error spike after v3.1-quant rollout

Quick triage notes (initial):
- Reproduced locally against a subset of the sample requests. The v3.1-quant qprofile enables an aggressive KV-cache compaction heuristic intended to reduce memory, but it seems to increase cache churn under your real-time burst pattern. That churn causes additional KV re-fetches and kernel re-init, pushing decode p95 up and occasionally tripping our request timeout fallback (hence the 5–15% observed timeouts).
- Error messages in logs point to "kv-cache-miss -> deferred fetch" sequences followed by longer kernel scheduling delays on your GPU pool (Dedicated config: g5x-large).

Proposed immediate actions: 
1) Hot rollback to model:v3.0 (non-quantized or the v3.0-qsafe profile) for your production route. This is the least risky; we can do a targeted swap on your route in ~25 minutes with no config change required.
2) Parallel: prepare a conservative qprofile for v3.1 (v3.1-qconservative) that disables the compaction heuristic and re-test in canary. Target 48-hour turnaround for validated qprofile.
3) If you prefer, we can enable adaptive per-customer kv thresholds (config flip) while we iterate — adds ~2 hours to cutover but avoids full model swap if you want to remain on v3.1.

I recommend option 1 for fastest user impact remediation. I’ll start the rollback immediately if I get an approval from Aditya/Elena.

— Marcus
Senior Inference Engineer
Redwood Inference

[attachment: profile-v3.1-qprofile.json]

messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Bianca Soto <bianca@soireeventco.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Ambassador table — host brief, guest clearance & security checklist

Hi Bianca —

Quick run-through from our side as we lock the Ambassador Table for the Helios Capital dinner on Nov 18:

- Target headcount for that table: 12 total (8 client guests + 4 Redwood hosts).
- We need the standard AV package (podium mic + wireless lav for two moderators, discreet tabletop mics for VIPs).
- Important: two guests are from a regulated fund; they require an NDA listing the host table only (no taping). Can you confirm you can accept our standard NDA?
- Dietary constraints: 3 pescatarian, 1 severe nut allergy — we will send final names by 10/20.

Attaching: guest_list_seed.xlsx (seed list; will iterate).

Can you send updated contract with exact AV line-item and a line on guest confidentiality (no photos/recording) so we can route to legal? Also confirm the on-site contact and arrival window for our AV drop-in.

Thanks —

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0127

[Attachment: guest_list_seed.xlsx]

From: Bianca Soto <bianca@soireeventco.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2026 11:03:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Ambassador table — host brief, guest clearance & security checklist

Hi Ben —

Thanks — this is clear and we can accommodate. Please see the attached draft (venue_contract_draft_v3.pdf) which:

- Adds an "Event AV & Tech" appendix with the options you requested (we priced podium mic + 2 lavs + two tabletop mics as a single line item).
- Includes a clause we use for privacy-sensitive dinners: Section 7.4 — "No Recording / Photography by external parties without prior written consent." We can make the clause Redwood-forwarded friendly if you send your NDA.
- Max capacity for the private dining room with seated dinner and staggered cocktail area is 48, so your table of 12 fits comfortably.

We can sign our standard bilateral NDA or accept your exec-table NDA. Attaching both: venue_contract_draft_v3.pdf and venue_rider_2026-10.pdf.

On-site contact: Olivia Park (olivia.park@soireeventco.com), cell +1 (415) 722-3344. Arrival for AV load-in: 4:30 PM day-of; event starts 7:00 PM.

Let me know if you want us to reserve the adjacent green room for hosts.

Best,

Bianca Soto
Director, Client Events | Soiré Event Co.
bianca@soireeventco.com | +1 (415) 555-9944

[Attachments: venue_contract_draft_v3.pdf (14 pages), venue_rider_2026-10.pdf]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2026 13:20:45 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Ambassador table — host brief, guest clearance & security checklist

Begin forwarded message:

From: Bianca Soto <bianca@soireeventco.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2026 11:03:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Ambassador table — host brief, guest clearance & security checklist
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>

(see attachments)

Team —

Quick notes after Bianca's reply:

- Contract is mostly standard; AV appendix is explicit now. Please flag any commercial terms that need negotiation.
- Bianca can accept our NDA or use hers — legal prefers we push our NDA for the regulated-fund guests (keeps the limitation of liability and no-recording language aligned).
- Action items: Karthik, can you confirm procurement path for the $3.2k AV line (we are fine paying from event budget but need PO process)? Marcus, can you loop in security or Ops if the list includes partners with extra screening?

Attaching: venue_contract_draft_v3.pdf, nda-executive-table-template.docx

Ben

[Attachments: venue_contract_draft_v3.pdf, nda-executive-table-template.docx]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 14 Oct 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Ambassador table — guest curation and NDAs

Thanks Ben — attached is a revised seed list (guest_list_seed_revised.xlsx) with priority tiers and notes on dietary and compliance flags.

Quick summary:
- Tier 1 (confirm asap): Oliver Grant (Helios Capital — partner), Priya Suresh (Helios — regulated fund), Aaron Cole (Helios — compliance flagged).
- We recommend we ask Tier 1 to sign the exec-table NDA (attached). For Tier 2+ we will send standard invite language.
- RSVP cadence: initial invite today (save-the-date language), formal invite with logistic details on 10/20, final reminder 11/08.
- I'll own the RSVP tracking; hubspot event record created here: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/company/Helios-Capital/event/EVT-3342

Please review Tier 1 list and confirm NDA routing. If legal wants different language we need that before we send to Priya & Aaron.

Laura Bennett
Senior Events Manager | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai | +1 (617) 555-0199

[Attachment: guest_list_seed_revised.xlsx]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Bianca Soto <bianca@soireeventco.com>, Oliver Grant <oliver.grant@helioscap.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Oct 2026 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Final: Ambassador table — contract signature & NDA for Tier 1 guests

Hi Bianca / Oliver —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. Per internal review we are ready to proceed:

- Attached is the signed exec-table NDA (nda-executive-table-signed.pdf) covering the three Tier 1 guests (Oliver Grant, Priya Suresh, Aaron Cole).
- Please accept the NDA and countersign the venue contract (we will return a countersigned copy once we receive the venue signature).
- Final VIPs for the Ambassador table (12 seats): Oliver Grant; Priya Suresh; Aaron Cole; Maya Srinivasan; Rafael Mendes; plus 6 Redwood hosts (names on the attached revised guest list).
- Confirm final AV line item and invoice to: finance@redwood.ai (Attn: Karthik Iyer). We expect PO processing within 5 business days — please hold the reservation for the 18th.
- We will need access to the green room for hosts from 5:00–7:30 PM.

Looking forward to a smooth night — Olivia (on-site) please confirm load-in window and final contact phone.

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0127

source.subject: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep
messages:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-10-11 09:04 PDT
Subject: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Team — kicking this off. High level and asks below; aim is to lock targets and a baseline scenario by the end of week 3 (Oct 29). Attaching the draft timeline and the scenario assumptions doc.

Why now
- We need a single set of anchors for FY27 targets before the product roadmap decouples from capacity assumptions.
- QBR deck needs to be draft-ready for exec review the week of Nov 8.

Primary asks (owners in parens)
1) Propose 3-target bands (Aggressive / Base / Conserv.) for ARR, Net Retention, and Hosting unit cost by Oct 22 (Marcus).
2) Define capacity and hiring assumptions that materially change cost-per-token (Laura + Jonas) — call out any procurement lead times.
3) Draft A/B scenario narrative for a 20% demand shock and a 35% latency-sensitive adoption (Grace).
4) Book a 1.5h cross-functional planning workshop (calendar invite will follow) and a separate 30m QBR dry-run for leadership (Ava).

Attachments: [Attachment: 2026_planning_timeline.xlsx (spreadsheet)], [Attachment: scenario_assumptions_v1.md (text)]

Quick timeline
- Oct 22: targets & assumptions due
- Oct 29: consolidated scenario and risk register
- Nov 2: pre-read to execs
- Nov 8: leadership QBR review

Notes and constraints to consider
- We should bake in a conservative buffer for Dedicated capacity lead times (6–8 weeks) and a second-tier fallback model (smaller quantized variant) for latency-critical customers.
- Call out any SLAs that would require SLA credits or changes to pricing.

I know this is busy — if you prefer to split deliverables and own fewer items, flag it in your reply.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Head of Product, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
[Attachment: 2026_planning_timeline.xlsx]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-11 10:18 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Thanks — good framing. A few clarifying Qs and an initial ask list below.

Clarifying Qs
- For "unit cost" do you want per-1k-token blended cost at 95th percentile latency or average cost? I'd recommend 95th.
- Are we assuming current quantization profiles or applying the Optimize recommendations?

Immediate deliverables (Marcus)
- I can roughly sketch target bands by Oct 18 if we assume current profiles, or Oct 22 if we incorporate Optimize changes (I prefer Oct 22 to keep it accurate).
- Preliminary numbers: Aggressive = +55% ARR growth target, Base = +35%, Conserv. = +15% (ballpark — will refine).

Blocking items
- Need a clear headcount plan from infra/procurement for dedicated pools — Laura, can you confirm hiring + procurement lead times this week?

I'll start filling the number sheet and flag assumptions inline.

—Marcus
 Marcus Lin
 Head of Revenue Ops
[Sent from my laptop]

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-11 15:02 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Ava / Marcus — quick status from capacity/procurement side.

- Hiring: realistic ramp for infra engineers + ops is +8 FTEs spread across Q1. Recruit + offer cadence suggests start-of-hire in mid-Nov; impact on capacity by late-Q1.
- Dedicated GPU lead time: 6–9 weeks for procurement -> install in cloud partner (conservative is 9 weeks if special hardware).
- Cost note: GPU price decline assumptions in scenario_assumptions_v1.md are optimistic; recommend a Base that assumes a modest 10% unit price drop vs. the optimistic 25%.

Attached a short note with procurement milestones and a quick headcount impact table.

[Attachment: procurement_headcount_note.pdf (doc stub)]

Laura Bennett
VP, Platform Operations
laura@redwood.com

On 2026-10-11 10:18, Marcus Lin wrote:
> Thanks — good framing. A few clarifying Qs and an initial ask list below.
> 
> Clarifying Qs
> - For "unit cost" do you want per-1k-token blended cost at 95th percentile latency or average cost? I'd recommend 95th.
> - Are we assuming current quantization profiles or applying the Optimize recommendations?
> 
> Immediate deliverables (Marcus)
> - I can roughly sketch target bands by Oct 18 if we assume current profiles, or Oct 22 if we incorporate Optimize changes (I prefer Oct 22 to keep it accurate).
> 
> —Marcus

From: Ken Park <ken@fathomventures.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-12 08:46 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Ava — quick external perspective from a partner lens. We run a similar playbook and have a simple rule of thumb on buffers: keep a 12-week cost and capacity buffer when you sell Dedicated for enterprise deals to avoid SLA exposure. That aligns with your 6–9 week procurement but gives commercial teams breathing room.

If helpful, I can share our buffer calc (anonymized) — happy to jump on a 20m call with the ops team.

Ken Park
Chief Product Officer, Fathom Ventures
ken@fathomventures.com

On Mon, Oct 11, 2026 at 09:04, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — kicking this off. High level and asks below; aim is to lock targets and a baseline scenario by the end of week 3 (Oct 29). Attaching the draft timeline and the scenario assumptions doc.
> 
>...

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: Ken Park <ken@fathomventures.com>
Date: 2026-10-13 15:22 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Thanks all — summary and commitments below so we have a single source of truth.

Decisions / owners (finalized)
- Targets draft (Marcus) — commit to Oct 22 with Optimize incorporated. Marcus: aggressive/base/conservative bands + a 1-pager rationale.
- Capacity & procurement assumptions (Laura + Jonas) — procurement note + headcount table by Oct 20.
- Scenario narratives (Grace) — A and B scenarios (20% demand shock / 35% latency adoption) due Oct 22.
- Buffer policy: adopt a 12-week commercial buffer for Dedicated as a temporary policy for FY27 (thanks Ken for the input). Ops to document exceptions.
- QBR dry-run scheduled: Nov 4 @ 10:00 PT (calendar invite sent).

Next steps
- Marcus to drop updated numbers in the sheet and mark cells that are high variance.
- Laura to attach procurement milestone timeline in the drive link and call out any vendor-specific latencies.
- Grace to sketch scenario one-pagers and circulate for a quick 30m review on Oct 23.

Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1X3-example-planning-folder
HubSpot reference: HS-98765

If anything materially changes, flag it in-thread — otherwise we treat these as commitments and will escalate tradeoffs in the Nov 4 dry-run.

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Head of Product, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
[Attachment: consolidated_action_tracker.csv (attachment stub)]

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-10-11 09:04 PDT
Subject: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Team — kicking this off. High level and asks below; aim is to lock targets and a baseline scenario by the end of week 3 (Oct 29). Attaching the draft timeline and the scenario assumptions doc.

Why now
- We need a single set of anchors for FY27 targets before the product roadmap decouples from capacity assumptions.
- QBR deck needs to be draft-ready for exec review the week of Nov 8.

Primary asks (owners in parens)
1) Propose 3-target bands (Aggressive / Base / Conserv.) for ARR, Net Retention, and Hosting unit cost by Oct 22 (Marcus).
2) Define capacity and hiring assumptions that materially change cost-per-token (Laura + Jonas) — call out any procurement lead times.
3) Draft A/B scenario narrative for a 20% demand shock and a 35% latency-sensitive adoption (Grace).
4) Book a 1.5h cross-functional planning workshop (calendar invite will follow) and a separate 30m QBR dry-run for leadership (Ava).

Attachments: [Attachment: 2026_planning_timeline.xlsx (spreadsheet)], [Attachment: scenario_assumptions_v1.md (text)]

Quick timeline
- Oct 22: targets & assumptions due
- Oct 29: consolidated scenario and risk register
- Nov 2: pre-read to execs
- Nov 8: leadership QBR review

Notes and constraints to consider
- We should bake in a conservative buffer for Dedicated capacity lead times (6–8 weeks) and a second-tier fallback model (smaller quantized variant) for latency-critical customers.
- Call out any SLAs that would require SLA credits or changes to pricing.

I know this is busy — if you prefer to split deliverables and own fewer items, flag it in your reply.

Thanks,
Ava
Ava Chen
Head of Product, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
[Attachment: 2026_planning_timeline.xlsx]


From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-11 10:18 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Thanks — good framing. A few clarifying Qs and an initial ask list below.

Clarifying Qs
- For \"unit cost\" do you want per-1k-token blended cost at 95th percentile latency or average cost? I'd recommend 95th.
- Are we assuming current quantization profiles or applying the Optimize recommendations?

Immediate deliverables (Marcus)
- I can roughly sketch target bands by Oct 18 if we assume current profiles, or Oct 22 if we incorporate Optimize changes (I prefer Oct 22 to keep it accurate).
- Preliminary numbers: Aggressive = +55% ARR growth target, Base = +35%, Conserv. = +15% (ballpark — will refine).

Blocking items
- Need a clear headcount plan from infra/procurement for dedicated pools — Laura, can you confirm hiring + procurement lead times this week?

I'll start filling the number sheet and flag assumptions inline.

—Marcus
 Marcus Lin
 Head of Revenue Ops
[Sent from my laptop]

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-11 15:02 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Ava / Marcus — quick status from capacity/procurement side.

- Hiring: realistic ramp for infra engineers + ops is +8 FTEs spread across Q1. Recruit + offer cadence suggests start-of-hire in mid-Nov; impact on capacity by late-Q1.
- Dedicated GPU lead time: 6–9 weeks for procurement -> install in cloud partner (conservative is 9 weeks if special hardware).
- Cost note: GPU price decline assumptions in scenario_assumptions_v1.md are optimistic; recommend a Base that assumes a modest 10% unit price drop vs. the optimistic 25%.

Attached a short note with procurement milestones and a quick headcount impact table.

[Attachment: procurement_headcount_note.pdf (doc stub)]

Laura Bennett
VP, Platform Operations
laura@redwood.com

On 2026-10-11 10:18, Marcus Lin wrote:
> Thanks — good framing. A few clarifying Qs and an initial ask list below.
> 
> Clarifying Qs
> - For \"unit cost\" do you want per-1k-token blended cost at 95th percentile latency or average cost? I'd recommend 95th.
> - Are we assuming current quantization profiles or applying the Optimize recommendations?
> 
> Immediate deliverables (Marcus)
> - I can roughly sketch target bands by Oct 18 if we assume current profiles, or Oct 22 if we incorporate Optimize changes (I prefer Oct 22 to keep it accurate).
> 
> —Marcus


From: Ken Park <ken@fathomventures.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-12 08:46 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Ava — quick external perspective from a partner lens. We run a similar playbook and have a simple rule of thumb on buffers: keep a 12-week cost and capacity buffer when you sell Dedicated for enterprise deals to avoid SLA exposure. That aligns with your 6–9 week procurement but gives commercial teams breathing room.

If helpful, I can share our buffer calc (anonymized) — happy to jump on a 20m call with the ops team.

Ken Park
Chief Product Officer, Fathom Ventures
ken@fathomventures.com

On Mon, Oct 11, 2026 at 09:04, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — kicking this off. High level and asks below; aim is to lock targets and a baseline scenario by the end of week 3 (Oct 29). Attaching the draft timeline and the scenario assumptions doc.
> 
>...


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: Ken Park <ken@fathomventures.com>
Date: 2026-10-13 15:22 PDT
Subject: Re: Annual planning kickoff: anchors, buffer policy, and QBR prep

Thanks all — summary and commitments below so we have a single source of truth.

Decisions / owners (finalized)
- Targets draft (Marcus) — commit to Oct 22 with Optimize incorporated. Marcus: aggressive/base/conservative bands + a 1-pager rationale.
- Capacity & procurement assumptions (Laura + Jonas) — procurement note + headcount table by Oct 20.
- Scenario narratives (Grace) — A and B scenarios (20% demand shock / 35% latency adoption) due Oct 22.
- Buffer policy: adopt a 12-week commercial buffer for Dedicated as a temporary policy for FY27 (thanks Ken for the input). Ops to document exceptions.
- QBR dry-run scheduled: Nov 4 @ 10:00 PT (calendar invite sent).

Next steps
- Marcus to drop updated numbers in the sheet and mark cells that are high variance.
- Laura to attach procurement milestone timeline in the drive link and call out any vendor-specific latencies.
- Grace to sketch scenario one-pagers and circulate for a quick 30m review on Oct 23.

Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1X3-example-planning-folder
HubSpot reference: HS-98765

If anything materially changes, flag it in-thread — otherwise we treat these as commitments and will escalate tradeoffs in the Nov 4 dry-run.

Best,
Ava
Ava Chen
Head of Product, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com
[Attachment: consolidated_action_tracker.csv (attachment stub)]

source.subject: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>, Tom Baker <tom.baker@novaforge.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 01 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Hi Elena / Tom,

Quick note as we approach your upcoming renewal window (Nov 1): thanks for the usage update last quarter — looks like Novaforge is trending ~45% above your current monthly commit during peak hours. To avoid throttle-induced latency and to keep unit economics predictable, I want to propose a short-term commit uplift for Q4 with a seasonal burst pocket for Black Friday/Cyber Monday traffic.

High level proposal (draft):
- Monthly committed tokens: increase from 90M -> 140M (hosted API)
- Tier move: Standard -> Premium (reduced per-token price at scale)
- Burst pocket: 25M tokens/month (one-time monthly allowance for peaks, bumped to consume before overage)
- Soft throttle buffer: allow 10% headroom for sudden spikes; billing applies only after burst pocket is consumed

Attached: draft mutual action plan and a short SOW amendment (stubs attached). If this looks reasonable I can circulate a redline-ready SOW and a DocuSign link.

Can we sync Tuesday for a 30-min ops + finance call? Kimberly will join for invoicing cadence.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
(415) 555-0187

Attachments: Novaforge_MAPlan_v2.pdf (PDF, 2 pages), Amendment_SOW_Novaforge.docx (DOCX, placeholder)
From: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Baker <tom.baker@novaforge.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Ben — thanks for the heads up and for drafting this. A few quick questions before we commit:

1) Pricing sensitivity: can you share the per-token price at the 90M, 140M, and 200M commit bands? We want to model cost vs expected traffic and see where the breakpoint is.
2) Burst behavior: does the burst pocket bypass rate limits or is it consumed within the same rate window? If our peak is very short (seconds-long spikes) we need to ensure the rate cap won’t throttle important user flows.
3) Term: would you be open to a 9-month ramp (Nov–Jul) instead of annual if the numbers are preferable?

Also looping Tom — he heads infra and will want a quick read on rate limit guarantees.

—Elena
Elena Morales
VP Product, Novaforge
elena@novaforge.ai

On Thu, Oct 1, 2026 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Quick note as we approach your upcoming renewal window (Nov 1): thanks for the usage update...
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>, Tom Baker <tom.baker@novaforge.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 03 Oct 2026 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Elena — great questions. I've pulled in the numbers and a short pricing matrix below. Marcus added a technical note in the next message about rate caps and burst semantics; please see that for the infra detail.

Pricing (hosted API) — illustrative net pricing after committed discount:
- 90M (current): $0.00028 / token
- 140M (proposal): $0.000225 / token
- 200M (stretch): $0.000195 / token

Burst pocket behavior:
- Burst pocket is a monthly token allowance billed at the committed rate but consumed prior to overage. It does not change per-second rate limits (QPS) on a single connection. For short spikes we recommend a combination of a small dedicated capacity reservation (if latency is critical) + burst pocket usage to avoid billing surprises.

Term flexibility: we can do a 9-month ramp with pro-rated commit months, but pricing is more favorable on the 12-month annualized commit. I can show the blended effective rate for both options on the call.

Next steps I propose:
1) Technical confirm with Marcus on rate characteristics (done below).
2) Joint call 30m Mon — ops + finance — Kimberly to own invoicing cadence; Tom / Marcus for infra.
3) I’ll circulate a redline-friendly SOW and DocuSign link once we align on tier and term.

If that works I’ll lock a provisional notch in our capacity plan for Nov.

Ben

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baker <tom.baker@novaforge.ai>, Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 04 Oct 2026 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Tom / Elena — quick technical note from infra/SE side so you have clarity before the call.

- Rate limits: hosted API enforces per-key QPS limits and a per-account aggregated concurrency window. Burst pockets affect token accounting, not instantaneous QPS caps. If your user flows need headroom across short spikes (sub-minute), the QPS limiter is the constraint.
- Recommendation: for Black Friday traffic spikes we typically recommend a small Dedicated reservation (single ranked pool) sized to cover peak QPS; that ensures per-request latency stays stable. The billing model would be: committed hosted tokens + small dedicated reservation (monthly fee) + burst pocket for overflow consumption.
- Optimizations: enable prefix KV-cache for repeated prompts in ad-serving flow and turn on auto-batching for small prompt sizes; both reduce per-token compute and latency. Quantization (4-bit) can also reduce cost if you onboard a compatible model variant.

Happy to scope dedicated sizing if you share peak QPS/avg token length.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

> On Sat, Oct 3, 2026 at 10:22 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Pricing (hosted API) — illustrative net pricing after committed discount:...

From: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 15:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Thanks — this looks good and addresses my team’s immediate concerns. We’re comfortable with the 140M monthly commit + 25M burst pocket and we’d like the 12-month term to lock the blended rate.

Two asks before signature:
1) Can you add a clause that gives Novaforge one cross-regional fallback request per day for DR testing without additional charge during the Q4 promotional window?
2) Please include a 30-day invoice payment term in the SOW (our procurement requires net-30).

I’ll route to procurement today; they’ll expect a DocuSign link and a PO once legal signs the amendment.

—Elena

On Sun, Oct 4, 2026 at 01:30 PM Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com> wrote:
> Tom / Elena — quick technical note from infra/SE side so you have clarity before the call.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 16:43:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Elena — fantastic, appreciate the clear asks. We can:
- Add the single cross-regional fallback request/day during Nov 1–Dec 31 to the amendment with zero additional charge (will mark as DR_test_window).
- Include Net-30 payment terms in the SOW.

I’ll update the SOW with those two clauses and circulate a redline + DocuSign. Once procurement returns a PO (or signs via DocuSign) we’ll finalize capacity provisioning (we’ll reserve the 140M notch and the 25M burst pocket in our plan).

Proposed immediate next steps:
- I’ll send the redline-ready Amendment_SOW_Novaforge.docx within 2 business hours.
- DocuSign link + HubSpot deal update (HS-53721) will follow.
- Kimberly can you confirm billing contact and PO reference format?

Thanks all — looking forward to getting Novaforge ready for Q4 spikes.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachments referenced: Amendment_SOW_Novaforge.docx (to follow, includes DR_test_window clause and Net-30)

- Burst pocket is a monthly token allowance billed at the committed rate but consumed prior to overage. It does not change per-second rate limits (QPS) on a single connection. For short spikes we recommend a combination of a small dedicated capacity reservation (if latency is critical) + burst pocket usage to avoid billing surprises.

Term flexibility: we can do a 9-month ramp with pro-rated commit months, but pricing is more favorable on the 12-month annualized commit. I can show the blended effective rate for both options on the call.

Next steps I propose:
1) Technical confirm with Marcus on rate characteristics (done below).
2) Joint call 30m Mon — ops + finance — Kimberly to own invoicing cadence; Tom / Marcus for infra.
3) I’ll circulate a redline-friendly SOW and DocuSign link once we align on tier and term.

If that works I’ll lock a provisional notch in our capacity plan for Nov.

Ben



From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baker <tom.baker@novaforge.ai>, Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 04 Oct 2026 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Tom / Elena — quick technical note from infra/SE side so you have clarity before the call.

- Rate limits: hosted API enforces per-key QPS limits and a per-account aggregated concurrency window. Burst pockets affect token accounting, not instantaneous QPS caps. If your user flows need headroom across short spikes (sub-minute), the QPS limiter is the constraint.
- Recommendation: for Black Friday traffic spikes we typically recommend a small Dedicated reservation (single ranked pool) sized to cover peak QPS; that ensures per-request latency stays stable. The billing model would be: committed hosted tokens + small dedicated reservation (monthly fee) + burst pocket for overflow consumption.
- Optimizations: enable prefix KV-cache for repeated prompts in ad-serving flow and turn on auto-batching for small prompt sizes; both reduce per-token compute and latency. Quantization (4-bit) can also reduce cost if you onboard a compatible model variant.

Happy to scope dedicated sizing if you share peak QPS/avg token length.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

> On Sat, Oct 3, 2026 at 10:22 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Pricing (hosted API) — illustrative net pricing after committed discount:...



From: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 15:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Thanks — this looks good and addresses my team’s immediate concerns. We’re comfortable with the 140M monthly commit + 25M burst pocket and we’d like the 12-month term to lock the blended rate.

Two asks before signature:
1) Can you add a clause that gives Novaforge one cross-regional fallback request per day for DR testing without additional charge during the Q4 promotional window?
2) Please include a 30-day invoice payment term in the SOW (our procurement requires net-30).

I’ll route to procurement today; they’ll expect a DocuSign link and a PO once legal signs the amendment.

—Elena

On Sun, Oct 4, 2026 at 01:30 PM Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com> wrote:
> Tom / Elena — quick technical note from infra/SE side so you have clarity before the call.



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@novaforge.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 16:43:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Q4 renewal + seasonal uptick — Hosted API capacity & tier rescale

Elena — fantastic, appreciate the clear asks. We can:
- Add the single cross-regional fallback request/day during Nov 1–Dec 31 to the amendment with zero additional charge (will mark as DR_test_window).
- Include Net-30 payment terms in the SOW.

I’ll update the SOW with those two clauses and circulate a redline + DocuSign. Once procurement returns a PO (or signs via DocuSign) we’ll finalize capacity provisioning (we’ll reserve the 140M notch and the 25M burst pocket in our plan).

Proposed immediate next steps:
- I’ll send the redline-ready Amendment_SOW_Novaforge.docx within 2 business hours.
- DocuSign link + HubSpot deal update (HS-53721) will follow.
- Kimberly can you confirm billing contact and PO reference format?

Thanks all — looking forward to getting Novaforge ready for Q4 spikes.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Amy Park <amy@greenleafconsulting.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Team —

Following the engagement survey and the early signals from our managers, I’ve pulled together an initial at-risk roster and a proposed manager care cadence ahead of the Q4 review cycle. This is intentionally conservative — the goal is to get ahead of attrition where we can, and to ensure managers have a clear, confidential playbook for sensitive conversations.

Included:
- at_risk_roster_q32026.xlsx (roster with risk tiering, manager notes column)
- manager_playbook_q42026.pdf (draft manager script + escalation checklist)

Asks:
1) Managers (Laura, Evelyn, Marcus) — review the roster for your org and flag any inaccuracies or privacy concerns by EOD Monday (10/4). Mark rows as RED/YELLOW/GREEN.
2) Vivek — can you confirm thresholds where we should escalate to Compensation for retention credits?
3) Amy (Greenleaf) — please review the manager playbook and sensitive-case checklist; want confirmation that our approach is compliant with best practice for sensitive terminations/offboarding.

If anyone sees a likely immediate departure (verbal intent to leave, manager concerned), escalate to me directly and mark the row RED — we’ll treat those as urgent.

I’ve also added a shared Drive folder (link below) for secure documents. Use the secure transfer checklist — do not email attachments with PII.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
VP, People Operations
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: at_risk_roster_q32026.xlsx (XLSX, 24KB)]
[Attachment: manager_playbook_q42026.pdf (PDF, 520KB)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Aditya — thanks for pulling this together. Quick clarifying Qs:
- For RED-tier: are we expected to open retention conversations proactively (i.e., offer counter / early retention credits) or only after the manager confirms an employee has indicated intent to leave?
- Any guidance on timeline if we’re contemplating org changes that may trigger voluntary exits?

I’m worried about our busiest IC team (Platform) — I count 3 REDs and 2 YELLOWs there. If we escalate early, we should align on messaging and approval matrix so managers aren’t negotiating credits ad hoc.

I’ll review the roster today and annotate.

—Laura
Director, Engineering Managers
Redwood Inference

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Amy Park <amy@greenleafconsulting.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Oct 2026 13:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Thanks, Aditya. Short answers:
- RED-tier = immediate manager outreach + compensation pre-approval for small retention credits up to $Xk (I’ll share the thresholds). If manager reports "verbal intent," treat as urgent and loop Compensation.
- YELLOW = manager cadence + targeted 1:1s over two weeks; escalate if signals persist.

I’m attaching the retention_thresholds.xlsx — this is the current guideline for approvals without exec signoff. Anything above the threshold needs a quick note to me + People Ops (Aditya) and finance.

Also: if there are planned org changes that could materially affect teams, we should block calendar time for team-level comms at least 48 hours before any public announcement where possible — that reduces rumor risk.

Vivek
Head of Product & Operations
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: retention_thresholds.xlsx (XLSX, 12KB)]

From: Amy Park <amy@greenleafconsulting.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Wells <sara.wells@hrlegal.co>
Date: Mon, 4 Oct 2026 08:07:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Thanks for including us. I reviewed the manager_playbook_q42026.pdf and have a few recommendations for handling sensitive cases:

1) Establish a two-person review for RED-tier offboarding (manager + People Ops) before any written offer/closure to ensure consistency.
2) Use a secure, audited channel for any PII — we recommend the Drive folder you linked but with access logged and limited to need-to-know.
3) Provide managers with a short script for voluntary exits vs. performance-related departures — helps reduce legal exposure.

I’ve attached a one-page Sensitive Case Checklist (sensitive_case_checklist.docx) with the operational steps and suggested evidence to collect for each case. Happy to run a 30-min drop-in for managers next week to walk through scenarios.

—Amy Park
Principal, Greenleaf Consulting
(attachment: sensitive_case_checklist.docx)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Amy Park <amy@greenleafconsulting.com>, Sara Wells <sara.wells@hrlegal.co>
Date: Mon, 4 Oct 2026 16:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Appreciate the fast reviews. Action items and owners:
- Laura / Marcus: finish roster verification and RED/YELLOW/GREEN marking by EOD Tuesday (10/5).
- Evelyn: run the three Platform REDs as priority — schedule 1:1s this week; if any verbal intent to leave, mark RED and escalate to me immediately.
- Vivek: confirm the approval threshold that can be applied without exec signoff (upload to the Drive folder).
- Amy / Sara: finalize the sensitive-case checklist and join the manager drop-in on Wed 10/6 @ 11:00 PT (I’ll send calendar invite).

I’ll consolidate final list on Wednesday morning and publish a short manager playbook update (one page) with the exact approval matrix and communication templates. If managers prefer, I can pre-author outreach language for them to use.

Note on secure transfers: use the Drive link and mark docs with the redwood-classification label; do not forward attachments externally without redaction.

I’ll set up a 30-min leader sync (Aditya, Vivek, Laura, Evelyn, Marcus) for Wed 10/6 at 10:00 PT to finalize thresholds.

Thanks — let’s keep this tight.
Aditya Rao
VP, People Operations
Redwood Inference

[Related link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Aq-ManagerCare-Q4]

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 5 Oct 2026 16:44:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q4 calibration: at-risk roster & manager care cadence

Acknowledged — I’ve scheduled the three Platform 1:1s for Thursday and Friday. Heads-up: one of the REDs indicated they’re exploring other options and asked for a conversation about scope and growth; I’ll handle that track and confirm whether retention discussion is appropriate after the 1:1. I’ll update the roster immediately after each conversation and flag any immediate risks.

Evelyn
Senior Engineering Manager
Redwood Inference

Heads-up: one of the REDs indicated they’re exploring other options and asked for a conversation about scope and growth; I’ll handle that track and confirm whether retention discussion is appropriate after the 1:1. I’ll update the roster immediately after each conversation and flag any immediate risks.

Evelyn
Senior Engineering Manager
Redwood Inference

source.subject: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates
messages:
From: Tom Hayes <tom.hayes@acmehealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates

Hi Ava / Grace,

We're putting together a short enablement pack for our field engineering team so they can triage and escalate cost & latency incidents against Redwood endpoints. Can you help with a lightweight \"quickstart\" that includes:
- Per-route alert templates (examples with suggested thresholds)
- A compact dashboard pack (1-page per-route view) showing cost breakdown, tail latency percentiles, and recent regressions
- A short runbook for on-call and a one-page escalation/approval flow for changes to thresholds

Ideal target: a 1-hour handoff + docs and a set of templates we can reuse across customers. We also attached our intake form (acme-request-form.pdf) with expected traffic profiles.

Two quick questions that will inform templates:
1) Do you have canonical threshold guidance for 95th/99th latency buckets vs baseline?
2) Is there an opinionated cost-spike rule (e.g., % week-over-week) you recommend?

Thanks — appreciate any off-the-shelf material we can adapt.

Tom Hayes
Principal SRE, Acme Health
attachments: acme-request-form.pdf

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Tom Hayes <tom.hayes@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates

Hi Tom — thanks for the context and the intake form. This is exactly the kind of lightweight kit we can assemble. Proposed scope for the quickstart (deliverables and owners):

1) Quickstart doc (rdw-observability-quickstart-outline.md) — overview, one-page runbook, handoff checklist (owner: me)
2) Alert templates (sample-alert-templates.json) — per-route templates for latency, error rate, and spend spikes (owner: Kimberly)
3) Dashboard pack — a 1-page per-route view with: 95th/99th latency, cost per 1k tokens, token-pathway breakdown, and recent regressions (owner: Marcus)
4) Approval & rollback flow — short decision tree and required approvers for changing thresholds (owner: Product / Legal as needed)

A couple of asks from our side so we don't overfit the templates:
- Can you confirm the 90/95/99 traffic percentiles and a representative peak qps from your intake?
- Any routes you consider "high-cost" or special (e.g., long-context retrieval, multimodal) so we can add callouts?

I'll attach the outline and a starter JSON for alert templates to iterate. Suggest a 30-min kickoff next week to align on acceptance criteria.

Best,
Ava Chen
Product Manager, Observability — Redwood Inference
attachments: rdw-observability-quickstart-outline.md

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates

+1 on Ava's scope. A few practical suggestions from the ops side:

- Latency thresholds: we typically start with baseline-based thresholds (baseline = 7d median). Suggested starters: 95th > baseline * 2 OR absolute > 1200ms --> trigger page; 99th > baseline * 3 OR > 2000ms -> page + start incident runbook.
- Cost spike: 30% WoW increase for a route or 2x typical per-1k-token cost in 1h window -> paging alert. Keep a lower-severity (email) alert at 15% WoW for ops review.
- Canary set: include 5 representative routes (low, mid, high cost; short and long context) so dashboards show sensible distributions.

I'll drop a folder in Drive with example templates: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1ObsTemplatesExample — contains a dashboard JSON and alert snippets we use internally.

Kimberly Park
SRE Lead, Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-03T14:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Observability quickstart for field engineering: cost/latency templates

Thanks — adding a couple of things we should include in acceptance criteria for the field kit:

- Dashboard tile: per-route cost breakdown (token compute vs model size vs I/O) + 95/99 latency percentiles for last 1h/24h/7d. Tile should link to trace/excerpt playback for the top 3 call chains in the time window.
- Sampling guidance: default 1% full-trace sampling, but allow 100% sampling for canary routes for 24h during rollouts.
- Regression detection: automated diff on 95th latency week-over-week with a configurable tolerance (start at 15%).
- Ownership & approvals: any change to a route's paging threshold must include a sign-off from Product (product-counsel@redwood.com) if it reduces alert sensitivity for a customer-facing SLA.

I'll create a short Jira (SUP-1294) to track the playbook work and link the templates.

— Marcus
Principal Observability Engineer, Redwood Inference

On Fri, Oct 2, 2026 at 08:27 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com> wrote:
> +1 on Ava's scope. A few practical suggestions from the ops side: ...

From: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
To: Tom Hayes <tom.hayes@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-05T16:04:00-07:00
Subject: Observability quickstart: next steps and kickoff (calendar invite attached)

Tom — thanks again for the request. Quick summary & next steps: 

Deliverables we will share in the first drop (EOD next Thu, 2026-10-14):
- rdw-observability-quickstart-outline.md (one-pager + checklist)
- sample-alert-templates.json (per-route templates, severity levels)
- dashboard-pack (1-page per-route starter dashboard)

Actions for your team: please confirm representative routes (or send traffic samples) and any special high-cost routes by EOD Wednesday so we can seed the canary set.

I've attached the outline and the sample alert JSON; Marcus opened SUP-1294 to track acceptance criteria. Proposed 30-min kickoff: Oct 8 10:00-10:30 PT — I'll send the invite.

If that works, we'll plan a 1-hour handoff with field-eng the week after the kickoff.

Best regards,
Ava Chen
Product Manager, Observability — Redwood Inference

source.subject: FluxHealth pricing path: staged billing + midcycle uplift discussion
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Dunn <claire@fluxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-26T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Claire — great talking earlier. Per our call, FluxHealth is looking to add ~30% extra committed capacity mid-contract and prefers not to move the renewal date. Two things I want to propose so we can get legal and finance aligned quickly:

1) Staged billing: split the incremental uplift across 3 invoices over the remaining 9 months (monthly amortization on the uplift portion) so cashflow stays predictable on your side.

2) Discount ladder: a blended uplift discount of 8% on the additional QPS if you commit to the staged payment schedule, or 5% if you prefer a one-time midcycle invoice.

I've attached a draft SOW and a sample invoice proposal (staged schedule). If you can confirm preferred path (split vs one-off) and if procurement needs PO or set of statements, we'll route the right paperwork.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0127

[Attachment: Flux_SOW_v3.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: Invoice_Proposal_Staged.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Claire Dunn <claire@fluxhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-27T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Ben — thanks, this is helpful. A few clarifying items from procurement and finance:

• We prefer staged billing but need a clear invoice schedule we can attach to the PO. Can the staged invoices be tied to calendar months (e.g., Oct/Nov/Dec) instead of equal monthly amortization?
• Is the blended uplift discount of 8% applied only to the incremental capacity, or to total committed run rate after the top-up?
• Our renewal still runs to 2027-03-31 — is there any operational impact if the new capacity is co-termed to the existing renewal (i.e., prorated to end-of-March)?

Also looping in Eli (procurement) for PO timing.

—Claire
Claire Dunn
Director, Infrastructure Procurement
FluxHealth
claire@fluxhealth.com

[Procurement cc: Eli Navarro <eli@fluxhealth.com>]

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Dunn <claire@fluxhealth.com>, Eli Navarro <eli@fluxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-28T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Claire / Eli — answers inline: 

• Calendar months OK. We can create three invoices aligned to Oct/Nov/Dec (first invoice the month after signature). We'll include invoice lines referencing the PO number for matching.
• Discount applies only to the incremental capacity (the uplift portion). Base commitment pricing stays as-is.
• Co-term: we can pro-rate the added capacity to your renewal date and present two options in the SOW: (A) keep uplift on its own 12-month clock (no co-term), or (B) co-term and prorate the added capacity to 2027-03-31. Option B reduces the effective monthly billing in the short term but results in a higher renewal baseline next year.

Attached a small CSV that shows the prorate math for the co-term option; please review and tell me which you prefer. If you choose co-term, legal will prepare a short amendment rather than a full SOW replacement.

Next steps: pick staged vs one-off and whether you want co-term. I can copy the final invoice schedule into the PO attachment.

Ben
[Attachment: co-term_timing_and_prorate.csv (csv)]

From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-29T11:20:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Internal note — FluxHealth prefers staged (calendar months) and is leaning toward co-term to simplify next-year budgeting. Operational items to flag if we co-term:

• KV-cache and model routing changes: engineering needs a 7-day lead to adjust prefix caching if we enable additional dedicated pools mid-month.
• Billing system: finance needs the PO + signed amendment at least 5 business days before the first staged invoice.
• SLO impact: Marcus, can ops confirm whether adding 30% committed capacity to their existing pool triggers a different placement policy (different GPU class)? If yes, include a short clause about placement parity.

I recommend we provide an appendix that shows both pro-forma outcomes (co-term vs 12-month ramp) with clear renewal impact. I'll draft the appendix once Ben confirms pricing details.

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager — Redwood Inference
monica@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T15:43:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: approval ask and discount guidance for FluxHealth uplift

Folks — FluxHealth picked staged billing (Oct/Nov/Dec) and prefers to co-term. They asked for the 8% discount on the uplift if they commit to staged billing; procurement is sensitive to any renewal baseline increase next year. Pricing request: approve up to 10% uplift discount on the incremental capacity if we get a signed amendment + PO by 2026-10-10. If we cannot get exec approval for 10%, propose we do 8% and offer a one-time credit vs extended discount.

Questions for leadership: Lauren, can you greenlight up to 10% for this midcycle uplift for FluxHealth (Strategic, 3yr pipeline)? Marcus, ops confirmation on placement parity would be helpful to avoid SLA exceptions. If approved, I will send a short amendment for signature and schedule the Oct invoice to go out 10/15.

Thanks — trying to keep it tight on timing.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

[Related link: https://hubspot.example/deal/DEAL-4532]

title: FluxHealth pricing path: staged billing + midcycle uplift discussion
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Dunn <claire@fluxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-26T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Claire — great talking earlier. Per our call, FluxHealth is looking to add ~30% extra committed capacity mid-contract and prefers not to move the renewal date. Two things I want to propose so we can get legal and finance aligned quickly:

1) Staged billing: split the incremental uplift across 3 invoices over the remaining 9 months (monthly amortization on the uplift portion) so cashflow stays predictable on your side.

2) Discount ladder: a blended uplift discount of 8% on the additional QPS if you commit to the staged payment schedule, or 5% if you prefer a one-time midcycle invoice.

I've attached a draft SOW and a sample invoice proposal (staged schedule). If you can confirm preferred path (split vs one-off) and if procurement needs PO or set of statements, we'll route the right paperwork.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0127

[Attachment: Flux_SOW_v3.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: Invoice_Proposal_Staged.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Claire Dunn <claire@fluxhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-27T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Ben — thanks, this is helpful. A few clarifying items from procurement and finance:

• We prefer staged billing but need a clear invoice schedule we can attach to the PO. Can the staged invoices be tied to calendar months (e.g., Oct/Nov/Dec) instead of equal monthly amortization?
• Is the blended uplift discount of 8% applied only to the incremental capacity, or to total committed run rate after the top-up?
• Our renewal still runs to 2027-03-31 — is there any operational impact if the new capacity is co-termed to the existing renewal (i.e., prorated to end-of-March)?

Also looping in Eli (procurement) for PO timing.

—Claire
Claire Dunn
Director, Infrastructure Procurement
FluxHealth
claire@fluxhealth.com

[Procurement cc: Eli Navarro <eli@fluxhealth.com>]

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Claire Dunn <claire@fluxhealth.com>, Eli Navarro <eli@fluxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-28T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Claire / Eli — answers inline: 

• Calendar months OK. We can create three invoices aligned to Oct/Nov/Dec (first invoice the month after signature). We'll include invoice lines referencing the PO number for matching.
• Discount applies only to the incremental capacity (the uplift portion). Base commitment pricing stays as-is.
• Co-term: we can pro-rate the added capacity to your renewal date and present two options in the SOW: (A) keep uplift on its own 12-month clock (no co-term), or (B) co-term and prorate the added capacity to 2027-03-31. Option B reduces the effective monthly billing in the short term but results in a higher renewal baseline next year.

Attached a small CSV that shows the prorate math for the co-term option; please review and tell me which you prefer. If you choose co-term, legal will prepare a short amendment rather than a full SOW replacement.

Next steps: pick staged vs one-off and whether you want co-term. I can copy the final invoice schedule into the PO attachment.

Ben
[Attachment: co-term_timing_and_prorate.csv (csv)]

From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-29T11:20:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Pricing path: staged billing + uplift options for midcycle growth

Internal note — FluxHealth prefers staged (calendar months) and is leaning toward co-term to simplify next-year budgeting. Operational items to flag if we co-term:

• KV-cache and model routing changes: engineering needs a 7-day lead to adjust prefix caching if we enable additional dedicated pools mid-month.
• Billing system: finance needs the PO + signed amendment at least 5 business days before the first staged invoice.
• SLO impact: Marcus, can ops confirm whether adding 30% committed capacity to their existing pool triggers a different placement policy (different GPU class)? If yes, include a short clause about placement parity.

I recommend we provide an appendix that shows both pro-forma outcomes (co-term vs 12-month ramp) with clear renewal impact. I'll draft the appendix once Ben confirms pricing details.

Thanks,
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager — Redwood Inference
monica@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T15:43:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: approval ask and discount guidance for FluxHealth uplift

Folks — FluxHealth picked staged billing (Oct/Nov/Dec) and prefers to co-term. They asked for the 8% discount on the uplift if they commit to staged billing; procurement is sensitive to any renewal baseline increase next year. Pricing request: approve up to 10% uplift discount on the incremental capacity if we get a signed amendment + PO by 2026-10-10. If we cannot get exec approval for 10%, propose we do 8% and offer a one-time credit vs extended discount.

Questions for leadership: Lauren, can you greenlight up to 10% for this midcycle uplift for FluxHealth (Strategic, 3yr pipeline)? Marcus, ops confirmation on placement parity would be helpful to avoid SLA exceptions. If approved, I will send a short amendment for signature and schedule the Oct invoice to go out 10/15.

Thanks — trying to keep it tight on timing.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

[Related link: https://hubspot.example/deal/DEAL-4532]
