source.subject: Invoice PRO-7891 — unexpected token spike during maintenance window
messages:
From: Samir Rao <samir@quickcart.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: QuickCart Finance <billing@quickcart.io>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Invoice PRO-7891 — apparent token spike during backup window

Ben —

We just received invoice PRO-7891 and there is a large overage line for 2,640,312 tokens on 2026-09-28. Our internal telemetry and sampling show ~1.1M tokens for the same timeframe. The spike appears to coincide with a scheduled backup/ingestion window at 02:00 UTC where we had increased ingestion throughput, but nothing that should generate that magnitude of tokens.

I've attached a CSV export of our request logs (quickcart_token_export_2026-09-28.csv) with request IDs, timestamps, and token counts for your team to reconcile. Can you please: 
- Confirm how Redwood attributes tokens to customers during short spikes and maintenance windows
- Provide the server-side token export you used to populate the invoice
- Pause collections on this invoice while we reconcile (we're close to our payment deadline)

We're happy to hop on a quick call if it's easier — this will block our AP team from approving payment otherwise.

Thanks,
Samir Rao
Head of Platform
QuickCart Inc.
---
Attachments: quickcart_token_export_2026-09-28.csv (CSV, 2.1MB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Rao <samir@quickcart.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice PRO-7891 — apparent token spike during backup window

Samir —

Thanks for sending this over and for the CSV — I've received it. I'm going to loop in Billing (Marissa) and our Runtime team (Marcus) to do a side-by-side reconciliation. Preliminary notes:

- We will pull the token attribution export for your account for 2026-09-28 and compare request_id-level counts.
- Please confirm the range you want us to consider (your CSV looks like 01:50:00–03:10:00 UTC). If there are additional off-hours windows, call them out.
- If possible, send 3 representative request IDs from your CSV that you believe are mismatched — that helps our engineers trace the exact request path.

Action plan / timing: 
1) Marcus will pull the server-side export and check for duplicate deliveries / retry storms.
2) Marissa will prepare a provisional billing delta and propose a credit if we identify attribution error.
3) I'll coordinate a 30m sync with your engineering lead if needed — propose today at 2pm PT.

I will also ask Billing to flag the invoice for review so collections are paused until we have the initial findings.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Rao <samir@quickcart.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice PRO-7891 — preliminary billing review and next steps

Hi Samir —

Thanks for the export and Ben for the heads up. We've completed a first-pass reconciliation for 2026-09-28 and wanted to share a quick summary and next steps. Attached is a short PDF with the row-level comparison (prelim-billing-breakdown-PRO-7891.pdf).

Summary (preliminary):
- Redwood invoice shows 2,640,312 tokens attributed between 01:45–03:15 UTC.
- Our server-side logs include a cluster of duplicate request deliveries during 02:03–02:11 UTC that were retried by a downstream consumer. Those retried deliveries were, in our opinion, incorrectly double-counted once they retried with a different request_id but identical payload.
- Matching your sample rows indicates ~1.15M unique tokens (matches what you reported) plus ~1.48M from duplicated deliveries, which is how the invoice number was reached.

What we need from you to confirm root cause: 
- A short list (3–5) of request IDs from your CSV that you want us to cross-check; for faster tracing, include the exact timestamp(s).
- Confirmation whether QuickCart treats retries from the streaming consumer as idempotent on your side (i.e., you de-duplicate before consumption).

Proposed commercial remediation (preliminary and subject to final verification):
- If duplicates were caused by our delivery path / routing retries, Redwood will issue a one-time credit for the duplicated token volume. Based on current numbers, we estimate a credit of ~$2,200.
- We can apply the credit to invoice PRO-7891 or issue a proforma credit note, whatever helps your AP team.
- If duplicates were caused upstream on QuickCart (consumer retries without idempotency), we'll document the mismatch and provide the row-level evidence but will not apply a credit.

Next steps: Marcus will paste a couple of matched examples in a follow-up email to aid the engineering investigation. If it helps, we can also share an aggregated token export for the 30‑minute spike window.

I'll put the invoice on hold in our collections system pending your confirmation and the next set of request IDs.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Billing Operations Lead, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai
---
Attachments: prelim-billing-breakdown-PRO-7891.pdf (PDF, 420KB)
Related: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/PRO-7891

From: Samir Rao <samir@quickcart.io>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, QuickCart Finance <billing@quickcart.io>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice PRO-7891 — preliminary billing review and next steps

Marissa / Ben —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the explanation. We appreciate Redwood flagging the invoice for review. Per your request, here are 4 request IDs from our export that show the discrepancy (timestamps in UTC):

- rq_20260928_020307_az12 (2026-09-28T02:03:07Z) — tokens: 12,410
- rq_20260928_020309_az15 (2026-09-28T02:03:09Z) — tokens: 9,834
- rq_20260928_020406_az03 (2026-09-28T02:04:06Z) — tokens: 14,220
- rq_20260928_020657_az09 (2026-09-28T02:06:57Z) — tokens: 8,132

We confirm that our consumer layer does de-duplicate by request_id, but we do not dedupe on payload similarity if a new request_id is observed. If duplicates are a result of your delivery retries that manifested as new request IDs, we agree that a credit is appropriate.

Please apply the provisional credit to PRO-7891 while you complete the verification and share the final row-level mapping when available — our AP team will accept a proforma credit note. If you want to jump on the 2pm PT sync Ben suggested, Samir (me) and our SRE will be available.

Samir
Head of Platform, QuickCart
---
Quoted text follows.

- Our server-side logs include a cluster of duplicate request deliveries during 02:03–02:11 UTC that were retried by a downstream consumer. Those retried deliveries were, in our opinion, incorrectly double-counted once they retried with a different request_id but identical payload.
- Matching your sample rows indicates ~1.15M unique tokens (matches what you reported) plus ~1.48M from duplicated deliveries, which is how the invoice number was reached.

What we need from you to confirm root cause: 
- A short list (3–5) of request IDs from your CSV that you want us to cross-check; for faster tracing, include the exact timestamp(s).
- Confirmation whether QuickCart treats retries from the streaming consumer as idempotent on your side (i.e., you de-duplicate before consumption).

Proposed commercial remediation (preliminary and subject to final verification):
- If duplicates were caused by our delivery path / routing retries, Redwood will issue a one-time credit for the duplicated token volume. Based on current numbers, we estimate a credit of ~$2,200.
- We can apply the credit to invoice PRO-7891 or issue a proforma credit note, whatever helps your AP team.
- If duplicates were caused upstream on QuickCart (consumer retries without idempotency), we'll document the mismatch and provide the row-level evidence but will not apply a credit.

Next steps: Marcus will paste a couple of matched examples in a follow-up email to aid the engineering investigation. If it helps, we can also share an aggregated token export for the 30‑minute spike window.

I'll put the invoice on hold in our collections system pending your confirmation and the next set of request IDs.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Billing Operations Lead, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai
---
Attachments: prelim-billing-breakdown-PRO-7891.pdf (PDF, 420KB)
Related: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/PRO-7891



From: Samir Rao <samir@quickcart.io>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, QuickCart Finance <billing@quickcart.io>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice PRO-7891 — preliminary billing review and next steps

Marissa / Ben —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the explanation. We appreciate Redwood flagging the invoice for review. Per your request, here are 4 request IDs from our export that show the discrepancy (timestamps in UTC):

- rq_20260928_020307_az12 (2026-09-28T02:03:07Z) — tokens: 12,410
- rq_20260928_020309_az15 (2026-09-28T02:03:09Z) — tokens: 9,834
- rq_20260928_020406_az03 (2026-09-28T02:04:06Z) — tokens: 14,220
- rq_20260928_020657_az09 (2026-09-28T02:06:57Z) — tokens: 8,132

We confirm that our consumer layer does de-duplicate by request_id, but we do not dedupe on payload similarity if a new request_id is observed. If duplicates are a result of your delivery retries that manifested as new request IDs, we agree that a credit is appropriate.

Please apply the provisional credit to PRO-7891 while you complete the verification and share the final row-level mapping when available — our AP team will accept a proforma credit note. If you want to jump on the 2pm PT sync Ben suggested, Samir (me) and our SRE will be available.

Samir
Head of Platform, QuickCart
---
Quoted text follows.

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Chen <laura@brightbridge.com>
Cc: ops@brightbridge.com
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2026 09:00:00 -0400
Subject: Redwood demo — Tuesday 9/15 at 10:00am ET (agenda attached)

Hi Laura —

Thanks again for taking time to meet. This is a quick confirmation for our 30-minute demo on Tuesday, Sept 15 at 10:00am ET.

Agenda (attached):
- 10m: Quick intros + BrightBridge priorities
- 12m: Demo — low-latency inference and routing policies
- 6m: Deployment options (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private) + next steps

Zoom link is on the calendar invite; I attached a short agenda PDF. If anything changes on your end, reply here and I can shift.

Cheers,
Ben
Ben Carter | Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0142

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Chen <laura@brightbridge.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2026 10:15:00 -0400
Subject: Missed you — quick next steps?

Laura —

I waited in the demo room for about 10 minutes and didn’t see anyone join. No worries — I know travel and ops days can get crazy.

If you’re still interested, would you prefer:
- A: Reschedule a full 30-minute demo with the same agenda
- B: A focused 20-minute architecture-and-costing call with Marcus (SE)
- C: An async recording + a 15-minute Q&A office hours slot next week

If you pick A or B, I can send three time buckets that work for our team. If it’s easier, tell me a preferred block and I’ll match.

Apologies if I missed a message from you earlier.

Best,
Ben

---

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2026 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Missed you — BrightBridge follow-up (quick internal)

Marcus / Monica —

Heads-up: BrightBridge (Laura) missed our demo on Tue. They’re a decent-fit pilot: healthcare analytics + strict latency SLA ask. I recommend we:
- Reopen with a compact set of options (20–30m) to reduce coordination friction
- Offer Marcus for the technical deep-dive (20m) or keep it product-led (30m) depending on their availability
- Present a short pilot mutual action plan in the follow-up if they commit to a weekday slot

Can Marcus take point if she prefers the SE call? Monica, can you prepare a single-slide pricing snapshot and a short note on our data-retention options?

I’ll send the outbound message with time buckets and an availability sweep (Calendly link).

—Ben

---

To: Laura Chen <laura@brightbridge.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 19 Sep 2026 11:00:00 -0400
Subject: Two-minute pick — 3 compact windows for BrightBridge (ET/UTC prefs)

Laura —

Totally understand if your calendar got swallowed last week. I wanted to make rescheduling as painless as possible — three compact windows below (all ET):

Option 1 — Thu, Sep 23 | 11:00–11:30 AM ET (30m, product + q&a)
Option 2 — Thu, Sep 23 | 02:00–02:20 PM ET (20m, Marcus for technical deep-dive)
Option 3 — Fri, Sep 24 | 09:00–09:30 AM ET (30m, product-led)

If none of those work, tell me 2x 1-hour blocks (morning or afternoon) that suit you and I’ll align people on our side. Quick notes:
- Marcus will join Option 2 if you want architecture and inference-cost discussion.
- We can keep it to 20m and follow up with a recorded walk-through and a short MO (mutual action) plan.

I also included a small screenshot of our Scheduling page in case you want to pick an open slot: https://cal.redwood.ai/p/ben/brightbridge-slot-sweep (link) — screenshot attached.

Would you be able to pick one by EOD Tue so we can lock a room? Thanks!

Best,
Ben
Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0142

Attachments: slots-screenshot-09-19.png

---

Sorry about the no-show earlier — we had an unexpected operational incident and the whole team was pulled into triage. I appreciate the follow-up and the clear options.

Option 1 (Thu, Sep 23 | 11:00 AM ET) works best for us. Marcus joining Option 2 is also fine if you prefer the technical path, but let’s start with Option 1 and keep it to 30 minutes.

source.subject: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@stitchwave.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial

Hi Lena / Tom —

Thanks again for taking the time today — good conversation. Per our call, I wanted to document the proposed POC scope, measurable success criteria, and the short ops runbook so we can align before the kickoff next week. High-level items below; details and the formal acceptance doc attached.

Quick recap (what we agreed):
- Scope: 2 endpoints — summarization for customer support tickets (batch) and routing/classification (real-time).
- Duration: 4-week trial (pilot window), starting week of Sep 21.
- Dataset: StitchWave will provide a scoped sample (up to 10k records) and 200 evaluator prompts + labels for quality checks.

Proposed POC acceptance KPIs (boarding targets; we can adjust):
- Latency: p95 & p99 for the real-time routing endpoint — p95 < 60ms, p99 < 150ms (in-region).
- Throughput: sustained 500 req/s for routing with 100 concurrent users simulated during load windows.
- Cost: target inference cost < $0.0009 / token (hosted Redwood config + quantized model profile).
- Quality: label-based accuracy on the 200 eval prompts — baseline target >= 92% F1 for intent classification; summary ROUGE-L improvement >= 12 over current baseline.

Evaluation plan / owners:
- StitchWave (Lena/Tom): deliver sample dataset + 200 labeled eval prompts by Sep 18.
- Redwood (Aditya / Marcus): provision trial tenant, run canonical workload benchmarks, provide evaluation harness and baseline runs by Sep 24.
- Monica: handle success tracking dashboard and weekly readout cadence with StitchWave.

Operational notes:
- Rollout & fallback policy: requests will default to model A (quantized) with automatic fallback to model B if latency > 250ms or OOM occurs.
- Logging & telemetry: we will provide per-request latency and token-usage traces; StitchWave to confirm HIPAA/GDPR constraints for logs.

Attachments: POC acceptance doc (attached), test prompts CSV (attached).

Next steps (action items):
1) Lena: confirm dataset delivery date (target Sep 18).
2) Marcus: confirm GPU pool sizing and expected throughput test window (I asked for 2 x A100 equivalent for throughput test).
3) Aditya: send final acceptance doc and HubSpot link (sent with this thread).

If that matches your notes, I’ll lock calendar invites for the kickoff (Sep 22) and the first weekly readout (Sep 29).

Thanks —
Aditya
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: StitchWave_POC_Acceptance_v1.pdf (application/pdf), stitchwave-test-prompts.csv (text/csv)

From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@stitchwave.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <t.nguyen@stitchwave.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 15:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial

Thanks Aditya — this looks great and matches what we discussed. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Dataset: we’ll deliver an initial 5k ticket sample and the 200 evaluator prompts by EOD Sep 17 (internal ops pushed earlier).
- Latency requirement: p99 < 150ms is acceptable, but can we capture p99 over both in-region and cross-region (we have EU users too)? If we can get cross-region p99 < 250ms that would be ideal.
- Cost: StitchWave procurement will want a unit-cost estimate for both hosted and dedicated options — can you include an estimated cost delta if we move to dedicated capacity?

Also — one ask: For the quality metric, rather than ROUGE-L improvement alone, can we include a small human-eval check for 30 random summaries to validate coherence? We’ll own the labeling, but would like Redwood to provide the human-eval rubric.

We’re aligned on the Sep 22 kickoff — I’ll accept once I’ve pushed the dataset and prompts.

Cheers,
Lena
Head of Product, StitchWave

On Tue, Sep 15, 2026 at 14:03, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Cc: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@stitchwave.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 08:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial

Looping in some infra detail here so we don’t miss timelines.

Aditya / Monica — I confirmed capacity for the week-of Sep 21 kickoff: we can provision a single reserved footprint of 2 x A100 equivalent (preemptible pool) for the throughput tests and an autoscaling policy for the live evaluation with an upper cap of 6 GPUs. Notes: 
- For the latency numbers, we’ll run both in-region and EU overlay tests. Expect p95 measurements to be stable; p99 can spike during cold-cache windows. We’ll do warm-up passes prior to any measurement windows.
- Cost modeling: hosted config (quantized) should hit ~ $0.00085/token at the tested batch sizes; dedicated perf (pinned) will be ~10–15% lower per-token but add fixed monthly infra. I’ll draft a quick cost delta and add to the acceptance doc.

I’ll also prepare a short-runbook for timeout/oom conditions and give Marcus infra ownership on the Friday readout.

—Marcus
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@stitchwave.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial

Perfect — thanks all for the fast turnaround. Updated items and final confirmation below (I’ll attach the versioned acceptance doc to HubSpot after this):

Confirmed changes / final targets:
- Latency: p95 < 60ms (in-region), p99 < 150ms (in-region), cross-region p99 < 250ms (EU).
- Throughput: sustained 500 req/s for routing endpoint during the throughput window; warm-up passes beforehand.
- Cost: target inference cost <= $0.0009/token for hosted quantized profile; expected dedicated delta -10–15% on per-token with monthly reserved fee (Marcus to add $ estimates).
- Quality: automated eval target >= 92% F1 on 200-labeled prompts + human-eval pass on 30 random summaries (rubric attached).

Action items (owners and dates):
- StitchWave: deliver dataset + 200 eval prompts by Sep 17 (Lena).
- Redwood infra: size GPU pool and provide cost delta by Sep 18 (Marcus).
- Redwood eval: run baseline + provide evaluation harness and first-run metrics by Sep 24 (Aditya + Marcus).
- Ops/reporting: weekly readout (Monica) — first session Sep 29.

Kickoff: Sep 22 at 10:00am PT — I’ll send calendar invites (30m kickoff + 60m throughput test session scheduled Sep 24).

If there are no objections I’ll finalize the acceptance doc and push the artifacts to the HubSpot deal record. Thanks!

Aditya
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

---
Attachments pushed to HubSpot deal and Drive: https://hubspot.example.com/deal/4321 | https://drive.google.com/file/d/1a2b3cPOCacceptance

I’ll also prepare a short-runbook for timeout/oom conditions and give Marcus infra ownership on the Friday readout.

—Marcus
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@stitchwave.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POC acceptance criteria & ops targets — StitchWave trial

Perfect — thanks all for the fast turnaround. Updated items and final confirmation below (I’ll attach the versioned acceptance doc to HubSpot after this):

Confirmed changes / final targets:
- Latency: p95 < 60ms (in-region), p99 < 150ms (in-region), cross-region p99 < 250ms (EU).
- Throughput: sustained 500 req/s for routing endpoint during the throughput window; warm-up passes beforehand.
- Cost: target inference cost <= $0.0009/token for hosted quantized profile; expected dedicated delta -10–15% on per-token with monthly reserved fee (Marcus to add $ estimates).
- Quality: automated eval target >= 92% F1 on 200-labeled prompts + human-eval pass on 30 random summaries (rubric attached).

Action items (owners and dates):
- StitchWave: deliver dataset + 200 eval prompts by Sep 17 (Lena).
- Redwood infra: size GPU pool and provide cost delta by Sep 18 (Marcus).
- Redwood eval: run baseline + provide evaluation harness and first-run metrics by Sep 24 (Aditya + Marcus).
- Ops/reporting: weekly readout (Monica) — first session Sep 29.

Kickoff: Sep 22 at 10:00am PT — I’ll send calendar invites (30m kickoff + 60m throughput test session scheduled Sep 24).

If there are no objections I’ll finalize the acceptance doc and push the artifacts to the HubSpot deal record. Thanks!

Aditya
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference

---

source.subject: Asteria Health - Dedicated capacity & SKU proposal (H100 / A100 / L4)
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Wu <samantha.wu@asteriahealth.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity sizing & SKU options — quick ask

Hi Samantha —

Great speaking yesterday. Per our discussion I pulled together an operational framing so we can pick the right Dedicated SKU mix (H100 / A100 / L4) for Asteria's inference footprint. Quick background questions that will help finalize the sizing: 

- Typical request latency SLO (p95) for real-time patient inference?
- Median and 95th percentile sequence length and tokens per request (examples OK)
- Percentage of long-context workloads (>8k tokens) vs short (<=2k)
- Any strict memory residency requirements (model state retention, KV cache expectations)?
- Monthly committed QPS or token budget (if already estimated)

Attached I included a couple of sample worksheets — one rough cost/throughput matrix and a blank mutual action plan worksheet. If you can share a small sample of production traces or the attached CSV filled with token distribution, we can map a recommended SKU mix and reserve/burst band.

Operationally, our typical guidance:
- H100: best for very large-context or memory-bound models where single-GPU memory and NVLink matters; higher per-hour cost but fewer nodes for memory-heavy workloads.
- A100: strong general-purpose option for throughput at a lower unit price than H100; good fit when model size fits within 40GB / 80GB variants and you want predictable throughput.
- L4: cost-efficient for short-context, latency-sensitive workloads and embedding-heavy pipelines; lower memory but high inference efficiency for smaller models and quantized weights.

Action: If you can paste a short telemetry sample by EOD Wed, I’ll run a quick slotting run and return a proposed Dedicated mix with reserve vs burst ratios and expected p95 latency impact.

Thanks —
Ben
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: asteria_workload_samples.csv (csv), RDW-dedicated-sku-estimate-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Samantha Wu <samantha.wu@asteriahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity sizing & SKU options — quick ask

Ben —

Thanks for the clear note. I attached a small sample trace and a rough QPS expectation from our alpha. High-level answers:

- p95 SLO target: 250ms for clinical routing calls; bulk batch jobs can be 2–3s.
- Token distribution (attached CSV): median ~480 tokens, 95th ~4,200 tokens, long tail to 12k (~2% of traffic).
- Long-context workload (12k) is mostly for multi-document synthesis in nightly batch; real-time calls are typically <2k.
- KV/state: we rely on a small per-session KV for conversation context; not large, but we prefer not to spill to disk during normal inference.
- Committed target: starting at 3k QPS with bursts to 6k during business hours; monthly token budget TBD but rough estimate ~1.8B tokens/month.

We can share the sample traces as a CSV — attached. Happy to hop on a short call if helpful.

Best,
Samantha Wu
Head of Product
Asteria Health

Attachments: asteria_workload_samples.csv (csv)

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 12:45:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Asteria traces — initial SKU mapping thoughts

Ben / Marcus —

Forwarding Samantha’s trace. Initial read: majority short requests (~480 tokens) and a small long-tail at ~12k (2%). Their p95 latency target 250ms is tight, and they plan to ramp to steady 3k QPS. Points to consider before we propose:

- Memory residency: "prefer not to spill to disk" — we should design for KV in-GPU cache for session state. That biases toward H100/A100 based on model sizes.
- Mixed workload: latency-sensitive short requests + occasional long-context jobs. H100 gives headroom for long-context batching but at higher cost. L4 could cover 70–80% of short requests for cost savings if we accept small p95 degradation.
- Autoscaling & fallbacks: propose a primary L4 pool for short-context traffic with automatic fallthrough to A100 in the event of L4 slot saturation, and a small H100 pool reserved for long-context batch and ad-hoc high-memory fallbacks.

I sketched a reserve/burst split in the attached spreadsheet — Marcus, can you sanity-check memory footprint assumptions and quantization paths?

Irene
Director, Solutions Architecture
Redwood Inference

Attachments: RDW-dedicated-sku-estimate-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), SAR-telemetry-snippets.txt (text)

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Asteria traces — initial SKU mapping thoughts

Irene —

Checked the samples. Quick technical notes:

- Model sizing: assume their production model variants are 30–40B parameter family (based on token patterns). That maps to ~40–80GB unquantized; we should expect to rely on 80GB A100s or H100s if they want full fp16 without sharding.
- Quantization: if Asteria is willing to accept 4-bit quantization for shorter-context models, L4 becomes very attractive; we can run embeddings + short generation on L4 with quantized weights for ~2–3x cost improvement.
- KV cache: built GPU-resident KV for session context is feasible on 80GB-class GPUs; 40GB will need more aggressive eviction or prefix caching.
- Concurrency/tiling: to meet 3k QPS steady-state with median 480 tokens, an L4 pool sized for throughput is cost-efficient. Reserve H100 slots at ~5–7% of capacity strictly for long-context batch and fallback. A100 (80GB) can be the medium tier for mixed or mid-size models.

Ops notes: we should recommend a staged rollout. Phase 1 (Q1) — reserve A100 80GB pool for baseline and an L4 pool at 50% of expected short traffic; Phase 2 (Q2) — ramp L4 to 70–80% coverage with A100 as fallback; keep H100 minimal reserved for long-context and emergency.

I updated the workbook with memory footprints and a sample reserve/burst calculator.

—Marcus
Principal Inference Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: RDW-dedicated-sku-estimate-v1.xlsx (updated)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samantha Wu <samantha.wu@asteriahealth.com>, procure@asteriahealth.com
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: hybrid Dedicated pools (L4 primary, A100 fallback, H100 reserved)

Hi Samantha —

Thanks again for the telemetry. Based on the traces and internal sizing review, here’s a pragmatic proposal we can take to contract: 

Summary recommendation (high-level)
- Primary pool: L4 (quantized models) sizing to cover ~70% of steady short-context QPS (cost-efficient for median 480-token requests).
- Fallback pool: A100 80GB to cover remaining short-to-mid workloads and handle L4 saturation without p95 regression.
- Reserved H100: a small pool (5–7% of peak capacity) for long-context runs and memory-bound fallbacks (nightly multi-document synthesis).

Reserve vs burst (example numbers)
- Reserve: L4 150 slots, A100 40 slots, H100 4 slots (baseline committed).
- Burst capacity: additional 80 L4 slots + 30 A100 slots available via burst pool; H100 burst on demand (pre-warmed) but billed only when active.

Expected outcomes- Meets p95 250ms for short-context when L4+fallback configured and autoscaling thresholds tuned.
- Keeps committed spend lower by offloading bulk short traffic to L4 while preserving latency via A100 fallback.
- H100 reserved for long-context ensures night-batch SLAs without overcommitting expensive capacity.

Next steps
1) Approve the reserve/burst example numbers (or provide preferred committed slot counts).
2) Share final token budget to finalize monthly pricing estimate.
3) Sign the attached mutual action plan; we’ll schedule a 45-minute run-through with Marcus and our Ops team to finalize autoscale fencing and pre-warm policies.

I attached a filled version of the estimate workbook with the slot math and cost delta. Happy to adjust numbers on a quick call.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: RDW-dedicated-sku-estimate-v1.xlsx (proposal), asteria_workload_samples.csv (reference)

source.subject: GCP co-sell: weekly partner scrum + pipeline prioritization
messages:
From: Samuel Harris <samuel.harris@google.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@stratusai.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: GCP co-sell weekly partner scrum — agenda & initial account list

Ben / team,

Thanks for standing up the regular sync. Quick note to propose a short weekly cadence (30 min) to go through the GCP co-sell accounts where StratusAI has indicated interest in Redwood Dedicated. Goal: agree on top 6 accounts to focus, confirm who owns the next touches, and surface any security/compliance blockers.

Proposed agenda (30m):
- 5m: roll call + updates on any new marketplace listings
- 10m: pipeline review (top 6 prioritized opps)
- 10m: blockers / procurement / SOC 2 evidence requests
- 5m: action items & next steps

Initial account list (suggested):
1) LumenHealth — procurement intro pending (high ARR)
2) Stratus R&D — pilot kick-off next week
3) Acme Financial — security questionnaire in flight
4) Optima Logistics — pricing sensitivity

messages:
From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.inference>
To: Michael Yates <michael.yates@asteriontech.com>, Sara Lopez <s.lopez@asteriontech.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 11 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Executive dry-run & contingency for Asterion product demo

Hi Michael / Sara,

Thanks again for the time last week. We want to make sure the executive portion of the demo hits the right business points for your CIO and VPs. Proposed plan:

- Main demo: Tue, Sep 21, 2026 — 10:00-11:00 PT (CIO requested slot)
- Executive dry-run (internal): Mon, Sep 20, 2026 — 10:00-10:30 PT
- Attendees requested: CIO (primary), VP of Product (backup), Solutions sponsor (your team)

Can you confirm that the CIO slot above is still the preferred time? If the CIO cannot attend we want to have a documented contingency so we bring the right audience (VP + Head of Infrastructure). I attached the agenda (Asterion_Demo_Agenda_v1.pdf) for context.

If there are any required IT/SSO checks on your side, please flag them now so we can run the pre-demo checklist.

Thanks,

Soojin Lee
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
soojin.lee@redwood.inference
(415) 555-1212

Attachment: Asterion_Demo_Agenda_v1.pdf
From: Michael Yates <michael.yates@asteriontech.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.inference>
Cc: Sara Lopez <s.lopez@asteriontech.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Executive dry-run & contingency for Asterion product demo

Hi Soojin —

Quick heads up: CIO Mark Hanson is traveling on Sep 21 and won't be available that day. He can either join a short follow-up briefing (if we keep the Sep 21 slot for the broader team) or we can shift to Sep 23 where his calendar is open.

VP of Product (Dana Ruiz) can cover the exec portion on Sep 21 if you're OK with that. I also want to loop in Procurement (Emily Kim) for any licensing/contract questions — I've cc'ed Emily on this reply.

Michael
Director, Enterprise IT | Asterion Tech
From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.inference>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 11 Sep 2026 12:20:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Re: Executive dry-run & contingency for Asterion product demo

Karthik / Marcus — forwarding Michael's note. CIO is likely out on the requested demo date; VP of Product (Dana Ruiz) may be available as a replacement. Two asks: 1) can we confirm an alternate VP-level speaker (Dana works for them) and 2) can we run the internal dry-run on Mon, Sep 20 10:00 PT to align positioning for both CIO and VP scenarios?

I want a short scenario script for the dry-run: 5 min intro, 15 min ops + cost demo, 10 min Q&A, 5 min closing w/ next steps. Marcus — please prepare the quick cost/latency slide and Karthik please prep the handoff language for procurement.

Thanks —
Soojin

> On Fri, Sep 11, 2026 at 11:03 AM Michael Yates <michael.yates@asteriontech.com> wrote:
> Hi Soojin —
> 
> Quick heads up: CIO Mark Hanson is traveling on Sep 21 and won't be available that day. ...

To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>
Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Re: Executive dry-run & contingency for Asterion product demo

Soojin —

Dana Ruiz (VP Product) confirmed she can lead the exec portion on Sep 21. We'll treat the CIO briefing as a separate follow-up but keep the main demo on Sep 21 for the broader technical audience.

I'm good to run the internal dry-run Mon Sep 20 10:00-10:30 PT. I'll attach the SSO checklist and quick performance slide so we can rehearse Karthik's handoff.

Attachment: SSO_Checklist_Asterion.xlsx

Marcus Lin
Principal Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference
From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.inference>
To: Michael Yates <michael.yates@asteriontech.com>, Sara Lopez <s.lopez@asteriontech.com>, Emily Kim <emily.kim@asteriontech.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.inference>
Date: Sun, 13 Sep 2026 16:15:00 -0700
Subject: Confirmed: Sep 21 demo (VP attending) + internal dry-run invite

Michael / Sara / Emily —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. Confirming:

- Public demo: Tue, Sep 21, 2026 — 10:00-11:00 PT (Dana Ruiz, VP Product will present the executive section)
- Internal dry-run: Mon, Sep 20, 2026 — 10:00-10:30 PT (join link in calendar invite)

Marcus will lead the dry-run; Karthik will own procurement handoff language. I've shared a read-only Drive folder with the agenda and checklist here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/0B-example-asterion-demo

Emily — appreciate if you can confirm any procurement checkpoints we should mention (trial terms, pilot SOW, support SLAs). If you'd like a separate procurement pre-call, we can book 30 min the week prior.

Calendar invites will follow; I attached the final agenda and the SSO checklist for your IT team to review.

Best,

Soojin Lee
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference

Attachments: Asterion_Demo_Agenda_v1.pdf, SSO_Checklist_Asterion.xlsx

To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.inference>
Cc: Sara Lopez <s.lopez@asteriontech.com>, Emily Kim <emily.kim@asteriontech.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 16:05:00 -0700

Soojin —

All set on our side. Dana will lead the exec section. Emily confirmed procurement will attend the demo and might drop into the dry-run if helpful. Please send the calendar invites (and the dial-in / webex link).

Also adding procurement's internal PO reviewer: Trevor Mills (trevor.mills@asteriontech.com) to the loop for contract timing.

Thanks — looking forward to it.

Michael

source.subject: Re: Production p99 latency spikes — remediation & concession request
messages:
From: Laura Jenkins <laura.jenkins@finovate.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@finovate.com
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0400
Subject: Production p99 latency spikes — impact to customers

Ava —

Over the last 72 hours we've seen repeated p99 tail-latency spikes on the Payments normalization route. Two downstream microservices timed out yesterday during our evening peak window and we had to roll traffic to a cached fallback, which degraded authorization accuracy for a short period. Our ops team attached a short CSV with the latency snapshots and a timeline.

This is impacting three of our largest merchants and we've been asked for a remediation plan + commercial offset while you investigate. Our renewals committee is watching this closely — we need a clear proposed concession (credit / free month / remediation commitments) and a timeline for the fix.

Please advise next steps and an ETA for a remediation mitigation we can communicate to our customers.

Attachments: finovate_p99_snapshot_sep14.csv (12KB)

Thanks,
Laura Jenkins
Head of Platform Reliability
Finovate Payments
laura.jenkins@finovate.com

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 10:05:00 -0400
Subject: Fwd: Production p99 latency spikes — impact to customers

Forwarding Laura's note — Finovate reports sustained tail-latency during evening peaks (attached). They explicitly asked for a remediation plan + commercial offset.

My read: the immediate ask is two-fold: 1) a commercial concession to restore trust (credit or free month) and 2) operational mitigation to stop recurrence (routing change / dedicated capacity window).

Proposed quick structure for exec review:
- Immediate one-month service credit applied to next invoice (covers customer pain window).
- Pro-rated usage credit for any overage during the incident days (auto-offset).
- Offer a 90-day dedicated capacity reservation for their peak window (we staff and pin nodes, limited to their busiest 4 hours/day) at a 25% discount; trialable and reversible.
- Condition: Finovate signs an amendment with limited waiver language covering historical incidents and accepting standard reversion terms.

I attached a draft concession term sheet (proposed_concession_term.docx). Will need legal to confirm wording and finance to confirm math. Karthik — this is renewal-risky; any commercial input? Marcus — any legal/contract constraints we should be aware of for an ad-hoc credit + capacity promise?

Attachments: proposed_concession_term.docx

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Account Lead — Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 13:40:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Production p99 latency spikes — impact to customers

+1 on Ava's suggested approach. From a commercial perspective:
- Finovate is in late-stage renewal discussions and this is the biggest immediate risk to close.
- A one-month unconditional credit plus the pro-rated offset is the minimum to stabilize the relationship; we should avoid an open-ended blanket concession.
- The dedicated-capacity trial is a strong retention lever (keeps them on Redwood vs. a competitor offering reserved capacity).

Ask for exec approval on the financial cap: recommend capping the concession at $150k total (credit + trial discount) unless leadership signs off on higher. If we can get Legal and Finance to pre-authorize the amendment, I can present to Finovate's renewal committee by EOD tomorrow.

Naomi — please prepare a customer-facing mitigation note summarizing what we'll do operationally and the expected timeline for eliminating the tail spikes. Marcus — if Legal can draft a short amendment with waiver + limited liability language, Karthik needs that to close the loop.

Karthik
SVP, Sales
Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 16:10:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Production p99 latency spikes — impact to customers

I've reviewed the draft concession term sheet. Key legal points / redlines:
1) Credit mechanics: specify that credits apply to future invoices and are non-refundable; include a $150k cap unless signed off by CFO.
2) Trial capacity: call this a "reserved-capacity pilot" for 90 days; include a clause for termination with 7 days' notice and SLA measurement windows.
3) Waiver language: limited release for the incidents described and a carve-out for negligence or gross misconduct — we cannot accept an unlimited indemnity.
4) Signature flow: amendments executed via DocuSign; include an explicit acceptance checkbox for the concession and pilot terms.

I've uploaded a redlined doc to the internal drive and created a DocuSign placeholder link for the amendment. If Sales confirms the $150k cap and Finance greenlights the accounting treatment, Legal is ok to proceed.

Related: please run final numbers on the proposed cap vs. expected churn economics.

Marcus Lin
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Jenkins <laura.jenkins@finovate.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@finovate.com, Carlos Ruiz <c.ruiz@finovate.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 18:02:00 -0400
Subject: Final: Concession & remediation plan — next steps

Laura / Finovate team — thanks for raising this and for the data you shared. We've reviewed the incident and coordinated across Sales, Legal, and Engineering. This is the remediation and concession package Redwood will extend: 

1) Commercial concession
- One (1) month service credit applied to Finovate's next invoice (non-refundable).
- Pro-rated usage credit for the incident days (applied as invoice offset).
- Total concession capped at $150,000 unless mutually agreed in writing by Redwood's CFO.

2) Operational remediation
- Redwood will provision a 90-day reserved-capacity pilot for Finovate's 18:00–22:00 ET peak window. The pilot will include dedicated nodes, monitoring runbooks, and priority ops escalation.
- Engineering will run traffic-shaping rules and increased KV cache retention for the pilot window; we will provide a mitigation playbook within 48 hours.

3) Governance & sign-off
- We will deliver a short amendment via DocuSign for signature (link below). The amendment contains the credit mechanics, pilot terms, and a limited release for the incidents cited.
- Once signed, credits will be applied to the next billing cycle and the pilot will be scheduled within 7 business days.

DocuSign amendment (placeholder): https://docusign.com/placeholder/ABC123-AMEND
Internal deal link: https://hubspot.redwoodinference.com/deal/DEAL-13472

Please confirm acceptance or propose edits. Karthik: I'll coordinate the customer presentation once we have the signed amendment. Marcus: thanks for the redlines; we accept the $150k cap and the waiver language as discussed.

Laura — thanks again. Hoping this reestablishes confidence with your ops + renewals teams.

Best,
Naomi Feldman
Head of Customer Success
Redwood Inference

---
On Tue, Sep 14, 2026 at 18:15 Laura Jenkins <laura.jenkins@finovate.com> wrote:
> Naomi / Ava / Karthik —
>
> Thanks. We appreciate the speed and the clarity. Finovate accepts the package and will sign the amendment; procurement will push the DocuSign to our legal team today.
>
> I'll confirm once countersigned and share any follow-up timelines.
>
> Laura
> Head of Platform Reliability, Finovate Payments

1) Credit mechanics: specify that credits apply to future invoices and are non-refundable; include a $150k cap unless signed off by CFO.
2) Trial capacity: call this a "reserved-capacity pilot" for 90 days; include a clause for termination with 7 days' notice and SLA measurement windows.
3) Waiver language: limited release for the incidents described and a carve-out for negligence or gross misconduct — we cannot accept an unlimited indemnity.
4) Signature flow: amendments executed via DocuSign; include an explicit acceptance checkbox for the concession and pilot terms.

I've uploaded a redlined doc to the internal drive and created a DocuSign placeholder link for the amendment. If Sales confirms the $150k cap and Finance greenlights the accounting treatment, Legal is ok to proceed.

Related: please run final numbers on the proposed cap vs. expected churn economics.

Marcus Lin
Senior Counsel, Contracts
Redwood Inference


From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Jenkins <laura.jenkins@finovate.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@finovate.com, Carlos Ruiz <c.ruiz@finovate.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 18:02:00 -0400
Subject: Final: Concession & remediation plan — next steps

Laura / Finovate team — thanks for raising this and for the data you shared. We've reviewed the incident and coordinated across Sales, Legal, and Engineering. This is the remediation and concession package Redwood will extend: 

1) Commercial concession
- One (1) month service credit applied to Finovate's next invoice (non-refundable).
- Pro-rated usage credit for the incident days (applied as invoice offset).
- Total concession capped at $150,000 unless mutually agreed in writing by Redwood's CFO.

2) Operational remediation
- Redwood will provision a 90-day reserved-capacity pilot for Finovate's 18:00–22:00 ET peak window. The pilot will include dedicated nodes, monitoring runbooks, and priority ops escalation.
- Engineering will run traffic-shaping rules and increased KV cache retention for the pilot window; we will provide a mitigation playbook within 48 hours.

3) Governance & sign-off
- We will deliver a short amendment via DocuSign for signature (link below). The amendment contains the credit mechanics, pilot terms, and a limited release for the incidents cited.
- Once signed, credits will be applied to the next billing cycle and the pilot will be scheduled within 7 business days.

DocuSign amendment (placeholder): https://docusign.com/placeholder/ABC123-AMEND
Internal deal link: https://hubspot.redwoodinference.com/deal/DEAL-13472

Please confirm acceptance or propose edits. Karthik: I'll coordinate the customer presentation once we have the signed amendment. Marcus: thanks for the redlines; we accept the $150k cap and the waiver language as discussed.

Laura — thanks again. Hoping this reestablishes confidence with your ops + renewals teams.

Best,
Naomi Feldman
Head of Customer Success
Redwood Inference

---

> Naomi / Ava / Karthik —
>
> Thanks. We appreciate the speed and the clarity. Finovate accepts the package and will sign the amendment; procurement will push the DocuSign to our legal team today.
>
> I'll confirm once countersigned and share any follow-up timelines.
>
> Laura
> Head of Platform Reliability, Finovate Payments

source.subject: Controls handoff and FedRAMP scope questions
messages:
From: Kelly Tran <ktran@secureaudit.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@boxcorp.com>
Date: 2026-08-30T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: FedRAMP / ISO scope follow-ups for BoxCorp intake

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the intake call this morning. Two quick items we need to lock before the evidence sprint:

1) FedRAMP scope — you mentioned earlier that logging for Dedicated customers is retained in a customer-controlled bucket. Can you confirm whether Redwood will include audit logs for Dedicated vs Hosted in the same evidence set, or should we treat them separately for control sampling?

2) ISO control owner list — could you provide a single spreadsheet with control owners, evidence paths, and expected retention windows (especially for SOC 2 / SIG / CAIQ crosswalks)? If you have a draft, a shared Drive link is fine.

3) Timing — BoxCorp is targeting a production cutoff for new integrations 10/15; we need to know when the control evidence will be available for sampling so we can schedule auditors.

If it's easier, a short mapping (owner, location, link) is perfect.

Best,
Kelly
Kelly Tran
Sr. Auditor, SecureAudit
[Attachment: standard-intake-checklist.pdf]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-08-31T10:07:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: FedRAMP / ISO scope follow-ups for BoxCorp intake

Team — forwarding Kelly's asks (see below). Quick proposed division of labor so we can reply to them tomorrow AM:

- Marcus: confirm how we want to present Dedicated vs Hosted logs (single evidence set with annotations vs separate sampling). Note: Dedicated customers have customer-controlled buckets with KMS; Hosted logs live in our central logging cluster.
- Rachel: assemble the control-owner spreadsheet (owner, slack/email, evidence path, retention) and mark any controls with 3P attestations (SaaS providers we rely on). Start with SOC 2 + ISO crosswalk and then SIG/CAIQ columns.
- Laura: estimate earliest deliverable date for the mapping and a realistic window for auditors to sample (consider our internal blackout windows). Also flag controls that would require engineering time to pull snapshots.

I will draft the reply to Kelly and attach the spreadsheet (we can iterate). Please add comments to the Drive file by EOD so we can send consolidated.

Thanks — Ava
Ava Chen
Head of GTM Security, Redwood Inference
[Internal note: Drive draft created: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1a2bControlsMapping]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kelly Tran <ktran@secureaudit.io>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@boxcorp.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-01T08:34:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FedRAMP / ISO scope follow-ups for BoxCorp intake

Hi Kelly / Sam —

Thanks for the clear asks. Summary below and attached is a first-pass mapping (controls-mapping-ava-v2.xlsx) and a draft FedRAMP scope PDF. Highlights:

1) Logging evidence sets
- Dedicated (customer-managed buckets): we'll provide a consolidated manifest that shows the bucket name, KMS key id, and a pointer to the stored audit logs. For sampling, please treat Dedicated customers separately — we will label evidence as "Dedicated" vs "Hosted" in the mapping so you can select the sample accordingly.

2) Control owner spreadsheet
- Attached spreadsheet includes control owners, primary contact, evidence path (Drive links or S3 manifest), expected retention (months), and whether there is a 3P attestation. Columns include SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / SIG / CAIQ crosswalks. Rachel created comments on rows where we anticipate engineering extraction.

3) Timing for BoxCorp sampling
- Earliest complete evidence handoff: 2026-09-10 for scoped controls that are already instrumented.
- Controls requiring engineering snapshots: target handoff 2026-09-24.
- If you need auditors on site / synchronous sampling windows, propose dates 2026-09-28 to 2026-10-06.

4) Outstanding items for us to confirm
- Laura will confirm any internal blackout windows during the proposed sampling window.
- We will provide a short README in the Drive link explaining the "Hosted" vs "Dedicated" labeling.

Attachments: controls-mapping-ava-v2.xlsx, fedramp-scope-draft.pdf

Let us know if this approach works or if you want the Dedicated/Hosted evidence combined into one manifest (we can do that, but for audit sampling we find separate labeling reduces ambiguity).

Best,
Ava Chen
Head of GTM Security, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: controls-mapping-ava-v2.xlsx]
[Attachment: fedramp-scope-draft.pdf]

From: Kelly Tran <ktran@secureaudit.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@boxcorp.com>
Date: 2026-09-02T15:48:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FedRAMP / ISO scope follow-ups for BoxCorp intake

Ava —

Thanks, this is exactly what we needed. Quick clarifications after I scanned the spreadsheet:

- On control AC-6 (access reviews) the evidence path points to a shared Drive folder but I couldn't find the last quarterly review report. Can you point me to the file name?
- For retention windows, there are a few items with "TBD" — can Rachel flag whether those are policy gaps or simply missing metadata so we can annotate the sample plan.

If you can drop the small updates by 9/07 we can lock the audit schedule for the 9/28 start.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.
Kelly
Sr. Auditor, SecureAudit
notes: Thread contains mixed external + internal coordination around evidence handoff for FedRAMP and ISO mapping.
All internal participants are employees listed under sources/gmail/.
Attachments are referenced as stubs per content rules.
Message ordering is oldest -> newest and includes realistic quoting and short internal action items.

source.subject: HealthNow DPA — signoff path & transfer-impact items
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Julia Ramos <julia.ramos@healthnow.com>
Cc: 
Date: Fri, 28 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Hi Julia,

Thanks again for the call yesterday. Attached are the redlines to our standard DPA (HealthNow_DPA_v3_redlines.docx). Key asks from your side that we discussed: clearer processor/processor-role mapping in Section 4 and confirmation of the SCCs clause alignment.

Two quick items where we'd appreciate direction up front:
- Is HealthNow planning to request any derogations from the standard EU SCC wording or rely on a Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) addendum?
- Do you need a list of Redwood subprocessors + data locality by region, or is monthly visibility sufficient?

Proposed timeline from our side: if we can get back to each other with comments by EOW (Fri 8/28), we can prepare a final version for legal signoff next week. If there are SCC deviations we can escalate.

Attachments: HealthNow_DPA_v3_redlines.docx (docx, 42KB)

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Lead, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0147
From: Julia Ramos <julia.ramos@healthnow.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthNow Legal <legal@healthnow.com>
Date: Fri, 28 Aug 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Ben — thanks for sending. I pulled this into our intake and legal is starting a first pass. A few notes up front:

- We will almost certainly want a TIA attached to any SCCs amendments; legal will ask our privacy team to put that together.
- On subprocessors: we need the current list + proposed cadence for updates (we prefer quarterly but can accept monthly if there is a business need).
- SOC2 evidence: our procurement requires the latest SOC2 Type II report and a summary of encryption at rest/in-transit.

Can we do a quick sync next week (30 min) to walk the redlines? I'm available Mon/Tue 10–2 PT.

I've attached our intake summary (HealthNow_response_notes.pdf).

Regards,
Julia Ramos
Sr. Counsel — Privacy & Data Protection
HealthNow
julia.ramos@healthnow.com
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Fri, 28 Aug 2026 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Forwarding HealthNow's note and the redlines they returned. Julia wants to confirm TIA expectations and SOC2 evidence. High level: client is comfortable with monthly subprocessor visibility but legal flagged SCCs/TIA as a potential blocker.

Asks:
- Karthik / Laura: quick legal read — any deal-breaker redlines in our doc? Specifically see my comments on Section 4 (processor roles) and Annex II (SCCs modifications).
- Marissa: can you draft a short TIA summary we can share with HealthNow? They said legal will want it attached before signoff.
- Marcus: confirm whether monthly subprocessor updates are fine commercially and how we reflect that in the order form.

Attachments forwarded: HealthNow_DPA_v3_redlines.docx, HealthNow_response_notes.pdf

Thanks — Ben
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 28 Aug 2026 14:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Thanks Ben — quick read and below are our priority items. I reviewed the redline doc and prepared a short summary (proposed_redline_summary.docx).

Priority/legal position (top three):
1) SCCs: HealthNow proposed language in Annex II expands downstream obligations and adds audit rights that materially exceed our standard SCCs. We should propose the standard SCCs + a scoped addendum tying any additional audit to reasonable notice/periodicity. If they insist on broader audit rights, escalate to Head of Legal.
2) Processor role delegation: The client's edits imply we accept joint-controller language in a few paragraphs — we need to preserve Processor status. Adjust proposed clause 4.2 to clarify processing purpose and controller-controller responsibilities.
3) Export/transfer carve-outs: there's a new clause requesting explicit data-export guarantees. We can commit to reasonable safeguards and a TIA, but cannot accept unconditional guarantees (risk/operational constraints).

Action items:
- Marissa: prepare a 1-pg TIA summary and a short list of current subprocessors + region (sensitive columns redacted). Put SCCs risk entries in SCCs_risk_log.xlsx.
- Laura: please prepare final suggested clause text for 4.2 and Annex II compromise language for client review.
- Marcus: confirm how we record the subprocessor cadence in the order form (monthly vs quarterly) and any pricing impact.

If HealthNow refuses Annex II compromise we may need to offer a two-stage signoff (DPA with SCCs in principle + TIA to be attached within 30 days). Let's aim to have our recommended text by Monday EOD.

Karthik
General Counsel, Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 29 Aug 2026 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Attached is the requested TIA summary and the initial SCCs risk log (SCCs_risk_log.xlsx). Key takeaways from the TIA draft:

- Transfer scope: most EU-origin datasets are pseudonymized before processing; limited exports to non-EU subprocessors for inference caching.
- Residual risk: medium due to third-country subprocessors in the region; mitigation: standard SCCs + AES-256 encryption in transit/rest + contractual subprocessor commitments.
- Recommended client deliverables: 1-pg TIA summary (attached), monthly subprocessor register export (CSV), and SOC2 Type II.

I've redacted specific subprocessor hostnames in the spreadsheet pending commercial review, but include region and service category. Let me know if you want me to surface specific hostnames to HealthNow — that'll require a confidentiality addendum.

Attachments: SCCs_risk_log.xlsx, TIA_onepager.pdf

Marissa Cole
Privacy & Compliance Manager
Redwood Inference
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Julia Ramos <julia.ramos@healthnow.com>, HealthNow Legal <legal@healthnow.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 01 Sep 2026 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: HealthNow DPA — signoff path & transfer-impact items

Julia — looping you back in with internal guidance and a suggested path to finalize:

Proposed action plan:
1) Redwood will provide the redline + proposed compromise text for clause 4.2 and Annex II by Mon 9/6 EOD (Laura + Karthik to deliver).
2) Marissa to attach the 1-pg TIA summary and SCCs risk log (attached).
3) We commit to monthly subprocessor register updates; commercially, Marcus confirms no pricing delta for this cadence.
4) SOC2 Type II and encryption summary will be provided in parallel.
5) If HealthNow requires SCCs deviations beyond the compromise language we will escalate to Head of Legal for final signoff and may propose a two-step execution (execute DPA subject to TIA acceptance within 30 days).

Next steps: please confirm a 30-min call slot for Tues 9/7 or Wed 9/8 to walk the compromise language and confirm signatory path. If acceptable, we'll send a DocuSign envelope as soon as redlines are agreed.

Action items assigned in-thread: Laura (clause text), Karthik (final review), Marissa (TIA + SCCs log), Marcus (order form cadence).

DocuSign preview (will be used after signoff): https://www.docusign.net/Signing/?s=ABC123xyz

Thanks — looking forward to closing this quickly.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Lead, Strategic Accounts

title: HealthNow DPA — signoff path & transfer-impact items
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Julia Ramos <julia.ramos@healthnow.com>
Cc: 
Date: Fri, 28 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Hi Julia,

Thanks again for the call yesterday. Attached are the redlines to our standard DPA (HealthNow_DPA_v3_redlines.docx). Key asks from your side that we discussed: clearer processor/processor-role mapping in Section 4 and confirmation of the SCCs clause alignment.

Two quick items where we'd appreciate direction up front:
- Is HealthNow planning to request any derogations from the standard EU SCC wording or rely on a Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) addendum?
- Do you need a list of Redwood subprocessors + data locality by region, or is monthly visibility sufficient?

Proposed timeline from our side: if we can get back to each other with comments by EOW (Fri 8/28), we can prepare a final version for legal signoff next week. If there are SCC deviations we can escalate.

Attachments: HealthNow_DPA_v3_redlines.docx (docx, 42KB)

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Lead, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0147


From: Julia Ramos <julia.ramos@healthnow.com>

Cc: HealthNow Legal <legal@healthnow.com>

Subject: Re: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Ben — thanks for sending. I pulled this into our intake and legal is starting a first pass. A few notes up front:

- We will almost certainly want a TIA attached to any SCCs amendments; legal will ask our privacy team to put that together.
- On subprocessors: we need the current list + proposed cadence for updates (we prefer quarterly but can accept monthly if there is a business need).
- SOC2 evidence: our procurement requires the latest SOC2 Type II report and a summary of encryption at rest/in-transit.

Can we do a quick sync next week (30 min) to walk the redlines? I'm available Mon/Tue 10–2 PT.

I've attached our intake summary (HealthNow_response_notes.pdf).

Regards,
Julia Ramos
Sr. Counsel — Privacy & Data Protection
HealthNow

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Fri, 28 Aug 2026 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Forwarding HealthNow's note and the redlines they returned. Julia wants to confirm TIA expectations and SOC2 evidence. High level: client is comfortable with monthly subprocessor visibility but legal flagged SCCs/TIA as a potential blocker.

Asks:
- Karthik / Laura: quick legal read — any deal-breaker redlines in our doc? Specifically see my comments on Section 4 (processor roles) and Annex II (SCCs modifications).
- Marissa: can you draft a short TIA summary we can share with HealthNow? They said legal will want it attached before signoff.
- Marcus: confirm whether monthly subprocessor updates are fine commercially and how we reflect that in the order form.

Attachments forwarded: HealthNow_DPA_v3_redlines.docx, HealthNow_response_notes.pdf

Thanks — Ben


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 28 Aug 2026 14:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Thanks Ben — quick read and below are our priority items. I reviewed the redline doc and prepared a short summary (proposed_redline_summary.docx).

Priority/legal position (top three):
1) SCCs: HealthNow proposed language in Annex II expands downstream obligations and adds audit rights that materially exceed our standard SCCs. We should propose the standard SCCs + a scoped addendum tying any additional audit to reasonable notice/periodicity. If they insist on broader audit rights, escalate to Head of Legal.
2) Processor role delegation: The client's edits imply we accept joint-controller language in a few paragraphs — we need to preserve Processor status. Adjust proposed clause 4.2 to clarify processing purpose and controller-controller responsibilities.
3) Export/transfer carve-outs: there's a new clause requesting explicit data-export guarantees. We can commit to reasonable safeguards and a TIA, but cannot accept unconditional guarantees (risk/operational constraints).

Action items:
- Marissa: prepare a 1-pg TIA summary and a short list of current subprocessors + region (sensitive columns redacted). Put SCCs risk entries in SCCs_risk_log.xlsx.
- Laura: please prepare final suggested clause text for 4.2 and Annex II compromise language for client review.
- Marcus: confirm how we record the subprocessor cadence in the order form (monthly vs quarterly) and any pricing impact.

If HealthNow refuses Annex II compromise we may need to offer a two-stage signoff (DPA with SCCs in principle + TIA to be attached within 30 days). Let's aim to have our recommended text by Monday EOD.

Karthik
General Counsel, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 29 Aug 2026 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: HealthNow DPA redlines — submitted for review

Attached is the requested TIA summary and the initial SCCs risk log (SCCs_risk_log.xlsx). Key takeaways from the TIA draft:

- Transfer scope: most EU-origin datasets are pseudonymized before processing; limited exports to non-EU subprocessors for inference caching.
- Residual risk: medium due to third-country subprocessors in the region; mitigation: standard SCCs + AES-256 encryption in transit/rest + contractual subprocessor commitments.
- Recommended client deliverables: 1-pg TIA summary (attached), monthly subprocessor register export (CSV), and SOC2 Type II.

I've redacted specific subprocessor hostnames in the spreadsheet pending commercial review, but include region and service category. Let me know if you want me to surface specific hostnames to HealthNow — that'll require a confidentiality addendum.

Attachments: SCCs_risk_log.xlsx, TIA_onepager.pdf

Marissa Cole
Privacy & Compliance Manager
Redwood Inference

Julia — looping you back in with internal guidance and a suggested path to finalize:

Proposed action plan:
1) Redwood will provide the redline + proposed compromise text for clause 4.2 and Annex II by Mon 9/6 EOD (Laura + Karthik to deliver).
2) Marissa to attach the 1-pg TIA summary and SCCs risk log (attached).
3) We commit to monthly subprocessor register updates; commercially, Marcus confirms no pricing delta for this cadence.
4) SOC2 Type II and encryption summary will be provided in parallel.
5) If HealthNow requires SCCs deviations beyond the compromise language we will escalate to Head of Legal for final signoff and may propose a two-step execution (execute DPA subject to TIA acceptance within 30 days).

Next steps: please confirm a 30-min call slot for Tues 9/7 or Wed 9/8 to walk the compromise language and confirm signatory path. If acceptable, we'll send a DocuSign envelope as soon as redlines are agreed.

source.subject: MedTechLabs demo — streaming model shortlist & UX clarifications
messages:
From: Hannah Schultz <hannah.schultz@medtechlabs.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up from this morning's demo — streaming concerns and next info

Hi Avery,

Thanks again for the demo today — Tom and I appreciated seeing the streaming preview and the low-latency routing examples. A quick recap from our side and a few asks so we can scope an internal POC: 

- Primary goal: real-time transcription/summary assistant embedded in our clinician dashboard (avg prompt length ~80-200 tokens, median 150).
- Observed issues during the demo: partial output coherence when the model emitted early stop tokens (we saw truncated sentences and stray punctuation) and a few moments where the stream lagged by ~800ms under burst loads.
- Security: any PII/PHI handling notes? We'll need KMS/HSM integration and SOC 2 evidence as part of procurement.

Could you share: 
1) A recommended shortlist of models you think fit low-latency streaming for this workload (we care about coherence under incremental decoding).
2) Any benchmark links or a short report showing latency and partial-output quality for those models (especially under variable seq lengths).
3) Example prompt sets and stop-sequence guidance you recommend for clinician-facing output.

Tom and I are aiming to get a short internal note to procurement by next Thursday (Sept 2). If you can send the shortlist + benchmark links and a sample promptset this week, that would be perfect.

Thanks —
Hannah Schultz
Product Manager, Clinical Tools
MedTechLabs

---

Note: attachment referenced on call: "medtech_streaming_requirements.pdf" (we'll attach our internal requirements doc after procurement loop).

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schultz <hannah.schultz@medtechlabs.com>, Tom Reyes <tom.reyes@medtechlabs.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-28T12:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up from this morning's demo — streaming concerns and next info

Hi Hannah/Tom — great to meet you both. Short reply now, fuller package attached by EOD. Quick bullets:

- Model shortlist: two low-latency variants and one fallback high-qual model for edge cases. I'll attach a PDF that maps model tradeoffs (latency vs. partial-output coherence).
- Benchmarks: we ran micro-benchmarks with seq lengths 64/128/256 and observed the streaming artifacts you mentioned when models were quantized beyond int8 for short prefixes; I'll include the CSV.
- Prompt sets: attaching sample_promptset_v1.txt with stop-sequence recommendations (clinician-friendly termination tokens and soft-stop heuristics).
- Security & procurement: SOC 2 attestation and KMS integration notes are on the compliance page I'll link.

Next steps I propose:
1) I’ll send the shortlist + benchmark summary (attached).
2) We schedule a 30min technical sync with Marcus (runtime) and Lauren (solutions) to review the POC constraints and define a 2-week trial scope. Are mornings next week OK?

Attaching: medtech_model_shortlist.pdf; sample_promptset_v1.txt

Best,
Avery Johnson
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: medtech_model_shortlist.pdf (PDF, 120KB), sample_promptset_v1.txt (text, 8KB)

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-31T10:30:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: MedTechLabs — shortlist + streaming UX notes (prep for tech sync)

Team — internal notes ahead of the technical sync with MedTechLabs (tentatively next Tue). High-level findings from the demo and our recommended actions: 

Context: clinician dashboard use-case, median prompt ~150 tokens, tolerance for tail latency <= 200ms. Prospect flagged partial sentence truncation when stop-sequences triggered during streaming; also observed ~700-900ms lag during bursts on the demo rig.

Recommended model shortlist (prioritized):
- redwood-lite-100M-stream (low-latency, optimized kernels) — best for 1st-pass streaming, strong prefix coherence when configured with token-level speculative decoding.
- redwood-2-7b-q4f16 (balanced) — better long-form coherence but ~1.5x token cost; good as a canary fallback when lite model fails quality checks.
- redwood-4-14b (fallback) — highest quality, higher latency/cost; use for post-session summary generation (non-streamed).

Key UX/regression observations and mitigations:
- Partial output coherence: often appears when decoding stops prematurely due to aggressive stop tokens or when quantized kernels mis-handle soft-stop heuristics. Mitigation: tune stop-sequence list to prefer sentence-final punctuation and add a 50-token soft buffer before enforcing hard stops for streaming.
- Speculative decoding: enable 1-step speculative decoding to fill in token gaps for prefix outputs — improves perceived coherence with <10% latency overhead.
- KV cache warmup & prefix caching: warm the KV cache with the clinician prompt template; this reduces initial-token jitter.
- Batching policy: use continuous small-batching (max batch size 4, time window 20ms) to balance latency and throughput for their expected traffic profile.

Benchmarks attached: redwood_streaming_benchmarks_aug26.csv — contains latency p50/p95, partial-output coherence scores (custom metric), and token-cost estimates across seq lengths 64/128/256. Prompt sets: prompt_sets_medtech_v1.json (internal) — includes clinician phrasing, stop sequence candidates, and evaluation prompts.

Ask: Lauren/Marcus please review the prompt set and the stop-sequence heuristics; Marcus, can you prepare a 20-min runbook we can walk MedTech through showing how to toggle speculative decoding and prefix caching in Redwood Console?

Thanks —
Avery

Attachments: redwood_streaming_benchmarks_aug26.csv, prompt_sets_medtech_v1.json

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schultz <hannah.schultz@medtechlabs.com>, Tom Reyes <tom.reyes@medtechlabs.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-01T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MedTechLabs demo — streaming model shortlist & UX clarifications

Hi Hannah/Tom — attaching the curated materials per your procurement timeline and next steps to push to POC. TL;DR and proposed plan below.

TL;DR (what we recommend for your clinician dashboard):
- Primary streaming model: redwood-lite-100M-stream (low cost, sub-100ms p50; best perceived coherence when paired with 1-step speculative decoding).
- Fallback for edge cases: redwood-2-7b-q4f16 (better continuity across multi-sentence outputs). Use this for on-demand non-streamed summaries.
- Stop-seq guidance: use the attached sample_promptset_v1.txt recommendations — prefer explicit sentence-final tokens and a 50-token soft buffer to avoid truncation mid-sentence.

Attachments (for procurement): medtech_model_shortlist.pdf, sample_promptset_v1.txt
Supporting internal artifacts (for your engineers): link to benchmarks: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-redwood-benchmarks (contains the CSV we referenced), and a short runbook for toggling speculative decoding in Redwood Console: https://docs.redwood.ai/streaming-best-practices#speculative-decoding

Proposed next steps: 
1) Quick tech sync (30min) — objectives: confirm latency targets, confirm KMS/HSM constraints, agree on trial data handling. Morning slots next Tue/Wed work for us; please suggest a time.
2) 2-week trial: we’ll provision a small Dedicated pool and pre-configure the primary model + fallbacks; we’ll include automated guardrails that roll to fallback model on coherence regressions.
3) Procurement packet: we’ll provide SOC 2 attestation + KMS integration notes once we have procurement contact details.

If you want, we can also run a short streaming eval using a subset of your clinician prompts (anonymized) to produce a short report showing p50/p95 latency and a partial-output coherence score — turnaround ~3 business days.

Looking forward —
Avery Johnson
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: medtech_model_shortlist.pdf (PDF, 120KB), sample_promptset_v1.txt (text, 8KB)
