- redwood-2-7b-q4f16 (balanced) — better long-form coherence but ~1.5x token cost; good as a canary fallback when lite model fails quality checks.
- redwood-4-14b (fallback) — highest quality, higher latency/cost; use for post-session summary generation (non-streamed).

Key UX/regression observations and mitigations:
- Partial output coherence: often appears when decoding stops prematurely due to aggressive stop tokens or when quantized kernels mis-handle soft-stop heuristics. Mitigation: tune stop-sequence list to prefer sentence-final punctuation and add a 50-token soft buffer before enforcing hard stops for streaming.
- Speculative decoding: enable 1-step speculative decoding to fill in token gaps for prefix outputs — improves perceived coherence with <10% latency overhead.
- KV cache warmup & prefix caching: warm the KV cache with the clinician prompt template; this reduces initial-token jitter.
- Batching policy: use continuous small-batching (max batch size 4, time window 20ms) to balance latency and throughput for their expected traffic profile.

Benchmarks attached: redwood_streaming_benchmarks_aug26.csv — contains latency p50/p95, partial-output coherence scores (custom metric), and token-cost estimates across seq lengths 64/128/256. Prompt sets: prompt_sets_medtech_v1.json (internal) — includes clinician phrasing, stop sequence candidates, and evaluation prompts.

Ask: Lauren/Marcus please review the prompt set and the stop-sequence heuristics; Marcus, can you prepare a 20-min runbook we can walk MedTech through showing how to toggle speculative decoding and prefix caching in Redwood Console?

Thanks —
Avery

Attachments: redwood_streaming_benchmarks_aug26.csv, prompt_sets_medtech_v1.json

From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schultz <hannah.schultz@medtechlabs.com>, Tom Reyes <tom.reyes@medtechlabs.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-01T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MedTechLabs demo — streaming model shortlist & UX clarifications

Hi Hannah/Tom — attaching the curated materials per your procurement timeline and next steps to push to POC. TL;DR and proposed plan below.

TL;DR (what we recommend for your clinician dashboard):
- Primary streaming model: redwood-lite-100M-stream (low cost, sub-100ms p50; best perceived coherence when paired with 1-step speculative decoding).
- Fallback for edge cases: redwood-2-7b-q4f16 (better continuity across multi-sentence outputs). Use this for on-demand non-streamed summaries.
- Stop-seq guidance: use the attached sample_promptset_v1.txt recommendations — prefer explicit sentence-final tokens and a 50-token soft buffer to avoid truncation mid-sentence.

Attachments (for procurement): medtech_model_shortlist.pdf, sample_promptset_v1.txt
Supporting internal artifacts (for your engineers): link to benchmarks: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-redwood-benchmarks (contains the CSV we referenced), and a short runbook for toggling speculative decoding in Redwood Console: https://docs.redwood.ai/streaming-best-practices#speculative-decoding

Proposed next steps: 
1) Quick tech sync (30min) — objectives: confirm latency targets, confirm KMS/HSM constraints, agree on trial data handling. Morning slots next Tue/Wed work for us; please suggest a time.
2) 2-week trial: we’ll provision a small Dedicated pool and pre-configure the primary model + fallbacks; we’ll include automated guardrails that roll to fallback model on coherence regressions.
3) Procurement packet: we’ll provide SOC 2 attestation + KMS integration notes once we have procurement contact details.

If you want, we can also run a short streaming eval using a subset of your clinician prompts (anonymized) to produce a short report showing p50/p95 latency and a partial-output coherence score — turnaround ~3 business days.

Looking forward —
Avery Johnson
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142

To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Follow-up from this morning's demo — streaming concerns and next info

Hi Avery,

Thanks again for the demo today — Tom and I appreciated seeing the streaming preview and the low-latency routing examples. A quick recap from our side and a few asks so we can scope an internal POC: 

- Primary goal: real-time transcription/summary assistant embedded in our clinician dashboard (avg prompt length ~80-200 tokens, median 150).
- Observed issues during the demo: partial output coherence when the model emitted early stop tokens (we saw truncated sentences and stray punctuation) and a few moments where the stream lagged by ~800ms under burst loads.
- Security: any PII/PHI handling notes? We'll need KMS/HSM integration and SOC 2 evidence as part of procurement.

Could you share: 
1) A recommended shortlist of models you think fit low-latency streaming for this workload (we care about coherence under incremental decoding).
2) Any benchmark links or a short report showing latency and partial-output quality for those models (especially under variable seq lengths).
3) Example prompt sets and stop-sequence guidance you recommend for clinician-facing output.

Tom and I are aiming to get a short internal note to procurement by next Thursday (Sept 2). If you can send the shortlist + benchmark links and a sample promptset this week, that would be perfect.

Thanks —
Hannah Schultz
Product Manager, Clinical Tools
MedTechLabs

---

Note: attachment referenced on call: "medtech_streaming_requirements.pdf" (we'll attach our internal requirements doc after procurement loop).

source.subject: Vendor onboarding kickoff — BrightLeaf legal & remit collection
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha@brightleaf.co>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-30 09:12 PDT
Subject: Vendor onboarding kickoff — BrightLeaf legal & remit collection

Hi Samantha —

Great to e-meet you. Per our procurement intake call, I wanted to kick off formal collection of the items our finance and legal teams need to get BrightLeaf set up as an approved supplier and to enable POs and electronic payments. To keep this tidy, below is a checklist of what we typically need to complete vendor activation and PO routing: 

- Legal entity full name and DBA (if applicable)
- W-9 (or applicable tax form for non-US entities) with EIN/Tax ID clearly visible
- Remit-to address (street, city, state, zip)
- Bank details for ACH and wire (bank name, account number, routing / IBAN, SWIFT) and confirmation if ACH is allowed for initial payments
- Copy of bank letter or bank-originated voided check for account verification (preferred for wires/ACH)
- Preferred invoice receipt email and invoice formatting requirements (PO number field)
- Any payment terms requested (we typically operate net-30; note early-pay discount requests up front)
- Certificate of insurance (COI) if available — our legal team will advise if required for the engagement

If you can share the W-9 and bank verification PDF, we can start the vendor record in our ERP and issue an initial PO template for signature. Attaching the PO template we’ll use for reference.

Timeline/asks: would you be able to upload the items above by EOD Friday (9/3)? If not, please share an ETA and any blockers. I’ll own coordinating with Marcus (procurement) and Neha (finance) for any follow-ups.

Thanks —
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0182
Redwood Inference

Attachments: PO-template-v1.pdf

From: Samantha Cole <samantha@brightleaf.co>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-30 12:46 PDT
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding kickoff — BrightLeaf legal & remit collection

Thanks Aditya —

Appreciate the clear checklist. Quick answers up front: BrightLeaf Logistics is a US registered LLC (BrightLeaf Logistics LLC). I’ve attached our completed W-9 and our bank wire instructions PDF. We can accept ACH and wire; prefer ACH for smaller invoices. Payment terms: standard net-30, we can consider a 1%/10 net-30 early-pay if that helps.

Attachments: BrightLeaf_W9.pdf, BrightLeaf_wire_instructions.pdf

Samantha Cole
Head of Finance Operations
BrightLeaf Logistics
samantha@brightleaf.co | +1 617-555-1222

On Mon, Aug 30, 2026 at 9:12 AM Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [original message]

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samantha Cole <samantha@brightleaf.co>
Date: 2026-08-31 08:03 PDT
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding kickoff — BrightLeaf legal & remit collection

Thanks — I reviewed the W-9 and wire doc Samantha sent. A couple of procurement items before we create the vendor code and issue the PO: 

1) Legal name on W-9 (BrightLeaf Logistics LLC) matches the bank letter? Samantha, can you confirm the bank account is held under the LLC name and not a parent company? If bank letter is in a different name we’ll need an ownership explanation.

2) Can we get a scanned bank letter or voided check for ACH verification? The wire instructions are fine for now, but ACH onboarding requires bank confirmation.

3) Please confirm remit-to address (we received city/state but need full street address) — that’s what populates on the PO and our AP system.

4) Any tax-exempt status or special invoices we should be aware of?

Assignee: Aditya, can you request the bank letter + full remit address and confirm the PO billing contact? I’ll prepare the vendor setup form upon receipt and route for approvals.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Procurement Lead, Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.ai

From: Samantha Cole <samantha@brightleaf.co>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-31 11:22 PDT
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding kickoff — BrightLeaf legal & remit collection

Marcus —

Confirming bank account is BrightLeaf Logistics LLC. I’ve requested a bank letter and should have a PDF from our bank by tomorrow. Full remit-to address is: 218 Riverbend Drive, Suite 4, Cambridge, MA 02139. No tax-exempt status.

Also, for invoicing, please send PO numbers to invoices@brightleaf.co and note that line-item descriptions should include shipping/handling separately.

Samantha

On Tue, Aug 31, 2026 at 8:03 AM Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [procurement items]

From: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Samantha Cole <samantha@brightleaf.co>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-01 15:03 PDT
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding kickoff — BrightLeaf legal & remit collection

Quick update from finance — thanks for sending the W-9 and wire instructions. Once we have the bank letter (or voided check) we can complete the ACH verification and create the vendor payment profile in our ERP. Next steps on our side:

- I’ll create the vendor record and set payment terms to Net-30 (unless you request otherwise in writing).
- We’ll generate a test $1 ACH deposit for verification (standard process) — Samantha, please confirm acceptance of a micro-deposit test.
- Once verified we’ll enable ACH and add the remit-to address to the vendor card.

Aditya — can you confirm Samantha will accept the micro-deposit test? I’ll also attach the PO once the vendor record is active.

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
Finance Manager, Redwood Inference
neha@redwood.ai

Attachments: (none)

source.subject: Facilitator pocket playbook + deck ownership and on-site handoff
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Julian Park <julian.park@eventops.co>
Date: Sat, 29 Aug 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Facilitator pocket playbook + deck ownership and on-site handoff

Team — putting this thread together so we have a single source of truth for what we hand the Lumina facilitators on arrival. Goal: concise pocket playbook (one pager + appendix), clear deck ownership per session, and a simple asset handoff plan for onsite AV/event ops.

Quick asks:
- Lauren: can you confirm final session owners and who will introduce each block? I want a single name per session.
- Marcus: can you convert the current slide set into a presenter-friendly version (notes + two-slide backup) and drop into the shared Drive by EOD Monday?
- Kimberly: please produce the presenter-asset-checklist CSV (laptop model, preferred resolution, A/V notes, arrival ETA) for the Lumina speakers. Julian / EventOps — can you confirm the earliest time you can meet the facilitator leads for a handoff?

I attached a first-draft pocket playbook and the latest deck export. Use the Drive link below for source material. We should plan a 20-minute dry run for each facilitator the evening before Day 1 — suggest 4:30–7pm onsite.

Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Field Events | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: facilitator-pocket-playbook-v1.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB)]
[Attachment: exec-day-1-deck-v3.pptx (PPTX, 18MB)]

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-pocket-playbook
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Jennings <sarah.j@lumina.com>, Julian Park <julian.park@eventops.co>
Date: Sat, 29 Aug 2026 10:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Facilitator pocket playbook + deck ownership and on-site handoff

Ben — thanks for pulling this together. Quick ownership notes below (one name per session as requested):
- 09:00–10:30 Kickoff & Context: Lauren Bishop (I will do the framing + intros)
- 10:45–12:00 Product Deep Dive: Marcus Lin (Marcus will lead, with Sarah J. moderating Q&A)
- 13:30–15:00 Hands-on Workshop: Kimberly Park (owns exercises and timing)
- 15:15–16:30 Executive Q&A: Ben Carter (I can intro Ben)

Marcus — if you can produce the presenter deck with notes by Monday EOD that would be ideal. I will collect speaker bios and 2-line headshots from Lumina (Sarah, can you send final bios by Monday?).

Julian — 4:30pm works for EventOps to do facilitator handoffs on Day 0; please confirm meeting room and AV tech name so we can include in the pocket playbook.

—Lauren
Lauren Bishop
Sr. Customer-Facing Program Manager | Redwood Inference
From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Julian Park <julian.park@eventops.co>
Date: Mon, 31 Aug 2026 09:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Facilitator pocket playbook + deck ownership and on-site handoff

On Mon, Aug 31, 2026 at 9:03 AM Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com> wrote:
> Marcus — if you can produce the presenter deck with notes by Monday EOD that would be ideal.

Looping back — I pulled the slides into presenter mode, trimmed content-heavy slides, and added a two-slide fallback for the Product Deep Dive. I uploaded three files to the Drive: exec-day-1-deck-v3-presenter.pptx, exec-day-1-deck-v3-presenter-notes.pdf, and presenter-asset-checklist.csv. The checklist includes laptop os, HDMI adapter required, and mic preference.

Julian — I flagged two rooms that need DVI-to-HDMI adapters; please confirm you have adapters on site or if we need to ship.

Files attached here as stubs; full versions in the Drive link Ben shared.
[Attachment: exec-day-1-deck-v3-presenter.pptx (PPTX, 14MB)]
[Attachment: exec-day-1-deck-v3-presenter-notes.pdf (PDF, 2.1MB)]
[Attachment: presenter-asset-checklist.csv (CSV, 4KB)]

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Product Marketing Manager | Redwood Inference
From: Julian Park <julian.park@eventops.co>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Jennings <sarah.j@lumina.com>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2026 17:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Facilitator pocket playbook + deck ownership and on-site handoff

Ben / team — EventOps confirmed:
- Facility contact: Marco Rojas (marco@eventops.co) onsite from 3:00pm Day 0.
- AV tech: Lisa Huang (will be onsite for both day run and dry runs).
- We have adapters (HDMI, USB-C to HDMI) for all rooms; DVI adapter is not standard but we can bring two if you confirm which rooms need them.
- We recommend a 25-minute walk-through per facilitator (10 min AV checklist, 10 min run of slides, 5 min logistics). We have a 1:1 room available at 4:30–7:30pm Day 0.

Handoff plan suggestion (short and actionable for the pocket playbook):
1) 15:30 — AV tech meets facilitator; quick mics check.
2) 15:45 — Facilitator gets pocket playbook + USB with presenter deck.
3) 16:00 — 20-minute run-through with backup slide check.
4) 16:30 — Confirm on-call contact (we will provide 24/7 event phone).

I will drop a PDF of the handoff checklist into the Drive by EOD and copy you on access links. Let me know which rooms need DVI adapters.

—Julian
Julian Park
Director, Client Events | EventOps
[Attachment: eventops-handoff-checklist.pdf (PDF, 220KB)]

source.subject: Embedding & rerank — index scale, SLO targets, and proposed playbook
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Tom Reynolds <tom@searchlytics.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 30 Aug 2026 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Embedding & rerank — index scale, SLO targets, and proposed playbook

Hi Tom / Ivy,

Thanks again for the time today — good discussion. This thread captures the quick notes I promised and a pragmatic set of next steps so your engineering and product teams can scope the work. If anything below is off, flag it and I’ll adjust.

Short summary of the problem you described:
- SearchLytics has a primary product search index of ~180M documents and a secondary fresh index of ~7M documents for recency queries.
- Current semantic layer uses a managed vector DB with 1536-d embeddings but most queries need sub-50ms p95 latency for interactive UX.
- Monthly query volume averages ~45M embeddings lookups with strong daily and weekly burst patterns tied to campaigns.
- Cost is a material concern: you target a 3–4x reduction in per-query cost compared to current managed endpoint spend.

Success criteria we discussed:
- p95 end-to-end rerank latency <= 60ms for short queries in us-west region (target 40–50ms ideally)
- 99th percentile tail for heavy bursts not to exceed 200ms with graceful fallbacks
- Unit cost per query reduced by at least 50% in steady-state, with predictable cost under bursts
- Accurate top-5 relevance for rerank (you’ll share a small eval set)

Current stack you shared:
- Primary store: Postgres for metadata + Elasticsearch for inverted retrieval
- Semantic store: Managed vector DB (proprietary) for dense vectors, 1536 dimensions
- Orchestration: Kubernetes + Airflow for batch offline embedding updates

Key technical constraints:
- Index size and update cadence: full re-embed monthly, incremental embedding of hot docs hourly (approx 20k/hr)
- Data residency: all inference traffic must remain in us-west cloud region; audit logging required for queries hitting PII buckets
- Tooling preference: your infra team prefers infrastructure we can operate via IaC and tight observability hooks

Attachments: [attachment: search-query-samples.csv (csv)]

Proposed immediate next steps (owner and timing):
1) Action: Send a 500–1k query eval set + labels (you) — target: Tue 2026-09-02
2) Action: Redwood to run a quick cost/latency projection for two candidate architectures (vector DB + Redwood hosted variant) and share a 1-page playbook — owner: Aditya — ETA: Fri 2026-09-04
3) Action: Technical deep-dive with your infra + data engineering (30–45m) to validate update patterns and auth constraints — owner: Lauren + Marcus — target week of 9/7
4) Action: Draft SLA and fallback policy options for spikes (Aditya) — ETA: 9/11 (attached draft SLA v1)

If that timeline looks good I’ll start the projection and book the deep-dive. Appreciate you walking through the traffic profile — gives us confidence we can hit latency targets with a hybrid strategy (shard-by-tenancy + hot-cache + quantized models).

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Solutions
Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com

From: Tom Reynolds <tom@searchlytics.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Ivy Chen <ivy@searchlytics.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 30 Aug 2026 16:00:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Embedding & rerank — index scale, SLO targets, and proposed playbook

Aditya — thanks for capturing this. A few clarifications and confirmations below: 

- Index numbers correct: 180M cold + 7M hot. Hot docs get frequent re-embeds from streaming updates.
- For latency, 60ms p95 is a hard bar for the primary UX; 40–50ms would be excellent but we need validation under real burst traces.
- Cost target: we want to get to ~50% of current vector-hosting spend on identical query-shaped workloads; we’re comfortable with a short POC that shows realistic throughput.
- Auth/audit: queries that touch the PII bucket must include a token that we can map back to an internal user ID; logs must be retained for 90 days.

We’ll upload the labelled eval set and a day-of-week traffic sample to the Drive link you shared by Tue.

One ask: when you do the projection, can you include a fallback latency/cost column for the option where we keep the managed vector DB as a fallback (no data movement for 5% of queries)? That will help Product signoff.

Thanks — Tom
Tom Reynolds
Head of Product
SearchLytics

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Tom Reynolds <tom@searchlytics.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 31 Aug 2026 09:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Embedding & rerank — index scale, SLO targets, and proposed playbook

Appreciate the fast turn on the clarifications — helpful. Quick updates and commitments from our side: 

1) Evaluation artifacts
- Please drop the labelled eval set and the Day-of-Week traffic CSV into the Drive folder by Tue 2026-09-02. I’ll confirm receipt and sanity-check volumes.

2) Redwood projections and playbook (deliverable):
- What you’ll get: a 1-page architecture comparison (hybrid sharded vector DB with local hot-cache vs Redwood-hosted runner + shard), a table with expected p50/p95 latency estimates for each option, and a cost projection for steady-state + 95th-percentile burst. I’ll add a fallback-row that keeps the managed DB for 5% of traffic as requested.
- Attachments: SLA-draft-v1.pdf (initial wording on SLOs, logging, and retention) — please review and call out any hard stops.
- ETA: Fri 2026-09-04 EOD.

3) Technical deep-dive scheduling
- Lauren and Marcus will run the 45m session. Proposed windows: 2026-09-08 10:00–11:00 PDT or 2026-09-09 13:00–14:00 PDT. Marcus will reach out with a calendar invite once you confirm preferred slot.

4) POC proposal (optional, next phase)
- A small POC we typically run: ingest a 5–10M shard subset, run live QPS at your burst multiplier (x3) to validate tail behavior, and provide perf counters + cost bake-off. If Product wants this, we can scope in the deep-dive.

Next concrete asks for you to unblock us:
- Upload eval set + traffic sample by 2026-09-02
- Confirm which deep-dive slot you prefer by 2026-09-03

If you approve I’ll send an invite and start the projection work.

Regards,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Solutions — Redwood Inference
[attachment: SLA-draft-v1.pdf (pdf)]

- A small POC we typically run: ingest a 5–10M shard subset, run live QPS at your burst multiplier (x3) to validate tail behavior, and provide perf counters + cost bake-off. If Product wants this, we can scope in the deep-dive.

Next concrete asks for you to unblock us:
- Upload eval set + traffic sample by 2026-09-02
- Confirm which deep-dive slot you prefer by 2026-09-03

If you approve I’ll send an invite and start the projection work.

Regards,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Solutions — Redwood Inference
[attachment: SLA-draft-v1.pdf (pdf)]

source.subject: Re: Credit corridor and EU withholding clarity
messages:
From: Trevor Hayes <trevor@mediform.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: payments@mediform.com
Date: Thu, 20 Aug 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Request to move to Net60 for Q3 invoices + VAT/withholding note

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the helpful demo last week. We wanted to circle back on payment terms for our pilot period. Our CFO is asking if Mediform can move to Net60 for the initial three invoices (Aug–Oct) while we smooth cashflow ahead of our Medicare billing cycle.

A few constraints from our side:
- We need a credit buffer (small reserve) while on extended terms.
- EU VAT handling: our AP team may withhold VAT on cross-border invoices unless we provide explicit VAT registration evidence.
- We can provide an upfront deposit to offset risk if that helps.

I've attached a proposed payment timeline (mediform_proposed_payment_timeline.xlsx). Can Redwood confirm whether Net60 is acceptable, and what documentation you need for VAT / withholding?

Best,
Trevor Hayes
VP Finance, Mediform Health

--- Attachment: mediform_proposed_payment_timeline.xlsx (spreadsheet) ---

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Trevor Hayes <trevor@mediform.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request to move to Net60 for Q3 invoices + VAT/withholding note

Trevor,

Appreciate the clear ask and the timeline. We're open to accommodating a temporary Net60 for the first three invoices with a couple of guardrails so both sides are covered. High level proposal:

- Net45 by default, but Net60 for Aug–Oct as a time-limited concession (three invoices).
- Mediform provides either a one-time deposit equal to 30% of the first invoice or a rolling credit buffer (we can size this after reviewing usage estimates).
- Redwood requests VAT registration evidence or a UK/EU VAT exemption certificate to avoid local withholding; if not available, we may need to gross-up invoice amounts to be VAT-neutral.
- We will place a conditional credit hold trigger at 45 days past due if the rolling buffer drops below threshold; automatic notification to your AP and our finance ops.

Can Marcus / Laura confirm internal approvals for the temporary Net60 + deposit option? Also attaching Redwood's short credit summary for context (redwood_credit_summary.pdf) and a VAT evidence checklist (vat_evidence_request.docx).

If this looks acceptable, we can draft an amendment to the MSA and a short mutual action plan to capture milestones and the deposit flow.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0142

--- Attachments: redwood_credit_summary.pdf, vat_evidence_request.docx ---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request to move to Net60 for Q3 invoices + VAT/withholding note

Ava / Laura,

I'm OK with the temporary Net60 concession under these conditions:
- Deposit equal to 25–30% of month-one invoice (we should ask for 30% given usage variability).
- A schedule for actual usage reconciliation at the end of the three-month window; any over/under will be credited or billed on the next invoice.
- VAT evidence required within 10 business days of signing the amendment. If not provided, we'll default to net amounts that assume potential withholding.
- Finance to enable a one-click payment link on our invoices and set up an early-pay discount (0.5%) if they pay within 10 days to encourage earlier settlement.

I'll green-light legal to prepare a short amendment with those points and add a clause that allows reverting to Net45 after the pilot unless both parties agree to continue Net60. Laura, please check SLA implications for the credit buffer.

Marcus Lin
Head of Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.ai

--- Note: internal approval, no attachments ---

From: Trevor Hayes <trevor@mediform.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: payments@mediform.com, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 29 Aug 2026 16:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Credit corridor and EU withholding clarity

Ava / Marcus,

Thanks — this is helpful and fair. Mediform agrees to:
- Provide a 30% deposit on the first invoice.
- Submit VAT registration evidence by 2026-09-10 (we'll upload to the Drive link you shared).
- Accept the reconciliation at the end of the three-month window; please include the early-pay link on invoices.

We'll sign the amendment once Laura's team confirms the SLA note in writing. I've attached a signed copy of the proposed timeline for reference.

Trevor Hayes
VP Finance, Mediform Health

--- Attachment: mediform_signed_timeline.pdf (PDF) ---

source.subject: Benchmark results handoff and slot coordination
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Reynolds <ava.reynolds@starlit-ai.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Benchmark results handoff and slot coordination

Hi Ava —

Thanks again for running the perf exercises with our SE team last week. We’ve compiled an initial results packet and would like to schedule a 60–75 minute handoff/readout where we:

- Walk through the topline latency throughput numbers
- Surface the KV/prefix caching behavior we observed
- Highlight suggested tuning and short-term mitigations for production
- Agree on success criteria for a follow-up run

Proposed slots (Pacific time):
- Wed 8/26 10:00–11:15 PT
- Thu 8/27 14:00–15:15 PT
- Fri 8/28 09:30–10:45 PT

I’ve attached a short agenda (agenda-v0.2.pdf). Please let us know which slot works and who from Starlit should join (engineers vs procurement/security). If procurement/security want a separate 20–30m sync after the technical readout, we can carve that out.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0193

Attachments: agenda-v0.2.pdf (PDF, 2 pages)
From: Ava Reynolds <ava.reynolds@starlit-ai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@starlit-ai.com, security@starlit-ai.com
Date: Wed, 26 Aug 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Benchmark results handoff and slot coordination

Hi Ben —

Thanks for circling back and for the agenda. Our engineers are available but procurement and InfoSec need to attend the call as well for the contract/retention questions. High-level constraints:

- Procurement available only Fri 8/28 after 11:00 PT
- InfoSec prefers timezone overlap with EMEA mornings; they can do Thu 8/27 14:00 PT but need a short pre-read
- Engineering (myself + two backend engineers) prefer Wed 8/26 morning or Thu 8/27 afternoon

Could we do Thu 8/27 14:00–15:15 PT and then schedule a 25m procurement follow-up on Fri 8/28 11:15–11:40 PT? Please confirm and include the pre-read for InfoSec.

—Ava
Ava Reynolds
Lead Platform Engineer, Starlit AI
ava.reynolds@starlit-ai.com

On Wed, Aug 25, 2026 at 9:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [original message trimmed]
From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 26 Aug 2026 13:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Benchmark results handoff and slot coordination

Ben / Marcus —

Ava replied and flagged InfoSec + procurement. Quick coordination notes:

- SE availability: Monica (me) can lead the deep-dive Thu 8/27 14:00–15:15 PT. I’ll prepare a 10–12 minute kernel-level explanation and keep the demo to 30–35 minutes to leave room for questions.
- Marcus: can you join the procurement portion on Fri 8/28? They’ll likely ask about data retention, audit logs, and a short SOW for the performance engagement.
- Ben: I’ll put together the InfoSec pre-read—short checklist + artifact pointers (SOC 2 scope, KMS notes). We should attach the initial results PDF ahead of the readout (benchmark_results_v1.pdf).

I’ll also assemble a one-slide TL;DR for executives in case Ava brings a procurement lead who wants the higher-level summary only.

Monica
Monica Patel
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.ai

On Wed, Aug 26, 2026 at 11:05 AM Ava Reynolds <ava.reynolds@starlit-ai.com> wrote:
> [customer availability and request]
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ava Reynolds <ava.reynolds@starlit-ai.com>
Date: Thu, 27 Aug 2026 08:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Benchmark results handoff and slot coordination

All —

I can cover the procurement follow-up on Fri 8/28 11:15 PT. Quick notes from my side:

- For the SOW ask, have the engagement scope be: one performance readout + one tuning pass + optional follow-up run (timeboxed). That usually satisfies procurement.
- For retention and logs: we’ll provide a short matrix of retention by artifact type (tokens, traces, audit logs) and KMS integration options.

Attaching an availability grid so we can finalize the invite. I recommend Thu 8/27 14:00–15:15 PT for the technical readout with Monica leading and a 25m procurement/contract follow-up Fri 8/28 11:15–11:40 PT.

Attachments: availability-grid.xlsx (XLSX)

Marcus Lin
Commercial Lead | Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.ai
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Reynolds <ava.reynolds@starlit-ai.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, procurement@starlit-ai.com, security@starlit-ai.com
Date: Thu, 27 Aug 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Confirmed: Thu 8/27 14:00 PT readout + Fri procurement follow-up

Hi Ava —

Confirmed for Thu 8/27 14:00–15:15 PT for the benchmark readout. Monica will lead the technical walk-through and we’ll send the InfoSec pre-read and the results PDF by EOD today.

Procurement follow-up confirmed for Fri 8/28 11:15–11:40 PT; Marcus will join that meeting. I’ve added procurement@starlit-ai.com and security@starlit-ai.com to the invite.

Please reply with any additional stakeholders who need calendar invites (legal, compliance, or additional engineers). Calendar invite and links: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=benchmark-starlit-20260827

Agenda recap (technical readout):
- 0–5m: introductions and goals
- 5–35m: topline metrics + demo of burst behavior
- 35–55m: KV/prefix cache behavior and tuning suggestions
- 55–75m: Q&A + next-steps (follow-up run / SOW)

Attachments sent: benchmark_results_v1.pdf, agenda-v0.2.pdf

Thanks everyone — see you Thursday.

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0193

On Thu, Aug 27, 2026 at 8:40 AM Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [availability grid attached]

From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@blueharbor.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 24 Aug 2026 17:04:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding — PO issuance and requested spend cap for pilot

Hi Ben,

Thanks again — attached is our completed onboarding form (BlueHarbor_Onboarding_Form.pdf). We are ready to kick off a 3-month pilot next week. To proceed with procurement on our side I need a Redwood PO number so our AP team can map costs and release the initial invoice.

Requested spend cap: $95,000 (estimate for compute + support blocks). Suggested line item split:
- compute-hours: $80,000
- support-blocks (SLA/phone): $15,000

One wrinkle: our internal ledger code is BH-TECH-01 but our legal / PO system needs Redwood to supply a PO number and indicate your ledger/cost center. Can you confirm which cost center we should map the PO to on your side? If you can issue a provisional PO number (or confirm what the final PO will be) that’ll get our AP to release initial payment.

Please cc procurement/finance as needed.

Thanks,
Elena Ruiz
Vendor Ops — Blue Harbor Logistics
elena.ruiz@blueharbor.com
+1 415 555 0123

To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@blueharbor.com>

Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding — PO issuance and requested spend cap for pilot

Elena — thanks for the form, received and uploaded.

Quick clarifying Qs before I route this to Finance for PO issuance:
- Confirm billing entity: Blue Harbor Logistics LLC (EIN/TIN)?
- Do you need the PO to reference BH-TECH-01 as the customer ledger, or do you want Redwood to put our cost center on the PO (we can add a mapping note) — I ask because our finance group prefers internal cost center ENG-LLM-01 for pilot spend.

Next steps from my side: I will open a spendcap override request and ask Finance to issue a provisional PO so your AP can match the invoice. I attached our quick onboarding checklist for any missing fields. If you can confirm the billing entity + tax id I’ll push for an expedited turnaround.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | (650) 555-0199

To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 11:23:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Vendor onboarding — PO issuance and requested spend cap for pilot

Laura — forwarding Elena's onboarding; Ben flagged the ledger mapping question. TL;DR: Blue Harbor is asking us to include their ledger BH-TECH-01 on the PO so their AP can post correctly. Our standard is to put internal cost center ENG-LLM-01.

Request: please create a provisional PO (provisional is fine for 1st invoice) with an initial spend cap of $100,000 (a small buffer over their ask) and two line items:
1) Compute-hours — $82,500 — GL: ENG-LLM-01
2) Support-blocks — $17,500 — GL: ENG-OPS-230

Reason: avoids immediate spend blocking on their side and gives us wiggle room for early usage spikes. If Finance is uncomfortable with $100k, the team is fine with a final of ~$98.5k. I attached a one-page spendcap override justification.

Can you advise expected PO number and whether we can add a "Customer ledger" free-text field with BH-TECH-01 so Blue Harbor's AP can map the incoming invoice?

Thanks — Karthik

Attachment: spendcap_override_request.docx (DOCX, 1 page)

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@blueharbor.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Aug 2026 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Vendor onboarding — PO issuance and requested spend cap for pilot

Elena / Ben —

Thanks — Finance has processed this as an initial PO. Please see attached PO PDF (PO-2026-4598.pdf) — issued today. Summary here:
- PO number: PO-2026-4598
- Total spend cap on PO: $98,500 (finalized after internal approval)
- Line items:
  * 0001 Compute-hours — $81,000 — Cost center: ENG-LLM-01
  * 0002 Support-blocks — $17,500 — Cost center: ENG-OPS-230
- Customer ledger mapping: added free-text field on the PO: "Customer ledger: BH-TECH-01" so your AP should be able to map in your system.
- Billing entity on PO: Blue Harbor Logistics LLC — EIN: 12-3456789 (as provided).

Notes: We adjusted compute estimate slightly to balance rounding. If you need a hard cap at exactly $95,000 we can re-run approvals, but this PO is approved for $98,500 so invoices up to that amount will be accepted against PO-2026-4598.

Please confirm receipt and acceptance of the PO. If accepted, we'll mark onboarding complete on our side and provide initial invoice instructions.

Laura Bennett
Senior Manager, Finance — Vendor Payments
Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com

Attachment: PO-2026-4598.pdf (PDF, 2 pages)

source.subject: Cascade Systems workshop — proposed twilight slots, travel notes, and backup virtual plan
messages:
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Asha Patel <asha.patel@cascade-sys.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@cascade-sys.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Cascade Systems workshop — proposed twilight slots, travel notes, and backup virtual plan

Hi Asha / Tom,

Thanks again for confirming interest in the two-day on-site workshop to finalize the integration playbook and run the QoS evaluation. To help the calendar/ops side, I pulled together three scheduling options that try to respect your EMEA and US stakeholders and leave an evening slot for exec presence (we call these the "twilight" windows). Attached is a short agenda and a timezone matrix.

Proposed on-site blocks (we can make any of these hybrid with a virtual dial-in):
- Option A: Tue/Wed Sep 28–29 — core hours 13:00–18:00 BST / 05:00–10:00 PDT (exec twilight 17:00–18:00 BST)
- Option B: Wed/Thu Oct 6–7 — core hours 10:00–15:00 BST / 02:00–07:00 PDT (less friction for APAC watchers)
- Option C: Mon/Tue Oct 18–19 — core hours 09:00–14:00 BST / 01:00–06:00 PDT (earlier for US on-call teams)

Logistics notes:
- We can reserve a small executive room for the twilight executive sessions and a larger workshop room for breakout labs.
- Marcus is lining up a Wi-Fi SLA with the venue; I'm attaching the draft agenda and a short hotel/visa note for attendees who need support.
- Virtual fallback: if travel restrictions / travel budget pushback occurs, we'll publish a dedicated Zoom + streaming link and a recording plan.

Next steps I suggest:
1) Tell me which option (A/B/C) you prefer — we can hold provisional calendar blocks for two weeks while the exec team decides.
2) Confirm the attendee roster for day 1/2 so I can share travel/visa guidance.
3) Any dietary or AV constraints?

I'll pencil provisional calendar holds for Option A on our side tomorrow if I don't hear back — those holds are soft and can be adjusted.

Best,
Vanessa Ortiz
Senior Strategic Customer Manager
Redwood Inference
+1 (415) 555-0199

Attachments: Cascade_Workshop_Agenda_v2.pdf (8 pages), Timezone_Matrix.csv (spreadsheet), Visa_and_Hotel_Instructions.docx
From: Asha Patel <asha.patel@cascade-sys.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@cascade-sys.com>
Cc: -
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 11:04:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Cascade Systems workshop — proposed twilight slots, travel notes, and backup virtual plan

Thanks Vanessa — this is helpful. Quick answers:
- Prefer Option C (Oct 18–19) because our EU infra lead will be back from PTO and Tom can keep his morning customer checks.
- Attendee roster (tentative): Asha Patel (IT lead), Tom Reynolds (Product), Priya Mehta (Security), Jonas Weber (Ops) — Jonas will be remote from Munich but can come in day 2 if needed.
- Dietary: two vegetarians, one gluten-free. AV: we need a single stream endpoint for our legal/compliance observers; recording is fine but needs restricted access.

Couple of asks: can you hold a single provisional hotel room block (3 rooms) near the venue and share approximate per-night rates? Also — Marcus, please confirm the venue Wi-Fi SLA target (bandwidth per attendee).

If Option C is locked, we'll get final approval by the end of next week tied to travel-bid approval.

Best,
Asha
Head of IT Integrations
Cascade Systems
asha.patel@cascade-sys.com
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Asha Patel <asha.patel@cascade-sys.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@cascade-sys.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 13:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cascade Systems workshop — proposed twilight slots, travel notes, and backup virtual plan

On the Wi‑Fi/venue SLA: we can target a sustained 40 Mbps per concurrent attendee for the breakout labs (sufficient for remote screen-sharing and KV cache downloads). I'll confirm with the venue manager and get the SLA text by EOD Monday.

Hotel block: I reached out to the venue hotel — ballpark rate is $245/night per room for third‑week October weekend block; will try to secure a small reduction for the group. Vanessa — I'll need the final room names and check-in dates to convert the block to guarantees.

Karthik — for the exec twilight session, do we want extra AV for a secondary camera (wide room + close-up) so remote execs can see both slides and the table? That would help compliance observers.

Marcus
Senior Events Engineer
Redwood Inference
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Asha Patel <asha.patel@cascade-sys.com>, Tom Reynolds <tom.reynolds@cascade-sys.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 17:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Cascade Systems workshop — proposed twilight slots, travel notes, and backup virtual plan

Perfect — thanks all. Summary / action items:
- We will provisionally hold Option C (Oct 18–19) calendar blocks for all attendees — I'll send soft holds today (MeetingInvite.ics attached).
- Marcus to confirm venue Wi‑Fi SLA by Monday and finalize hotel block rates by EOD Tuesday.
- Asha to share final attendee roster and compliance requirements by Aug 28; Vanessa will add dietary to hotel reservations.
- Karthik to scope secondary camera/streaming for executive twilight and confirm cost estimate.

source.subject: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Markus / Evelyn —

Quick one for exec consideration. Veridian Health (clinical SaaS, ~1.2M annual revenue run-rate in pilot) are asking for a deeper-than-normal onboarding credit and a rate concession on Hosted API usage for the first 12 months as part of a strategic marketplace co-marketing and multi-region pilot.

Summary:
- Ask: 27% unit price concession on Hosted API token rates for 12 months, plus $100k onboarding credit applied to first invoice.
- Commitment: 18 month engagement, 12 month minimum commitment to the Billing Tier with a 6-month ramp schedule; expected usage year 1 = 18M tokens/month by month 6.
- Rationale: High strategic value — Veridian is a large referral network; they’ve agreed to co-market a Redwood-hosted integration and list us in their marketplace. Marketing exposure + proof point in health vertical could accelerate similar mid-market deals.
- Risk: Revenue dilution and short-term margin hit; finance estimates ~22% gross margin impact on blended unit economics for year 1 if fully granted.

Attachments: pricing_workbook_v3.xlsx (narrowed scenarios), veridian_mou_draft.pdf, usage_forecast_q3-q4.csv
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/9872
Deal team recommendation: Approve up to 28% for 12 months with a mandatory upsell milestone at month 9 (if usage < 60% of forecast, credit is clawed back pro-rata).

Request: need an exec decision today (EOD PST) to keep momentum — they’re asking for an offer to present to their CFO on Monday.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Ben —
Thanks for the write-up, good summary. A couple quick clarifying points from finance that will affect approval thresholds:
1) Is the $100k onboarding credit refundable / offset if they churn before 6 months? (it currently reads as non-refundable).
2) The forecasted peak usage (18M tokens/mo) — what’s the committed minimum? We need a committed minimum MRR or token floor to model ROI.

Prelim math: at 27% concession our blended token margin drops ~20–24% depending on the caching assumptions in the workbook. If Marketing delivers the co-marketing deliverables by month 3 (we need specific KPIs), I’m comfortable recommending up to 25% short-term. Above that, we need a legal addendum with clawback language and a minimum AMR.

Can you update the MOU draft with a 12-month minimum token floor and a 6-month clawback on onboarding credit? Also flag for legal to review SLA/PII clauses — Veridian will be in scope for HIPAA-adjacent workflows.

—Mina
Mina Haddad | Head of Finance, Redwood Inference
mina.haddad@redwood.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Legal (contract-review) <legal@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 12:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Recommendation: Approve with conditions — up to 30% for 12 months (structured)

Team —
I reviewed the deal economics and strategic value. My recommendation: approve the concession but structured as follows so we protect unit economics while preserving momentum.

Terms I propose for exec signoff:
- Max concession: 30% off base Hosted API token rate, but applied on a tapering schedule: 30% for months 1–3, 28% for months 4–6, 25% for months 7–12 (weighted blended ≈ 27%).
- Onboarding: $75k credit upfront (not refundable); remaining $25k released only after achieving the month-6 usage milestone (>= 60% of forecast).
- Minimum payment floor: annualized minimum billing = $420k (if actuals fall below, they pay the difference).
- Legal addendum: 6-month clawback on credits, data-security attestation, and co-marketing commitments (deliverables and timelines).
- Review cadence: Finance & CS to re-evaluate at month 6 for renewal / rate reset.

This preserves flexibility (we can advertise a stronger initial concession to the buyer) while ensuring a guardrail for finance. Legal, please start a targeted addendum.

Marcus
Marcus Lin | VP, Sales — Enterprise Accounts
marcus.lin@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Legal (contract-review) <legal@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 15:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Markus / Evelyn —
Looping you both for a quick decision. Options below — we need one selected so I can present a firm offer to Veridian before their CFO meeting Monday.

Options for quick vote:
A) Approve Marcus structure (tapered concession up to 30% with $75k upfront credit + $25k deferred) — legal to finalize addendum.
B) Approve up to 25% (shorter approval, less legal overhead).
C) Decline concession and offer standard onboarding + a co-marketing pilot instead.

If we don’t respond by EOD PST, they’ve indicated they’ll pause negotiations and re-run the RFP.

Thanks — Ben

--- Forwarded message attached below ---

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Legal (contract-review) <legal@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 16:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Approved (A) with the following clarifications:
- Approve Marcus’ structured taper up to 30% for the first three months then taper as described.
- $75k onboarding credit up front; $25k contingent on month-6 milestone (>=60% forecast).
- Legal to add explicit clawback language and the minimum payment floor ($420k annualized).
- CS to own the month-6 review and provide usage verification report.

Legal: please draft the addendum today so we can send the offer to Veridian by COB tomorrow.

Markus Klein | Chief Revenue Officer, Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwood.com | ext. 2211

Ben Carter | Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Ben —
Thanks for the write-up, good summary. A couple quick clarifying points from finance that will affect approval thresholds:
1) Is the $100k onboarding credit refundable / offset if they churn before 6 months? (it currently reads as non-refundable).
2) The forecasted peak usage (18M tokens/mo) — what’s the committed minimum? We need a committed minimum MRR or token floor to model ROI.

Prelim math: at 27% concession our blended token margin drops ~20–24% depending on the caching assumptions in the workbook. If Marketing delivers the co-marketing deliverables by month 3 (we need specific KPIs), I’m comfortable recommending up to 25% short-term. Above that, we need a legal addendum with clawback language and a minimum AMR.

Can you update the MOU draft with a 12-month minimum token floor and a 6-month clawback on onboarding credit? Also flag for legal to review SLA/PII clauses — Veridian will be in scope for HIPAA-adjacent workflows.

—Mina
Mina Haddad | Head of Finance, Redwood Inference
mina.haddad@redwood.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Legal (contract-review) <legal@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 12:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Recommendation: Approve with conditions — up to 30% for 12 months (structured)

Team —
I reviewed the deal economics and strategic value. My recommendation: approve the concession but structured as follows so we protect unit economics while preserving momentum.

Terms I propose for exec signoff:
- Max concession: 30% off base Hosted API token rate, but applied on a tapering schedule: 30% for months 1–3, 28% for months 4–6, 25% for months 7–12 (weighted blended ≈ 27%).
- Onboarding: $75k credit upfront (not refundable); remaining $25k released only after achieving the month-6 usage milestone (>= 60% of forecast).
- Minimum payment floor: annualized minimum billing = $420k (if actuals fall below, they pay the difference).
- Legal addendum: 6-month clawback on credits, data-security attestation, and co-marketing commitments (deliverables and timelines).
- Review cadence: Finance & CS to re-evaluate at month 6 for renewal / rate reset.

This preserves flexibility (we can advertise a stronger initial concession to the buyer) while ensuring a guardrail for finance. Legal, please start a targeted addendum.

Marcus
Marcus Lin | VP, Sales — Enterprise Accounts
marcus.lin@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Legal (contract-review) <legal@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 15:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Markus / Evelyn —
Looping you both for a quick decision. Options below — we need one selected so I can present a firm offer to Veridian before their CFO meeting Monday.

Options for quick vote:
A) Approve Marcus structure (tapered concession up to 30% with $75k upfront credit + $25k deferred) — legal to finalize addendum.
B) Approve up to 25% (shorter approval, less legal overhead).
C) Decline concession and offer standard onboarding + a co-marketing pilot instead.

If we don’t respond by EOD PST, they’ve indicated they’ll pause negotiations and re-run the RFP.

Thanks — Ben

--- Forwarded message attached below ---

From: Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Legal (contract-review) <legal@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Aug 2026 16:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Exec signoff requested: Strategic Hosted API onboarding incentive (>20%)

Approved (A) with the following clarifications:
- Approve Marcus’ structured taper up to 30% for the first three months then taper as described.
- $75k onboarding credit up front; $25k contingent on month-6 milestone (>=60% forecast).
- Legal to add explicit clawback language and the minimum payment floor ($420k annualized).
- CS to own the month-6 review and provide usage verification report.

Legal: please draft the addendum today so we can send the offer to Veridian by COB tomorrow.

Markus Klein | Chief Revenue Officer, Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwood.com | ext. 2211

source.subject: Realtime: Hosted API latency spike impacting Furnishly checkout - urgent
messages:
From: Alicia Gomez <agomez@furnishly.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-17T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Realtime: Hosted API latency spike impacting Furnishly checkout

Hi Lauren,

We're seeing a sharp regression in p95/p99 latency on the Hosted API that's spiking our checkout times and causing payment timeouts. Impact started ~08:52 PDT. Quick numbers from our monitoring: p95 went from ~120ms to ~1.1s, p99 from ~240ms to ~2.8s, error rate jumped from 0.2% to ~2.4% over the last 15 minutes. Throughput for our integration has dipped ~18% as requests back off.

Sample request id: req_9f3b2a (from 2026-08-17T08:54:12Z). We can reproduce on multiple regions (us-west1 primarily). This is customer-facing and blocking checkout flows for ~6% of sessions.

We urgently need a triage and a mitigation plan. Attaching a small snippet of our request logs and a synthetic trace.

Attachments: request_logs_20260817.gz

Thanks,
Alicia Gomez
Head of SRE, Furnishly
Alicia.Gomez@furnishly.com

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
To: Alicia Gomez <agomez@furnishly.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-17T09:07:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Realtime: Hosted API latency spike impacting Furnishly checkout

Hi Alicia — sorry you're seeing this. I'm escalating immediately to SRE and our support squad. Marcus/Jonas/Evelyn: Furnishly reporting a sharp p95/p99 regression starting ~08:52 PDT; sample req id req_9f3b2a attached. Can someone pick this up and confirm scope + mitigation? Will circle back in 30–45 mins with an update.

On Tue, Aug 17, 2026 at 9:03 AM Alicia Gomez <agomez@furnishly.com> wrote:
> We're seeing a sharp regression in p95/p99 latency on the Hosted API...

Thanks,
Lauren Bishop
Account Manager, Redwood Inference
lauren_bishop@redwood.com

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <agomez@furnishly.com>
Date: 2026-08-17T09:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Realtime: Hosted API latency spike impacting Furnishly checkout — initial triage

Quick triage notes from the runtime team:
- We see a correlated spike in p95/p99 for Furnishly's tenant across the us-west1 pool starting 2026-08-17T08:50:40Z.
- Error-rate increase lines up with several long tail token-generation operations.
- Hypothesis: rapid eviction of prefix/KV cache entries during an autoscale burst caused many cold-key lookups; cold KV hits are adding ~700–1,800ms to tail latency.
- Evidence: KV miss rate for tenant_id=furnishly increased from ~1.8% to ~28% in the 9:00–9:15 window; cold-cache requests show higher kernel time and repeated KV fetch retries.

Immediate actions taken:
1) Rotated Furnishly traffic to a warmed routing plane in us-west1 to reduce cross-pool cold starts (rolled at 09:14 PDT).
2) Triggered an ad-hoc async KV pre-warm job for the hot prefixes observed (started 09:20 PDT).
3) Throttled low-priority background jobs that were contributing to bursty writes to the prefix index.

Request for Furnishly: can you send any recent traffic pattern changes (batch imports, traffic spikes from a new campaign) and confirm the exact request IDs if you have more than the sample.

Attaching host trace we captured: host_trace_20260817.log.gz

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
SRE Manager, Redwood Inference
marcus_lin@redwood.com

From: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Alicia Gomez <agomez@furnishly.com>
Date: 2026-08-17T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Realtime: Hosted API latency spike — deeper analysis + mitigations

Log excerpt and quick analysis (redacted):
--- begin log snippet ---
2026-08-17T08:54:12.324Z tenant=furnishly req=req_9f3b2a route=hosted-req latency=1824ms kv_hit=false kv_fetch_ms=1396
2026-08-17T08:54:12.328Z tenant=furnishly req=req_9f3b2a retry=1 kv_fetch_ms=1420
--- end log snippet ---

What happened: the routing plane experienced a short concurrent scaling event which caused the placement of several hot prefixes to shift. The automatic placement policy evicted a subset of in-memory prefix entries; when warmed replicas were not immediately available, requests fell back to cold KV which is slower.

Short-term mitigations we recommend (can be enacted in the next 30–120 minutes):
- Enable forced prefix warmup for Furnishly's tenant on their current model/route (we can run this now).
- Temporarily pin Furnishly to the warmed routing plane for 2–6 hours while we run continuous warmers.
- As a safety net, route a small percentage of traffic to a quantized-but-warmed model variant (slightly lower QoE but much faster tail).

Longer term: adjust placement heuristics to avoid evicting hot prefixes during autoscale bursts; add prefix-warmup as part of scale-up lifecycle.

Approval needed from Lauren/Evelyn to: 1) pin routing plane and 2) allow a short-term increase in reserved capacity (this may incur credits/cost). Once approved we'll enact the routing change and confirm with Furnishly.

—Jonas Weber
Support Lead, Redwood Inference
jonas_weber@redwood.com

From: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-17T10:18:00-07:00
Subject: Exec alert: Furnishly outage impact + recommended mitigations (requesting approval)

Evelyn — Furnishly (important customer, checkout impact) is experiencing a p95/p99 regression caused by prefix cache evictions during a scale event. Marcus and Jonas have implemented temporary mitigations (warmed routing and pre-warm jobs) and recommend:
- Pin to warmed routing plane for 4 hours
- Run continuous prefix warmers for the next 8 hours
- Temporarily increase reserved capacity for Furnishly (estimate ~$1,800 for the next 24 hours)

Requesting your approval to apply the pinned routing + capacity bump now. If approved I'll communicate the mitigation plan and timeline to Alicia; we should also offer Furnishly a goodwill credit for the disruption (TBD).

On the customer side Alicia has accepted our workaround but asks for an RCA and timeline for a permanent fix.

Lauren Bishop
Account Manager, Redwood Inference
lauren_bishop@redwood.com

From: Alicia Gomez <agomez@furnishly.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-18T16:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Realtime: Hosted API latency spike impacting Furnishly checkout — ack and next steps

Thanks for the fast response and the temporary mitigation. We saw tail latency return to expected ranges after the pin/warm job (p95 back to ~140ms, p99 ~260ms). Furnishly Product wants a short RCA and a timeline for the placement/eviction fix and any permanent guardrails you plan to add.

Please also document the goodwill credit and a short statement on what monitoring/alerts will prevent this without manual pinning in the future. We can coordinate a post-mortem call tomorrow morning if needed.

Attachments: none

Alicia
Head of SRE, Furnishly
agomez@furnishly.com

messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ramos <elena.ramos@amazon.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Aug 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Q3 Partner Program Tiering & Marketplace Fee Recalibration — AWS/Azure/GCP

Hi Elena / AWS team,

Thanks for the heads up on the new marketplace fee bands you circulated last week. We reviewed the change and it materially impacts our unit economics on the hosted API (tokens/embeddings) and will push our per-query cost up ~9–12% on the mid-tail workload. Before we get pulled into escalations with Finance, I wanted to align internally and with you on negotiation levers.

High-level asks/asks from our side:
- Can AWS preserve our current effective fee band for an additional 12 months while we migrate a portion of traffic to Dedicated?
- If not, will you entertain a commit-based rebate (quarterly true-up) or a one-time listing credit for Q4 to offset pass-through costs?
- Are co-sell / market development credits available if we bump to the next partner tier?

Attached: AWS_fee_schedule_Q3.pdf — our annotated copy of the proposed bands.

Next steps I propose: internal cost modeling from Finance + a single negotiation call with Elena + procurement/legal to get redlines ready. Karthik/Marcus/Monica — can you pls run a quick impact model (3 months and 12 months) and flag non-starters on terms?

Elena — can we get a commitment from AWS on available program levers (credit, rebate, co-sell) so we know what to push for on the call?

Thanks,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Principal Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0182
[Attachment: AWS_fee_schedule_Q3.pdf]
From: Elena Ramos <elena.ramos@amazon.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: AWS Partner Marketplace Team <aws-partner-marketplace@amazon.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 3 Aug 2026 13:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q3 Partner Program Tiering & Marketplace Fee Recalibration — AWS/Azure/GCP

Hi Ben / team,

Appreciate the clear summary. Short answers up front: yes — there are a few levers we can use on a case-by-case basis, but they require commercial commitment and an approved partner plan.

Options we can offer:
- 9–12 month transitional credit: available for verified migration plans or committed ARR growth (requires legal amendment).
- Quarterly volume rebate: needs a minimum booking threshold and is assessed after quarterly reconciliation.
- Co-sell credits: available if you meet the co-sell activity thresholds and joint pipeline commitments.

I attached a redline of the standard partner amortization we typically use and a draft of the offer terms we've preliminarily scoped (AWS_offer_terms_v3.docx).

Propose a 45-minute call this Thursday at 10:00 PT to walk the options and map to your expected traffic split between Hosted/Dedicated. If that works, I'll send a calendar invite and include our Commercial Agreements lead.

Best,
Elena

--
Elena Ramos
Sr. Partner Manager, AWS Marketplace
elena.ramos@amazon.com | +1 206-555-0177
[Attachment: AWS_offer_terms_v3.docx]
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q3 Partner Program Tiering & Marketplace Fee Recalibration — AWS/Azure/GCP

Ben / team,

Quick notes from my side after a fast look at the fee schedule:
- Immediate ask: finance-run P&L for both baseline (no change) and two mitigation scenarios: (a) accept fee bands + one-time credit; (b) push for rebate + 12-month transitional credit. Please include token-level unit economics and impact on Hosted API gross margin.
- Procurement/legal — we'll need a short amendment (1–2 pages) that secures the credit and sets clear measurement / reconciliation cadence. Monica, can you provision a draft amendment skeleton based on AWS_offer_terms_v3.docx?
- Timeline: if we want Q4 coverage, we need signed amendments by 9/20. That leaves ~6 weeks for negotiation and internal approvals.

Marcus — can you run the 3-month and 12-month modeling (quick sensitivity table)? Thx.

Karthik

--
Karthik Iyer
Head of Partnerships | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Elena Ramos <elena.ramos@amazon.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 4 Aug 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q3 Partner Program Tiering & Marketplace Fee Recalibration — AWS/Azure/GCP

Folks — thanks for the quick back-and-forth.

Summary / Mutual Action Plan (MAP):
1) Ben to confirm call with Elena for Thu 8/6 10:00 PT — Ben, please send invite and include AWS Commercial Agreements lead.
2) Marcus to deliver 3mo and 12mo P&L sensitivity by EOD Wed 8/5.
3) Monica to draft a 1–2 page amendment skeleton and circulate redlines by EOD Thu 8/6.
4) Karthik to consolidate negotiation priorities (credit vs rebate vs co-sell) and target ask levels.
5) Ben/Karthik to present internal approval ask to Revenue Operations and execs by 8/12 if necessary.

Attachments: MAP_aws_marketplace_Q3.xlsx (tracking sheet for tasks, owners, and timelines). I've also added a link to the HubSpot deal and the draft amendment folder.

Ben — please loop Rajiv from Consulting Partners if you want a quick external benchmark on typical credit sizes for comparable ISVs (he can run precedent comps).

I'll treat this as a high-priority procurement/partner negotiation — we should aim to close before the public fee change hits customers.

Thanks,
Lauren

--
Lauren Bishop
VP, Strategic Partnerships | Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.com | +1 212-555-0139
[Attachment: MAP_aws_marketplace_Q3.xlsx]

source.subject: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request
messages:
From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

Hi team,

Quick summary: Orion Health is planning a multi-week integration rehearsal (end-to-end traffic with production-like payloads) to validate cold-start behavior, cache warming, and billing events ahead of their public launch. They want to run two sustained weekends of high-concurrency tests starting the weekend of Aug 14.

Why we need an exception:
- Expected steady-state QPS: 1,200 reqs/s during peak windows (short bursts to 2,000).
- Model: r-infer-13B (FP16) with KV cache; warm-up time for cold starts is ~18s without prefix cache.
- Primary concern: cold-start tail latency causing false-positive SLA alerts and noisy replay for their telemetry.

Ask:
1) Ephemeral reservation: 12 GPU-equivalents (A100-class) reserved for Aug 14-16 and Aug 21-23 (weekend windows) with no preemption during the test windows (08:00-22:00 PDT).
2) Autoscale soft-limit increase: allow burst up to 16 GPUs for 30m windows during the scheduled tests to absorb spikes.
3) Billing: charge to DEAL-7193 cost center; product will absorb overage for the two rehearsal weekends.

Trade-offs we can accept:
- Preemptible outside the test windows (22:01-07:59).
- Lower priority than enterprise SLA customers; will fall back if capacity is exhausted outside the reserved windows.

Attachments: integration-runbook.pdf (runbook + rollback steps), capacity-forecast-aug.csv (detailed expected token volume).

Next step: Marcus or infra team, can you confirm feasibility and conflict with other reservations? Kimberly/Tessa, if ops can sign off, I will lock the dates with Orion.

Thanks,
Laura
--
Laura Bennett
Senior PM, Dedicated Capacity Programs
Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

On Sun, Aug 1, 2026 at 9:12 AM Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Quick summary: Orion Health is planning a multi-week integration rehearsal...

Thanks for the detail, Laura. Short answer: possible, but a few operational constraints and questions before we can commit to non-preemptible reservations at that scale.

Operational notes / concerns:
- US-WEST1 (the requested region) has two overlapping pilots (Brightline and Nimbus) the week prior; 12 A100-equivalents blocks a sizable chunk of our headroom.
- Autoscale bursts to 16 are feasible, but we need a guardrail: maximum sustained burst duration per hour cannot exceed 45m without impacting other commitments.
- We'd need the runbook to include an automated rollback step that reclaims the reservation if cold-start tail persists beyond target SLA (to avoid noisy alarms).

Alternatives to reduce footprint:
- Shorter token contexts via prefix trimming (Orion agreed to try this in earlier tests).
- Use a 27B quantized variant that drops per-request GPU time by ~25% (tradeoff: slightly worse quality).
- Schedule heavy replay during off-peak night windows to shift load.

Please send the runbook link and a single-sheet business justification so I can pre-authorize with capacity planning. If Kimberly approves prioritization trade (time-of-day handback), I can tentatively reserve the slice and flag it as "ephemeral-runway-ORION-7193".

- Marcus
Principal Infra Ops

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-01T13:21:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

Thanks — I support giving Orion a runway for the rehearsal provided we attach strict conditions. My approval in principle with the following must-haves:

1) Time-boxed windows only (exact start/end times per weekend).
2) Auto-reclaim: if utilization is < 30% for a continuous 60-minute window during the midday block, infra reclaims capacity automatically.
3) Telemetry hooks: product team must stream token-level latency histograms to the Console and tag the rehearsal with DEAL-7193.
4) Cost cap: limit spending to $XXk per weekend (Finance will sign off once we have the single-sheet).

If Marcus can implement the auto-reclaim and we get the single-sheet business justification by EOD Aug 6, Kimberly approves the reservation.

Also: Laura, please confirm whether Orion will accept a pre-warmed cache artifact (we'll provide a KV snapshot) — that would reduce the GPU runway needed by ~25%.

- Kimberly
Director, Product Ops

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-02T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

Kimberly — thanks for the guidance. I want to lock this down today so Orion can finalize their test plan. Summary of asks against Kimberly's conditions:
- Runbook (attached) includes auto-reclaim implementation and exact window boundaries (we already added reclaimer hook).
- Orion agrees to the telemetry hooks and cost cap; finance contact is cc'd on their side.
- Orion will try the KV snapshot approach; if warm artifacts reduce needed GPUs we will scale down the reservation to 9 GPUs and keep burst to 12.

Please confirm (explicit "approve") and I'll create the reservation request into procurement with costs charged to DEAL-7193. Marcus, if you can provision a tentative ephemeral slice named ephemeral-runway-ORION-7193 and a reserve ticket in INFRA-8421, we'll tie the Jira ticket to the runbook and HubSpot record.

Regards,
Aditya
Head of Customer Integrations
Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-03T15:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

Approved and actioned per Kimberly's conditions.

What I did:
- Created ephemeral reservation: ephemeral-runway-ORION-7193 (initial size 12 GPUs) in us-west1.
- Configured autoscale soft-limit to 16 GPUs with per-hour burst cap of 45m; auto-reclaim rule deployed (INFRA-8421).
- Linked runbook and Jira: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3C_example-runbook and JIRA: INFRA-8421.
- Added billing tag DEAL-7193 to the reservation.

Notes and next steps:
- If Orion accepts the KV snapshot and we observe steady-state utilization < 70% during the dry-run on Aug 10, we'll reduce the slice to 9 GPUs and lower burst limit to 12.
- Marcus will monitor and post a daily digest to the Console channel during the rehearsal weekends.

Marcus
Principal Infra Ops
Redwood Inference
