Attachments: integration-runbook.pdf (runbook + rollback steps), capacity-forecast-aug.csv (detailed expected token volume).

Next step: Marcus or infra team, can you confirm feasibility and conflict with other reservations? Kimberly/Tessa, if ops can sign off, I will lock the dates with Orion.

Thanks,
Laura
--
Laura Bennett
Senior PM, Dedicated Capacity Programs
Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-01T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

On Sun, Aug 1, 2026 at 9:12 AM Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Quick summary: Orion Health is planning a multi-week integration rehearsal...

Thanks for the detail, Laura. Short answer: possible, but a few operational constraints and questions before we can commit to non-preemptible reservations at that scale.

Operational notes / concerns:
- US-WEST1 (the requested region) has two overlapping pilots (Brightline and Nimbus) the week prior; 12 A100-equivalents blocks a sizable chunk of our headroom.
- Autoscale bursts to 16 are feasible, but we need a guardrail: maximum sustained burst duration per hour cannot exceed 45m without impacting other commitments.
- We'd need the runbook to include an automated rollback step that reclaims the reservation if cold-start tail persists beyond target SLA (to avoid noisy alarms).

Alternatives to reduce footprint:
- Shorter token contexts via prefix trimming (Orion agreed to try this in earlier tests).
- Use a 27B quantized variant that drops per-request GPU time by ~25% (tradeoff: slightly worse quality).
- Schedule heavy replay during off-peak night windows to shift load.

Please send the runbook link and a single-sheet business justification so I can pre-authorize with capacity planning. If Kimberly approves prioritization trade (time-of-day handback), I can tentatively reserve the slice and flag it as \"ephemeral-runway-ORION-7193\".

- Marcus
Principal Infra Ops

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-01T13:21:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

Thanks — I support giving Orion a runway for the rehearsal provided we attach strict conditions. My approval in principle with the following must-haves:

1) Time-boxed windows only (exact start/end times per weekend).
2) Auto-reclaim: if utilization is < 30% for a continuous 60-minute window during the midday block, infra reclaims capacity automatically.
3) Telemetry hooks: product team must stream token-level latency histograms to the Console and tag the rehearsal with DEAL-7193.
4) Cost cap: limit spending to $XXk per weekend (Finance will sign off once we have the single-sheet).

If Marcus can implement the auto-reclaim and we get the single-sheet business justification by EOD Aug 6, Kimberly approves the reservation.

Also: Laura, please confirm whether Orion will accept a pre-warmed cache artifact (we'll provide a KV snapshot) — that would reduce the GPU runway needed by ~25%.

- Kimberly
Director, Product Ops

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-02T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

Kimberly — thanks for the guidance. I want to lock this down today so Orion can finalize their test plan. Summary of asks against Kimberly's conditions:
- Runbook (attached) includes auto-reclaim implementation and exact window boundaries (we already added reclaimer hook).
- Orion agrees to the telemetry hooks and cost cap; finance contact is cc'd on their side.
- Orion will try the KV snapshot approach; if warm artifacts reduce needed GPUs we will scale down the reservation to 9 GPUs and keep burst to 12.

Please confirm (explicit \"approve\") and I'll create the reservation request into procurement with costs charged to DEAL-7193. Marcus, if you can provision a tentative ephemeral slice named ephemeral-runway-ORION-7193 and a reserve ticket in INFRA-8421, we'll tie the Jira ticket to the runbook and HubSpot record.

Regards,
Aditya
Head of Customer Integrations
Redwood Inference

From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-03T15:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

Approved and actioned per Kimberly's conditions.

What I did:
- Created ephemeral reservation: ephemeral-runway-ORION-7193 (initial size 12 GPUs) in us-west1.
- Configured autoscale soft-limit to 16 GPUs with per-hour burst cap of 45m; auto-reclaim rule deployed (INFRA-8421).
- Linked runbook and Jira: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3C_example-runbook and JIRA: INFRA-8421.
- Added billing tag DEAL-7193 to the reservation.

Notes and next steps:
- If Orion accepts the KV snapshot and we observe steady-state utilization < 70% during the dry-run on Aug 10, we'll reduce the slice to 9 GPUs and lower burst limit to 12.
- Marcus will monitor and post a daily digest to the Console channel during the rehearsal weekends.

Marcus
Principal Infra Ops
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Integration rehearsal: temporary runway & autoscale exception request

Hi team,

Quick summary: Orion Health is planning a multi-week integration rehearsal (end-to-end traffic with production-like payloads) to validate cold-start behavior, cache warming, and billing events ahead of their public launch. They want to run two sustained weekends of high-concurrency tests starting the weekend of Aug 14.

Why we need an exception:
- Expected steady-state QPS: 1,200 reqs/s during peak windows (short bursts to 2,000).
- Model: r-infer-13B (FP16) with KV cache; warm-up time for cold starts is ~18s without prefix cache.
- Primary concern: cold-start tail latency causing false-positive SLA alerts and noisy replay for their telemetry.

Ask:
1) Ephemeral reservation: 12 GPU-equivalents (A100-class) reserved for Aug 14-16 and Aug 21-23 (weekend windows) with no preemption during the test windows (08:00-22:00 PDT).
2) Autoscale soft-limit increase: allow burst up to 16 GPUs for 30m windows during the scheduled tests to absorb spikes.
3) Billing: charge to DEAL-7193 cost center; product will absorb overage for the two rehearsal weekends.

Trade-offs we can accept:
- Preemptible outside the test windows (22:01-07:59).
- Lower priority than enterprise SLA customers; will fall back if capacity is exhausted outside the reserved windows.

Attachments: integration-runbook.pdf (runbook + rollback steps), capacity-forecast-aug.csv (detailed expected token volume).

Next step: Marcus or infra team, can you confirm feasibility and conflict with other reservations? Kimberly/Tessa, if ops can sign off, I will lock the dates with Orion.

Thanks,
Laura
--
Laura Bennett
Senior PM, Dedicated Capacity Programs
Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah West <sarah.west@fastpassevents.com>, events@fastpassevents.com
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-07-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: FastPass / Redwood — event collateral & demo runthrough

Hi Sarah / FastPass events team,

Thanks again for partnering with us on the FastPass Summit (Aug 12). We want to finalize the attendee one-pager, the 12-minute demo script, and the short case study excerpt this week so creative can lock assets by Friday. Attached is a first-pass case study and a rough outline of the demo flow.

Asks: 
- Can you confirm the final logo and speaker bios we should use?
- Do you have any brand lock dates we need to hit?
- Would you prefer the demo use your sandbox or our hosted demo tenant? (we can mirror either)

Internal: Laura / Marcus / Mina — let's do a content sprint today to produce a concise one-pager and a tightened demo script. Laura, can you take the lead on the one-pager? Marcus, please draft the demo script (12 minutes, two segments: intro + live inference). Mina, compile the customer quotes. I'll coordinate with FastPass on assets and approvals.

Attachments: fastpass_case_v1.pdf (draft case study)

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
+1 415-555-0167

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>, Sarah West <sarah.west@fastpassevents.com>
Date: 2026-07-28T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FastPass / Redwood — event collateral & demo runthrough

Ben — putting this on my calendar now. Quick notes after a first read of the attached case study: 

- Case study: tighten the TL;DR to 2 bullets (impact + quantifiable outcome). I'll edit and push a one-pager draft by 3pm PT.
- One-pager: propose a 1-column layout with 3 sections (challenge, solution, results + call-to-action). Ill include a small callout for the demo time slot.
- Demo script: Marcus, I recommend a 90s intro, 8:30 live demo, 1:30 closing Q&A prompt. Keep the live demo focused on latency/cost tradeoff story.

I've attached a lightweight Word version of the one-pager template and a short comments doc. Also tagging brand items we need from FastPass: official logo (.svg), headshots for Sarah + lead PM, and preferred quote attribution.

Attachments: onepager_fp_v1.docx

Onward —
Laura Bennett
Field Marketing Manager | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com

> On 2026-07-28 09:12, Ben Carter wrote:
> > Hi Sarah / FastPass events team,
> > 
> > Thanks again for partnering with us...
> > (original message trimmed)

From: Sarah West <sarah.west@fastpassevents.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: events@fastpassevents.com
Date: 2026-07-29T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FastPass / Redwood — event collateral & demo runthrough

Hi Ben / Laura — great to connect. 

We're on the same page and can hit your timeline. Assets attached: fastpass_logo.svg (use the horizontal lockup), speaker_bios.pdf (Sarah + Miguel Ramos — please use Miguel's preferred attribution: "Miguel Ramos, Head of Platform, FastPass"). Brand lock date is Aug 6 EOD — after that we can only do micro-edits.

Demo environment: we prefer you use your hosted demo tenant (easier to manage on our side). We'll provide a sample dataset and a service account this afternoon. For the live demo, please plan for a 2-minute backup fallback in case of connectivity (slide with pre-rendered inference trace).

Also, attaching a short legal-approved quote from the customer wed like to include in the one-pager.

Attachments: fastpass_logo.svg; speaker_bios.pdf

Best,
Sarah West
Head of Events | FastPass Events
sarah.west@fastpassevents.com

> On Jul 28, 2026, at 09:12, Ben Carter wrote:
> > Thanks again for partnering with us on the FastPass Summit...

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah West <sarah.west@fastpassevents.com>, events@fastpassevents.com
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-07-29T14:55:00-07:00
Subject: Re: FastPass / Redwood — event collateral & demo runthrough

Thanks — this is helpful and keeps us on-track. Quick checklist and next steps: 

- Laura: one-pager draft (2 bullets + CTA) due 2026-07-29 17:00 PT — please attach final .pdf to the Drive link.
- Marcus: final demo script v3 due 2026-07-30 10:00 PT (12-minute rehearsal slot booked Aug 10).
- Mina: pull the customer quote and final attribution, confirm permission language (deadline 2026-07-30).
- Sarah / FastPass: share the service account + sample dataset by EOD today; confirm Aug 6 brand lock.
- Ops note: we'll include a fallback slide with pre-rendered trace in the deck.

Attaching final demo script for internal review: final_demo_script_v3.docx (internal). I'll merge Laura's one-pager into the drive and ping once live.

If everyone can hit these, creative can finalize assets Aug 2 and we can publish the co-branded one-pager on Aug 5.

Thanks all —
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com
+1 415-555-0167

source.subject: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0000
Subject: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows

Hi Maya —

Thanks again for taking time yesterday. As discussed, we want to make the evaluation exchange as low-friction as possible while protecting Chronicle's IP and meeting your procurement needs. Attached is a first-pass QA_Kit_v1.zip that contains: a limited prompt pack (redacted customer PII), a 150-testcase seed set, and a small README with ingestion recommendations.

Key asks from our side:
- Confirm the narrow usage permissions (only evaluation against REDWOOD_PROD models for 60 days, no re-distribution)
- Confirm retention window for raw logs and derived embeddings (we propose 90 days for raw logs, 1 year for masked embeddings)
- Agree on a mutual action plan (MAP) with clear milestones so we can align engineering support windows

Proposed high-level MAP timeline (subject to your procurement):
- 7/21: QA kit delivery (this email)
- 7/23: Chronicle ingest validation (internal)
- 7/28: First eval run + quality review
- 8/04: Review call to agree on go/no-go and next steps

Please let us know if Chronicle needs alternate retention windows or an additional redaction pass. If procurement wants a different SOW shape I can loop legal immediately.

Thanks,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
https://redwood.com

Attachment: QA_Kit_v1.zip (prompt_pack/seed_testcases/README.md)

From: Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Arun Patel <arun@chronicle.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 23 Jul 2026 13:41:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows

Thanks Aisha — appreciate the quick turnaround and the clear timeline. A couple of things from my side after a quick pass:

- Usage: Chronicle is comfortable with a 60-day evaluation window, but we need a contractual clause that prohibits retention beyond agreed windows and forbids derivative dataset creation for third-party sharing.
- Redaction: The prompt_pack looks good but could you confirm whether any system-level logs will include unhashed user identifiers? We require either hashing or tokenization before storage.
- Procurement: Copying our procurement lead (procurement@chronicle.ai). They may propose edits to the SOW.

We'll review the SOW draft and get back with a procurement redline by end of this week. For technical items, can Rishi confirm the ingest and watermarking/tracing approach?

—Maya
Maya Gupta
Head of Product, Chronicle Labs
Attachment: SOW_Draft_Chronicle.pdf

On Tue, Jul 21, 2026 at 09:12 Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com> wrote:
> [original message omitted]

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 24 Jul 2026 15:06:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows

Hi Maya —

Quick technical notes to address your questions:
- Ingest: We can offer two ingest modes: (1) Redwood-managed staging (default) where we store raw logs for 90 days in an encrypted bucket with access audits, or (2) Chronicle-managed S3 ingestion into a private VPC where Redwood only receives masked/hashed artifacts.
- Identifiers: We will apply one-way hashing to any user identifier fields before they are written to persistent logs, by default. If Chronicle prefers tokenization with a custody key we can support that via your KMS.
- Watermarking/tracing: For testcases we can embed lightweight artifacts (SHA256 of testcase ID + salt) into our usage logs so we can prove lineage without exposing raw data. That allows rollback and deletion if requested.

Attachment: ingest_playbook.md (ingest steps, endpoints, and sample curl commands)

If procurement needs a stronger retention cap we can set the raw log TTL to 30 days; that will change observability for debugging but is doable.

Cheers,
Rishi Malhotra
Senior Reliability Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Chronicle Procurement <procurement@chronicle.ai>
Date: Tue, 28 Jul 2026 10:20:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows — MAP + sign-offs

Thanks everyone — summarizing decisions and next actions below so we have a single source of truth. Please reply with any corrections.

Finalized points:
- Usage: Evaluation-only, 60-day active window on Redwood endpoints. No redistribution or derivative dataset publication.
- Retention: Raw logs TTL set to 30 days (Chronicle request). Masked embeddings retained up to 1 year.
- Identifiers: One-way hashing of user ids by default; Chronicle may opt for KMS tokenization (action: Chronicle to confirm).
- Lineage: We will include salted SHA256 artifacts to prove testcase lineage — non-reversible.

MAP milestones: (attached MAP_timeline_Chronicle.xlsx)
- 7/28: Chronicle ingest verification complete (Chronicle confirms pass/fail)
- 7/30: First eval run on Redwood dedicated infra (Redwood runs and shares quality report)
- 8/04: Review + go/no-go meeting (final exec decision)
- 8/11: If go, procurement SOW signature and prepare production pilot (reserved capacity planning)

Next actions:
- Chronicle procurement to send SOW redlines (procurement@chronicle.ai) — Arun please confirm timeline
- Chronicle to confirm whether they want Redwood-managed staging or VPC tokenization by 7/30
- Redwood (Rishi) to provide a short checklist for ingest validation by EOD 7/28
- Aisha to push final MAP and DocuSign link after procurement alignment

Thanks,
Aisha Rahman
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference

Attachment: MAP_timeline_Chronicle.xlsx
Link: https://app.docusign.com/embeddedSigning?token=abc123-chronicle-map

From: Arun Patel <arun@chronicle.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Maya Gupta <maya@chronicle.ai>
Cc: procurement@chronicle.ai, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Jul 2026 16:40:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Consolidated QA kit onboarding plan + dataset retention windows — procurement confirmation

Hi Aisha / Team —

Procurement here. We reviewed the SOW_Draft and the MAP timeline. Two quick notes:
1) We'll request a 30-day payment term and ask for a modest pilot credit if we sign by 8/11 (internal escalation in progress).
2) We confirm Chronicle prefers the VPC/tokenization option for sensitive testcases. Please update the SOW to reference KMS key rotation and audit log access for our security team.

We aim to send redlines by 7/30. Once redlines are in, please push the DocuSign envelope so we can loop legal.

Arun Patel
Procurement Manager, Chronicle Labs
procurement@chronicle.ai

source.subject: Nimbus: Routing for Third‑Party IP Claims & Escrow point-of-contact
messages:
From: Sara Greene <sara.greene@nimbusthera.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Paul Mendes <paul.mendes@nimbusthera.com>
Date: 2026-07-20T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Questions on indemnity routing and escrow contact (MSA redline attached)

Ben — thanks for sending the draft order form and the MSA redline. Our counsel flagged two items that need quick alignment before we escalate to procurement: defend/control allocation for third‑party IP claims and the proposed escrow activation point for remediation costs.

Two quick questions so we can pull legal ops together:
- Who would be the operational contact at Redwood for evidence intake (triage + custody) if a claim is noticed?
- Can you confirm whether Redwood expects full defend-and-control for 3rd‑party infringement claims, or a defend-with-right-to-step-in model?

I've attached the MSA redline you sent and a short summary of our position. Paul (cc) will be the procurement lead on our side. If possible we’d like a brief call with legal ops tomorrow.

Thanks,
Sara Greene
Head of Legal & Ops
Nimbus Therapeutics

Attachments: Nimbus_MSA_redline_v3.pdf
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Greene <sara.greene@nimbusthera.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Paul Mendes <paul.mendes@nimbusthera.com>
Date: 2026-07-20T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Questions on indemnity routing and escrow contact (MSA redline attached)

Sara — appreciate the note. High level posture from Redwood (so legal ops can prep):
- We propose defend-and-control for third‑party IP claims we are alleged to cause, with a reimbursement path for amounts we successfully defend against (typical defend-vs-reimburse mechanics).
- We are open to narrow carveouts where customer-provided training data or customer-integrated third‑party models change the allocation.
- Escrow activation: we propose escrow only for remediation costs that exceed a $250k threshold (we can discuss the threshold), not for routine defense fees.

Operational contacts:
- Evidence intake & custodian: Marcus Lin (marcus.lin@redwood.com) — he runs Contract Ops and will own evidence staging.
- Legal lead on Redwood side: Kimberly Park (kimberly.park@redwood.com).

Can Kimberly weigh in on specific redline language? Paul — would you share the internal budget/timing constraints so we can evaluate the escrow threshold tradeoff?

Next steps I propose:
1) Kimberly to propose clean redline language for Section 11 (IP claims) and an evidence checklist.
2) 30‑minute call tomorrow to confirm contact points and timeline.

-Ben
Account Exec, Redwood Inference

Attachment: Nimbus_Evidence_Checklist.xlsx
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Sara Greene <sara.greene@nimbusthera.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Paul Mendes <paul.mendes@nimbusthera.com>
Date: 2026-07-20T14:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Questions on indemnity routing and escrow contact (MSA redline attached)

Sara / Ben / Paul — following Ben's note, attaching a short, marked paragraph for the MSA Section X (IP Indemnity) that implements our defend-and-control approach while preserving customer obligations to cooperate. Key points:
- Redwood will have primary defense control for covered third‑party IP claims; we will not settle adversely to the customer's material rights without consent (consent not to be unreasonably withheld).
- Customer must provide prompt notice and reasonable cooperation; failure to provide timely notice that prejudices Redwood's defense can limit recovery.
- Remedies limited to direct damages and remediation costs; consequential damages excluded per Section Y.

We prefer escrow language that limits activation to remediation/correction costs (coding fixes, redeployments, content takedown) that are demonstrably caused by Redwood's deliverable and above the specified threshold. We are flexible on the threshold but need a bright‑line.

preview:
From: Sara Greene <sara.greene@nimbusthera.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Paul Mendes <paul.mendes@nimbusthera.com>
Date: 2026-07-20T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Questions on indemnity routing and escrow contact (MSA redline attached)

Ben — thanks for sending the draft order form and the MSA redline. Our counsel flagged two items that need quick alignment before we escalate to procurement: defend/control allocation for third‑party IP claims and the proposed escrow activation point for remediation costs.

Two quick questions so we can pull legal ops together:
- Who would be the operational contact at Redwood for evidence intake (triage + custody) if a claim is noticed?
- Can you confirm whether Redwood expects full defend-and-control for 3rd‑party infringement claims, or a defend-with-right-to-step-in model?

I've attached the MSA redline you sent and a short summary of our position. Paul (cc) will be the procurement lead on our side. If possible we’d like a brief call with legal ops tomorrow.

Thanks,
Sara Greene
Head of Legal & Ops
Nimbus Therapeutics

Attachments: Nimbus_MSA_redline_v3.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sara Greene <sara.greene@nimbusthera.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Paul Mendes <paul.mendes@nimbusthera.com>
Date: 2026-07-20T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Questions on indemnity routing and escrow contact (MSA redline attached)

Sara — appreciate the note. High level posture from Redwood (so legal ops can prep):
- We propose defend-and-control for third‑party IP claims we are alleged to cause, with a reimbursement path for amounts we successfully defend against (typical defend-vs-reimburse mechanics).
- We are open to narrow carveouts where customer-provided training data or customer-integrated third‑party models change the allocation.
- Escrow activation: we propose escrow only for remediation costs that exceed a $250k threshold (we can discuss the threshold), not for routine defense fees.

Operational contacts:
- Evidence intake & custodian: Marcus Lin (marcus.lin@redwood.com) — he runs Contract Ops and will own evidence staging.
- Legal lead on Redwood side: Kimberly Park (kimberly.park@redwood.com).

Can Kimberly weigh in on specific redline language? Paul — would you share the internal budget/timing constraints so we can evaluate the escrow threshold tradeoff?

Next steps I propose:
1) Kimberly to propose clean redline language for Section 11 (IP claims) and an evidence checklist.
2) 30‑minute call tomorrow to confirm contact points and timeline.

-Ben
Account Exec, Redwood Inference

Attachment: Nimbus_Evidence_Checklist.xlsx

source.subject: Quick quote & permission for Azure co-sell badge (GreenTech.ai)
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Will Summers <will.summers@greentech.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Quick quote & permission for Azure co-sell badge (GreenTech.ai)

Hi Will —

Thanks again for the great demo last week. GreenTech.ai's model-forward pipeline with your telemetry use case looks like a perfect fit for Redwood's low-latency serving. Azure Marketplace has asked us for a one-line customer quote and logo permission so we can complete the co-sell badge application. Would you be comfortable providing a short line we can publish (1-2 sentences) and confirmation we can use your logo?

Suggested quick quote you can edit: "Redwood Inference helped us reduce inference latency by ~55% while improving throughput predictability for our real-time models."

If you can reply with that or something shorter, Monica will slot it into the marketplace entry and I’ll submit. If there are procurement approvals needed on your side, happy to loop in the right folks.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0162

Attachment: azure-co-sell-requirements.pdf (stub)

From: Will Summers <will.summers@greentech.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@greentech.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Jul 2026 12:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick quote & permission for Azure co-sell badge (GreenTech.ai)

Ben —

Thanks for the suggested copy. We’re happy to help. Please use the following quote and our logo (attached to this reply).

"Redwood Inference reduced inference latency for our production models, enabling faster feature delivery and improving user experience."

Authorization: I confirm GreenTech.ai is happy for Redwood to publish this quote and use our company logo for the Azure Marketplace listing and promotional materials.

Elena and I copied for visibility — procurement is aware and will sign any required marketplace agreements; let us know if you need anything formalized.

Best,
Will Summers
Head of ML Engineering, GreenTech.ai
will.summers@greentech.ai

Attachment: greentech-logo.png (stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Will Summers <will.summers@greentech.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Jul 2026 08:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick quote & permission for Azure co-sell badge (GreenTech.ai)

Will —

Perfect, this is exactly what we need. I’ll upload the quote and logo to the Azure submission. Two quick asks so Marketplace can process without delay: 

- Can you confirm a point of contact for Microsoft procurement questions (name + email)?
- Do you have a preferred logo file or a brand usage note? The Marketplace team sometimes needs a transparent PNG and a short usage statement.

Looping in Laura for the co-sell approval signoff and Marcus for legal checks on publishing partner quotes. Monica will prepare the submission draft.

Appreciate it — will keep things lightweight.

Ben

> Ben —
>
> Thanks for the suggested copy. We’re happy to help. Please use the following quote and our logo (attached to this reply).

Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Jul 2026 10:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Quick quote & permission for Azure co-sell badge (GreenTech.ai)

Thanks team — I review co-sell partner material for approvals. Two small notes before we publish: 

1) Marcus, please confirm the quote text is fine as-is from a liability perspective (no forward-looking claims).
2) Ben/Monica, ensure we link GreenTech.ai’s production story in the marketplace entry (one paragraph) and include contact for case study follow-up — keeps the listing conversion-friendly.

If legal signs off, I give my go-ahead. Laura

Laura Bennett
Head of Sales Operations, Redwood Inference
laura@redwood.com

> Will —
>
> Perfect, this is exactly what we need...
